context.title: The 1930s — The Wild Early Years
context.summary: The decade that started it all. Warner Bros. launched Looney Tunes in 1930 as a vehicle to promote their music catalog, with Merrie Melodies following a year later. Early cartoons featured Bosko — animation's first Black cartoon star — and later Buddy, both now largely forgotten. The studio was finding its voice, experimenting with sound synchronization and musical comedy in the wake of Disney's Silly Symphonies. Directors like Tex Avery, Chuck Jones, and Bob Clampett arrived late in the decade and would transform everything. By 1939, the groundwork was laid for the golden age to come. Mel Blanc joined the studio in 1937 and would go on to voice nearly every major character in the canon.

cartoons.title: Congo Jazz
cartoons.desc: Bosko goes hunting in the jungle but ends up befriending the animals and joining them in a musical jam session.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bosko hunts in the jungle, but ends up playing music with the animals.

cartoons.title: Sinkin' in the Bathtub
cartoons.desc: Bosko takes a bath and goes on a wild car ride with his girlfriend Honey in the first-ever Looney Tunes cartoon.

cartoons.title: The Booze Hangs High
cartoons.desc: Bosko visits a farm and dances with the animals, including a family of pigs who get tipsy on corn mash.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bosko has a grand time on the farm, dancing with a cow, playing a horse's tail like a violin and getting drunk with three pigs.

cartoons.title: Bosko the Doughboy
cartoons.desc: Bosko serves as a soldier in World War I, battling enemy forces with slapstick antics.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bosko is a doughboy in the Great War. Bullets and bombs are everywhere. (A bomb even blows up the title card.) Bosko and his fellow infantrymen are hardly safe in their trench. Bosko is happily eating from a pan full of beans when a bomb hits the pan and destroys his meal. Bosko misses Honey; he pulls out her picture and kisses it. A cannonball tears through it, making her head a gaping hole. Now Bosko is angry. He vows revenge but the moment his helmet appears above the trench, it’s hit with dozens of bullets, knocking him back down. Another soldier briefly cheers him up with harmonica music. Bosko gets his chance to be a hero when his buddy swallows a cannonball.

cartoons.title: Lady, Play Your Mandolin!
cartoons.desc: Foxy visits a rowdy saloon in the debut cartoon of the Merrie Melodies series.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Things are hopping at a certain Mexican cafe and then Foxy walks in and the customers go really wild.

cartoons.title: One More Time
cartoons.desc: Foxy works as a police officer dealing with criminals and traffic violations.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foxy the Cop is trying to enforce the law in town, but dangerous drivers and strangers who also kidnap Roxy are making this difficult.

cartoons.title: Smile, Darn Ya, Smile!
cartoons.desc: Foxy has a nightmare about being a trolley engineer plagued by mishaps.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A streetcar conductor, Foxy has adventures with a would-be passenger hippo, a cow blocking the tracks, and a runaway train while Foxy, his passengers, and some hobos sing the title song.

cartoons.title: You Don't Know What You're Doin'!
cartoons.desc: Piggy takes his girlfriend Fluffy to a theater for an evening of orchestral entertainment gone wrong.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Piggy picks up his girlfriend and takes her to a theater where a hot jazz orchestra is playing.

cartoons.title: A Great Big Bunch of You
cartoons.desc: A Christmas Eve scene where Santa arrives and toys come to life in a holiday celebration.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A mannequin in the city dump improvises a working piano from junk, then plays and sings the title song. Various discarded items join in with song or dance.

cartoons.title: Goopy Geer
cartoons.desc: The eccentric Goopy Geer performs a wild piano act at a saloon, entertaining the crowd with his antics.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Goopy, a dog of no particular personality, but a crackerjack piano player, plays several songs on the stage of a nightclub. We spend a fair amount of time watching the patrons and staff of the nightclub.

cartoons.title: I Love A Parade
cartoons.desc: A circus parade features a variety of sideshow acts, performing animals, and musical entertainment marching through town.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A circus parade, to the title tune. Next, a series of sideshow acts: the wild boy, the rubber man, siamese twin pigs, a tattooed man, a hula-dancing hippo, an Indian snake (or goat) charmer. Into the ring, we have a hippo riding a horse (much to the horse's dismay), a high-wire act (again, to the title song), and finally a lion tamer.

cartoons.title: I Wish I Had Wings
cartoons.desc: A wingless black chick uses discarded clothing to fashion makeshift wings and attempts to fly with the other birds.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An expectant father rooster fetches doctor stork, who comes out with a basket full of white chicks and one little black one, who gets crowded out of the food. After singing the title song, he manages to improvise a pair of wings and fly over the chicken coop, but regrets it when he is chased by a mean scarecrow.

cartoons.title: It's Got Me Again
cartoons.desc: Mice play instruments and party in a house until a cat shows up, sending them into a frenzied escape.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Late at night, the mice come out and sing and play to the title tune, among others. That is, until the cat arrives, but he's quickly sent packing.

cartoons.title: Moonlight for Two
cartoons.desc: Hillbilly Goopy Geer takes his sweetheart to a cabin for a lively square dance with the local folk.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two courting hillbilly dogs, which is Goopy Geer and his girlfriend, go to the big barn dance. Then a villian comes over to try to shoot him with a gun, then fights, and the villian got on fire and escaped thanks to the walking stove.

cartoons.title: Ride Him, Bosko!
cartoons.desc: Bosko rides his horse through the Old West as a cowboy in a musical adventure.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Ride Him, Bosko! is a western-flavored cartoon with lots of shooting gags involving body reduction, and card characters singing! There's also an alcohol gag that has a really strong one turning a male piano player into a woman instantly!

cartoons.title: The Queen Was in the Parlor
cartoons.desc: When a king hippo returns to his castle questioning the queen's whereabouts, he discovers she's been entertaining Goopy Geer.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The last Goopy Geer cartoon. The king returns to his castle, and asks where the queen is; she's in the parlor, and won't be seen, according to the title song. He goes to his throne and summons his jester, Goopy Geer. A black knight arrives and threatens one of the young ladies in court; Goopy Geer fights him off, first with an ax, then in armor from kitchen utensils, then butting him with a mounted animal head, which makes the knight's armor fall apart. He pulls it together again and runs away.

cartoons.title: You're Too Careless With Your Kisses!
cartoons.desc: A bumblebee comes home to his accusatory wife, who scolds him for his late-night drinking and flirtatious ways.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A bee returns home late after a night out having too much honey. His wife leaves him, but quickly ends up in the clutches of an evil large predator.

cartoons.title: Bosko In Person
cartoons.desc: Bosko and friends engage in a musical cartoon adventure full of song, dance, and early animation gags.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bosko and Honey perform a wacky stage act that includes doing imitations of Maurice Chevalier, Greta Garbo and Jimmy Durante.

cartoons.title: Bosko's Picture Show
cartoons.desc: Bosko and friends engage in a musical cartoon adventure full of song, dance, and early animation gags.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bosko runs a movie theater that shows a wacky newsreel with Jack Dumpsey, a slapstick short from Haurel and Lardy, and a turn-of-the-century melodrama starring Honey.

cartoons.title: Buddy's Beer Garden
cartoons.desc: Buddy, Warner Bros.' early cartoon star, stumbles through a lighthearted adventure with his girlfriend Cookie.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Buddy's musical antics as a waiter at a German beer garden are truly delightful! Cookie appears as a cigarette girl and scarf-dancer, and a drag-decked lounge-singing Buddy brings down the house.

cartoons.title: Buddy's Day Out
cartoons.desc: Buddy and Cookie's picnic is disrupted when Cookie's baby brother Elmer wanders into danger.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: This cartoon has some amusing sight gags like a car going back through a fence and crashing into various animals before landing in Cookie's yard or a train that seems on its way into crashing into a car with Elmer and Happy inside when Buddy and Cookie use a ladder to derail the locomotive in another direction.

cartoons.title: I Like Mountain Music
cartoons.desc: Magazine characters at a drugstore come to life and put on a show until a masked villain threatens the fun.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After hours, individuals on various magazine covers in a drugstore come to life and sing, speak, or perform. Caricature celebrity depictions include George Arliss, Eddie Cantor, Sonja Henie, Benito Mussolini, Ignacy Paderewski, Edward G. Robinson, Will Rogers, and Ed Wynn. A robbery sequence features bad guys breaking into the cash register and Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson on the case. King Kong also makes an appearance. A Merrie Melody cartoon.

cartoons.title: Pettin' in the Park
cartoons.desc: Bird couples romance in the park while singing, until rain sends everyone scrambling.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While at the park, a group of birds engage in a swimming contest. Another cartoon by Warner Brothers promoting a song from its movie "Gold Diggers of 1933".

cartoons.title: Shuffle Off To Buffalo
cartoons.desc: A peek inside the stork's baby factory where newborn babies are assembled, packaged, and delivered to families.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An animated singing and dancing revue of babies (representing a variety of stereotypes) who are being prepared for delivery by stork.

cartoons.title: Sittin' on a Backyard Fence
cartoons.desc: Two cats serenade each other at night until chaos erupts when a rowdy dog gets involved.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Male cats fight each other over a female cat around the backyard fence.

cartoons.title: The Dish Ran Away With The Spoon
cartoons.desc: Kitchen dishes and utensils come alive in a bakery at midnight, dancing until the monstrous Yeast Beast attacks.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Utensils and food dance, sing, and play in the kitchen, until a lump of dough turns into a monster and they all unite to stop it.

cartoons.title: The Organ Grinder
cartoons.desc: An Italian organ grinder plays his hurdy-gurdy in a ghetto neighborhood, entertaining the immigrant characters with music.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A beloved Italian organ grinder plays his music and sings his song through an immigrant-filled ghetto neighborhood, where hard-scrabble kids and well-proportioned matrons get hep to the beat that flows from the organ grinder's calliope. There are lots of spoofs of life on the Lower East Side and lots of monkeyshines as the organ grinder's pet performs "42nd Street" and imitates some Hollywood greats. The monkey takes wing and gets behind the wheel of a car, driving all over the area and wrecking everything.

cartoons.title: We're In The Money
cartoons.desc: When an old shopkeeper closes up for the night, his toy store merchandise comes alive for a musical celebration.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After the last human has left the department store, the toys proceed to the music department where they start performing the Warren/Dubin song "We're in the money". The money soon joins for a chorus, as well as display dolls in the wardrobe department.

cartoons.title: Young and Healthy
cartoons.desc: A bored king leaves his court to search for entertainment and finds it among the common folk outside.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A king is bored with the stupid people in his court. He goes out in search of young people.

cartoons.title: Beauty and the Beast
cartoons.desc: A young girl's nightmare transforms her ceramic figurines into a dark fairy tale adventure.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A little girl is eating too many snacks when she doesn't realize that it is her bedtime. Then the Sandman comes out of nowhere and, sure enough, the girl falls asleep in the blink of an eye. Just then, she has a dream that she is in Toyland, where she encounters all kinds of fairy tale characters.

cartoons.title: Buddy's Circus
cartoons.desc: Buddy, Warner Bros.' early cartoon star, stumbles through a lighthearted adventure with his girlfriend Cookie.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Buddy runs a circus with a variety of zany acts, and ends up having to rescue a baby who gets lost during the performance.

cartoons.title: Honeymoon Hotel
cartoons.desc: A pair of lovebugs check into a hotel for their honeymoon and encounter mischievous bedbugs.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After introducing the small town Bugtown, inhabitated by bugs, this short shows what happens to two honeymooning lovebugs at the Honeymoon Hotel in town, due to the fact, that their love is a little bit to hot.

cartoons.title: How Do I Know It's Sunday?
cartoons.desc: Products in a general store come to life on Sunday and sing and dance to the title song.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Inside a general store, the products all come to life and happily sing the title song. An Eskimo falls for a cookie and has to come to the rescue when a swarm of flies invades.

cartoons.title: Pop Goes Your Heart
cartoons.desc: When spring arrives in the forest, woodland animals go about their business until a bear disrupts the peace.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A robin sings, and the vegetation turns spring-like, followed by some more action by birds and bees...

cartoons.title: Shake Your Powder Puff
cartoons.desc: Barnyard animals put on musical performances until a dog gets thrown out and returns for revenge.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In this one, we're witness to a sort of vaudeville show with an all-animal cast, including a myopic turtle and a pig very proud of his ability to play the flute. There's a troupe of dancing "girls" who sing about "shaking your powder puff," and a dog (or wolf, or fox or something) keeps getting thrown out by the management.

cartoons.title: The Girl at the Ironing Board
cartoons.desc: Laundry in a washroom mysteriously comes to life late at night and engages in musical antics.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An early Merrie Melodies featuring the title song.

cartoons.title: The Miller's Daughter
cartoons.desc: After a ceramic figurine of a country girl is broken by a cat, the pieces come alive for an adventure.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Ceramic figurines come to life after one of them is knocked over and broken by the household cat.

cartoons.title: Those Were Wonderful Days
cartoons.desc: A nostalgic look back at 1898 featuring a barbershop quartet and Fourth of July celebrations.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Animated musical short. Songs including Oh You Beautiful Doll, Put on Your Old Grey Bonnet, In the Shade of the Old Apple Tree.

cartoons.title: Why Do I Dream Those Dreams
cartoons.desc: A fantasy retelling of Rip Van Winkle featuring beer-guzzling leprechauns and musical dream sequences.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Rip van Winkle gets the musical cartoon treatment...

cartoons.title: A Cartoonist's Nightmare
cartoons.desc: An animator at a cartoon studio must finish a drawing before closing time, but his creation causes chaos.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A cartoonist falls asleep at the drawing board and into the clutches of his own villains, until Beans the Cat comes to the rescue.

cartoons.title: Billboard Frolics
cartoons.desc: Billboards and advertising signs come to life at night, singing and dancing to 'Merrily We Roll Along.'
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Billboards come to life. Eddie Camphor and his "wioleen" player Rub-Him-Off do a song and dance to "Merrily We Roll Along" with new lyrics...

cartoons.title: Country Boy
cartoons.desc: Peter Rabbit sneaks into a garden while his siblings go to school, getting into mischief among the vegetables.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Mother rabbit sends her charges off to school. Peter needs a bit more attention; he's hiding with the chickens, using a feather duster to stand in for a tail. On his way to school, Peter starts to sneak into a farmer's field, but is caught by three schoolmates. They threaten to tell the teacher, and Peter heads off to school.

cartoons.title: Country Mouse
cartoons.desc: A small mouse named Elmer dreams of becoming a wrestler, but his grandmother warns him of the dangers.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A strong mouse says that he will become the heavyweight champion of the world. But his grandma (who is just as strong) doesn't want him to fight...

cartoons.title: Flowers for Madame
cartoons.desc: Flowers in a garden come to life and dance until a lit match threatens to burn everything down.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A tour of the garden, where every flower dances or plays a tune, especially today, since it's the flower pageant. Fire strikes as a badly placed magnifying glass ignites a match. The flowers do what they can to fight it, but the fire manages to get through the sprinkler.

cartoons.title: Hollywood Capers
cartoons.desc: Beans the Cat sneaks into a Hollywood sound stage where he encounters Oliver Owl and chaos ensues.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: W.C.Fields enters the Warmer Bros. Studio. Beans tries to drive in, but the guard throws him and his car against a tree. Charlie Chaplin drives in, followed by Oliver Hardy on foot - but we see that it's really Beans in disguise. Oliver Owl is directing a picture; Beans sneaks onto the stage. He's watching from a catwalk when someone knocks him off, into the middle of the scene. Beans is thrown off the set, right into the set of a Frankenstein movie. He accidentally brings the robotic monster to life, and it crashes into the original studio, eating the camera. Beans tries to stop the monster, but is sent flying. He lands against a wind machine. which chops up the monster.

cartoons.title: I Haven't Got a Hat
cartoons.desc: A school recital featuring Porky Pig's debut, stuttering through a poetry recitation alongside other young animals.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's recital day at the schoolhouse. First up: Porky, who recites The Midnight Ride of Paul Revere. A nervous kitten recites Mary Had a Little Lamb. The puppies Ham and Ex sing the title song. Oliver Owl plays the piano; Beans the cat puts a cat and dog inside, and they play a tune as well.

cartoons.title: Into Your Dance
cartoons.desc: A concert is held on Captain Benny's showboat featuring various musical performances.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The showboat comes to town, piloted by Captain Benny. A quartet sings "Go Into Your Dance" in honor of its originator, Al Jolson. They introduce the conductor, who is a pig caricature of orchestra leader Paul Whiteman. One of the musicians plugs the pig's tail into a light socket so he conducts at super speed. Then Captain Benny announces the start of the amateur hour, with an operatic cow and a tough guy reciting poetry with sound effects.

cartoons.title: Little Dutch Plate
cartoons.desc: Things in a Dutch kitchen come to life, including a girl painted on a plate and a mischievous salt shaker.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A cheery tune in a Dutch kitchen; the girl on the plate and the salt shaker boy are in love. They dance....

cartoons.title: Mr. and Mrs. Is the Name
cartoons.desc: Buddy and Cookie appear as mer-kids living under the sea in a musical underwater adventure.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A merman boy and mermaid girl play, explore a sunken ship, and deal with a giant octopus.

cartoons.title: My Green Fedora
cartoons.desc: Peter Rabbit is assigned to babysit his baby brother Elmer but gets distracted, leading to trouble.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A rabbit is told by his mother to watch out for his baby brother Elmer while she's out of the house, but a wolf has other plans for Elmer after he hears the older brother sing "My Green Fedora."

cartoons.title: The Cat Came Back
cartoons.desc: A curious kitten plays with a small mouse from across the hallway in a game of cat-and-mouse friendship.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Mama cat is teaching her kittens to catch mice. Meanwhile, across the basement, mama mouse is teaching her little ones how to avoid cats.

cartoons.title: The Fire Alarm
cartoons.desc: Ham and Ex play at a fire station, pulling pranks and causing mayhem with the firefighting equipment.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: 2 puppets are left to their uncle's attention who works at the Fire house.

cartoons.title: The Lady in Red
cartoons.desc: A Mexican cockroach café is overrun by dancing bugs while the owner Manuel is away.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: At a Spanish restaurant, the owner is at the bullfights and the restaurant is closed. The roaches come out to eat, drink and be merry. At a roach night club, a comely little female roach, dressed in red, flamenco dances for an appreciative crowd. A pet parrot escapes his cage and finds the night club and chases the little dancer.

cartoons.title: The Merry Old Soul
cartoons.desc: After Old King Cole marries the Woman in the Shoe, they must manage a household full of unruly children.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Old King Cole marries the Woman in the Shoe. As soon as they get home, the babies show up from every drawer and closet, much to the king's chagrin.

cartoons.title: Alpine Antics
cartoons.desc: Beans the Cat and Porky Pig enter a skiing contest in the mountains with slapstick winter sports gags.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Prologue: various animals enjoy winter sports. Beans sees a notice of a ski race, and decides to enter. But so does a bad guy (who looks more than a little like Disney's Pete). The bad guy sabotages the other contestants in various ways, takes short cuts, etc. But Beans manages to tie up the bad guy in his own trip line. A duck riding a dachshund knocks the bad guy out for a while; he and Beans trade places a few more times before Beans wins the race, just barely.

cartoons.title: At Your Service Madame
cartoons.desc: A widow pig receives unwanted romantic attention from the sleazy W.C. Squeals.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Mrs. Hamhock finds herself the object of unwanted attention following an article in the paper about...

cartoons.title: Bingo Crosbyana
cartoons.desc: Fun-loving insects take over a kitchen, with the female bugs swooning over a Bing Crosby-like cricket.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs take over a kitchen, prompting a Bing Crosby-esque croon.

cartoons.title: Boulevardier from the Bronx
cartoons.desc: A baseball game between teams of chickens features Emily the Chicken and her boyfriend Claude.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A baseball team full of anthropomorphic animals play on.

cartoons.title: Don't Look Now
cartoons.desc: On Valentine's Day, Cupid makes animals fall in love while Satan tries to sabotage every romance.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's St. Valentine's Day. Cupid is having fun arranging, while a young devil is making mischief sabotaging, love affairs.

cartoons.title: Fish Tales
cartoons.desc: After a day of fishing, Porky Pig dreams that the tables are turned and a fish catches him instead.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig goes fishing. It's not quite the outing he anticipated.

cartoons.title: I Love to Singa
cartoons.desc: Young Owl Jolson defies his classical music-loving father to pursue his passion for jazz singing.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A stern classical music teacher becomes a father of four musically-inclined sons, but when one of them demonstrates a preference for jazz music, his father kicks him out of the house.

cartoons.title: I Wanna Play House
cartoons.desc: Two bear cubs sneak away from Papa Bear to play hide and seek in the forest.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two bear cubs, one black, one brown, frolic near their sleeping father.

cartoons.title: I'd Love to Take Orders From You
cartoons.desc: A hardworking father scarecrow protects the cornfield while his small son tries to prove himself.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Quitting time for a scarecrow. He gets home, and his little boy scares him. To the title song, he teaches his son the basics of scare-crowing. Bedtime for junior; he prays to be a big scarecrow, just like his daddy. The next morning he sneaks out before anyone else is up and practices scaring a rooster, a squirrel, and a rabbit. He takes up his father's place, but the crows are not impressed; in fact, the crow goes on the attack.

cartoons.title: Let It Be Me
cartoons.desc: Emily the Chicken must choose between dating suave Mr. Bingo and the jealous rooster Clem.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A radio crooner spirits a girl away to the big city only to drop her like a hot potato.

cartoons.title: Page Miss Glory
cartoons.desc: A bellboy in the small town of Hicksville daydreams about the glamorous Miss Glory arriving at his hotel.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While the rural town of Hicksville anticipates the arrival of one Miss Glory, the bellhop at her modest hotel falls asleep and dreams of paging her at a luxurious metropolitan high rise hotel.

cartoons.title: Pigs Is Pigs
cartoons.desc: Greedy young Piggy Hamhock is scolded by his mother for overeating, then dreams of being force-fed endlessly.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A hungry little pig eats a couple of pies off the windowsill. When it's time for dinner, he ties together the spaghetti of all the other little pigs and eats it all. That night, he has a nightmare where he is force-fed by a mad scientist.

cartoons.title: Plane Dippy
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig applies for a military flying job but fails every test before accidentally ending up in a plane.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig joins the Army Air Corps. After overcoming the problems caused by his small stature and surviving the rigors of basic training...

cartoons.title: Porky in the North Woods
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig battles a rough French Canadian lumberjack causing havoc in the northern wilderness.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig runs a game refuge. Despite the abundant signs to the contrary, Jean-Baptiste the trapper sets numerous traps, ensnaring many animals.

cartoons.title: Porky the Rain-Maker
cartoons.desc: Porky buys weather pills to end a drought on his father's farm, with unpredictable results.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The farm is suffering through a terrible drought. Porky's father sends him to the store to buy some feed with their last dollar...

cartoons.title: Porky's Pet
cartoons.desc: Porky receives an offer from a producer for him and his pet ostrich to perform, leading to showbiz chaos.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky and his pet ostrich, Lulu, get invited to perform on Broadway for $75/week. But first they have to get there, and the train conductor won't let the ostrich board. Porky sneaks her on. She gets loose and eats a sleeping woman's wig, a boy's toy airplane, and a concertina. Porky hides her in a guitar case, but she gets out as the conductor comes by, and they are both thrown off. They enlist a hand cart and a cow to outrun the train.

cartoons.title: Porky's Road Race
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig enters a car race against celebrity caricatures in a wild cross-country competition.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's race day, and first prize is $2 million (less $1,999,998.37 in taxes). Porky's little car is matched against cars driven by stars of yesteryear, including Laurel and Hardy and Charlie Chaplin. When the black #13 driven by "Borax Karoff" makes a bid for the finish line, can Porky fend him off?

cartoons.title: Porkys Poultry Plant
cartoons.desc: Porky runs a chicken farm that comes under attack from a hungry hawk threatening his flock.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig is raising chickens, ducks, and geese. Many birds have fallen victim to the hawk. Porky's going to do everything he can to fight back.

cartoons.title: The Blow Out
cartoons.desc: The Mad Bomber plants time bombs all over the city, and Porky Pig must stop him before they explode.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A crazed bomber is terrorizing the city. Meanwhile, a young Porky Pig is a few cents shy of buying an ice cream soda; he starts earning it by picking up items people drop and handing them back to them.

cartoons.title: The CooCoo Nut Grove
cartoons.desc: Hollywood celebrity caricatures gather for performances at the glamorous CooCoo Nut Grove nightclub.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A visit to a Hollywood nightclub, featuring caricatures of, among others, Walter Winchell, Hugh Herbert, W.C. Fields, Katharine Hepburn, Ned Sparks, Johnny Weissmuller, Lupe Velez, John Barrymore, Harpo Marx, George Arliss, Mae West, Stan Laurel, Oliver Hardy, Clark Gable, Edna May Oliver, Gary Cooper, The Dionne Quintuplets, Groucho Marx, Helen Morgan, Wallace Beery, Edward G. Robinson and George Raft.

cartoons.title: The Phantom Ship
cartoons.desc: Beans the Cat flies to the Arctic with Ham and Ex on an aircraft and explores a mysterious ghost ship.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Uncle Beans and the kids are off to visit a haunted ship ('The Phantom') trapped in the ice, hoping to find pirate treasure. They encounter all manner of ghosts and goblins, but eventually find what they've been looking for. When Beans tries to warm up by throwing some chairs in a stove and lighting it, he thaws out a pair of pirates that chase the trio around. They treasure-seekers are eventually forced back into their plane and they decide to fly away.

cartoons.title: The Village Smithy
cartoons.desc: Based on the poem 'The Village Blacksmith,' Porky gives the smithy a hard time at his forge.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The narrator sets the scene for a warped version of the classic poem, and the hijinks when assistant Porky gives the blacksmith a rubber horseshoe, then a hot horseshoe on the horse's backside by accident.

cartoons.title: Toy Town Hall
cartoons.desc: A child's toys come to life at night and put on a musical variety show inspired by Fred Astaire.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A child would rather listen to the radio than go to bed, but mother insists. He sleeps, but at midnight, his toys come alive and put on a show for him (much of it recycled, though often with different backgrounds, from earlier cartoons).

cartoons.title: When I Yoo Hoo
cartoons.desc: The feuding Weaver and Matthews families fight each other in a hillbilly war over mountain territory.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's the Weavers vs. the Mathews in Hickory Holler. The Weavers are singing (the title song), the Mathews are sleeping...

cartoons.title: A Star is Hatched
cartoons.desc: Emily the Chicken reads a movie magazine and travels to Hollywood hoping to become a film star.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Emily the chicken lives in Hickville but dreams of Hollywood. Her chance comes when director J. Megga-Phone happens to drive past and gives her his card.

cartoons.title: Ain't We Got Fun
cartoons.desc: A group of playful mice trick a cat into taking the blame for the mess they make in the house.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The cat's asleep, so the mice are on the loose, for a while at least, in the pantry. When he wakes up, they pile the food on him and get him thrown out, and then they *really* have the run of the house.

cartoons.title: Daffy Duck and Egghead
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck's first color appearance as the zany duck battles dimwitted hunter Egghead in the forest.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy taunts a hunter in Tex Avery's classic, meta short.

cartoons.title: Dog Daze
cartoons.desc: Various dog breeds compete for prizes at a fancy dog show with comedic results.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A series of gags at a dog show, including a stage revue. A dog gets into a trunk of roller skates and crashes through the stage show.

cartoons.title: Egghead Rides Again
cartoons.desc: The bumbling Egghead tries to prove himself as a cowboy on a ranch out West.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: City dweller Egghead dreams of being a cowboy, but his bouncing around gets him kicked out of his boarding house. He sees an ad for a ranch looking for a cowboy and applies. His tryout includes tests of marksmanship and use of a branding iron, but most of it consist of chasing down and roping a troublesome little calf. He passes the test, but the job isn't exactly what he dreamed of.

cartoons.title: Get Rich Quick Porky
cartoons.desc: A con artist named John Gusher tricks Porky and Gabby Goat into digging for oil on worthless land.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky invests his savings. Mayhem ensues.

cartoons.title: I Only Have Eyes for You
cartoons.desc: An ice delivery bird is wooed by a persistent spinster bird while he tries to court someone else.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The iceman is in love with a pretty girl, and an old spinster is pining and cooking for him. But his dreamgirl prefers crooners like Bing Crosby, Rudy Vallee, or Eddie Cantor. After leaving her, he spots the sign of an imitator, and thinks he could ask him to do the crooning for him while he is trying to date his girl. The imitator accepts, and at first the trick is working, until the imitator gets too cold amid the ice in the back of the van and the girl gets suspicious.

cartoons.title: I Wanna Be a Sailor
cartoons.desc: Peter Parrot wants to be a sailor instead of eating crackers, dreaming of life on the high seas.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Momma parrot is teaching her young-uns to say "Polly want a cracker" but little Peter doesn't want a cracker, he wants to be a sailor like dad. Mom tells him what a no-account his dad really was, setting sail for Hawaii ("no, Maw, it was Catalina") right after the kids were born. Peter is unswayed, and takes off. He turns a barrel into a boat, and crews it with an annoyingly talkative duckling, then sets sail on a lake. They get caught in a thunderstorm (the duck loves it). Peter calls for help and momma comes running, but the duck has already saved him. But he still wants to be a sailor.

cartoons.title: Little Red Walking Hood
cartoons.desc: An adaptation of Little Red Riding Hood featuring Elmer Fudd in an early character appearance.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Red walks past a pool hall; the wolf sees her and pursues her, but, she is oblivious to his come-ons.

cartoons.title: Picador Porky
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig participates in a bullfight in Mexico — the first short where Mel Blanc voices Porky.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky and two pals stumble onto a Mexican town on the day of the town's annual bullfight. When they learn the contest winner gets $1,000...

cartoons.title: Plenty of Money and You
cartoons.desc: One of a hen's eggs hatches into an ostrich who causes chaos around the farmyard.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A hen's chicks hatch, but one of them is actually an ostrich. She treats it as her own, but the ostrich keeps getting into trouble.

cartoons.title: Porky and Gabby
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig and his grumpy companion Gabby Goat go on a camping trip ruined by car trouble.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig and ill-tempered Gabby Goat go on a camping outing. Chaos ensues.

cartoons.title: Porky the Wrestler
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig travels to a wrestling arena and must face the fearsome wrestling champion in the ring.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig, hitchhiking to the big wrestling match, gets a ride from the challenger and unexpectedly winds up also in the ring.

cartoons.title: Porky's Badtime Story
cartoons.desc: Porky and Gabby Goat are warned by their boss that oversleeping one more time means getting fired.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After Porky and Gabby oversleep yet again, their boss warns them that they'll be fired if they're late again...

cartoons.title: Porky's Building
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig and rival contractor Dirty Digg compete in a fierce bidding war for a building project.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky and another contractor are competing to submit the lower bid for a new city hall. When they submit identical bids, the city has them compete, whichever finishes first gets the job.

cartoons.title: Porky's Duck Hunt
cartoons.desc: Porky goes duck hunting but encounters a zany, unflappable duck — Daffy Duck's debut appearance.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Inexperienced duck hunter Porky Pig is taunted by a mischievous duck (Daffy, making his screen debut).

cartoons.title: Porky's Five & Ten
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig sets sail for the South Seas islands with a boat full of five-and-dime store merchandise.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky sets sail for the Boola-Boola islands in the South Seas with a ship full of general merchandise and plans to open a 5 & 10 cent store. But a swordfish cuts a hole in the ship and Porky's goods fall into the ocean, where the fish make creative uses of them, ultimately opening a Hollywood nightclub, complete with fish impersonating various stars.

cartoons.title: Porky's Garden
cartoons.desc: Porky enters a contest to grow the largest homegrown produce at the agricultural fair.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky decides to start a garden. Mayhem ensues.

cartoons.title: Porky's Hare Hunt
cartoons.desc: Porky goes rabbit hunting but encounters a zany white rabbit — a proto-Bugs Bunny character.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig goes after a rogue rabbit who manages to frustrate him at every turn.

cartoons.title: Porky's Hero Agency
cartoons.desc: Porky reads a Greek myth and dreams about being a hero battling a gorgon who turns people to stone.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky is reading the Greek myth of the gorgon, who turned everyone she looked at into stone. Mother tells him it's bedtime; he dreams of being Porkykarkus, the hero that saves Greece.

cartoons.title: Porky's Poppa
cartoons.desc: Porky's father Phineas buys an Acme cow contraption after their real cow stops producing milk.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: We take a tour of Porky's Poppa's farm, to the tune of Old MacDonald. After meeting several animals, "on this farm, he has a mortgage" which he frets over, particularly since Bessie has stopped producing milk. Poppa orders an Acme milk producing robot, and the beast vs. machine battle is on.

cartoons.title: Porky's Railroad
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig drives his little locomotive in a race against a streamlined modern train.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky is the engineer on the most pathetic train in the fleet. After some routine episodes (using pepper to get the engine to sneeze itself up a hill, chasing a cow off the tracks, only to discover too late that it's been replaced by a very angry bull), Porky gets word that he's going to be replaced by the new streamlined Silver Fish. He insults it under his breath, but the Silver Fish engineer hears and challenges him to a race. The angry bull catapults Porky to victory.

cartoons.title: Porky's Super Service
cartoons.desc: While working at a gas station, Porky must deal with a bratty baby customer causing destruction.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky owns a full-service gas station; he deals with a wide variety of problems, like a bump that migrates to different parts of the car. But his real nemesis is a supposedly sleeping baby in a car whose tire needs changing; in fact, the baby is wide awake and a real brat. Both Porky and the brat end up covered in grease; the irate mother drives off, but the child has tied a pump to a tire, which ends up pulling the whole station into the ground.

cartoons.title: Rover's Rival
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig teaches his dog Rover tricks, but a rival puppy shows up and tries to steal his spot.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky reads a book of new dog tricks; unfortunately, his dog, Rover, is old. A puppy comes by and taunts him.

cartoons.title: She Was an Acrobat's Daughter
cartoons.desc: A local movie theater shows newsreels, a sing-along, and a feature film with comedic interruptions.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An evening at the local movie theater, including a sing-along led by Maestro Stickoutski at the Mighty Fertilizer organ, a Goofy-Tone newsreel, and the feature, Petrified Florist, featuring caricatures of Bette Davis and Leslie Howard.

cartoons.title: Speaking of the Weather
cartoons.desc: Characters from books and magazines come alive in a closed drugstore at midnight for a wild party.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's midnight at the bookstore and all the book and magazine characters are coming to life. When a bulldog from an adventure book uses a Boswell Sisters-like performance by girls in a travel magazine as a distraction to rob a bank, he is chased, caught, and sentenced to, of course "Life" (the magazine). But there's also a conveniently placed "Escape" magazine....

cartoons.title: Streamlined Greta Green
cartoons.desc: In a town of anthropomorphic cars, a young male car dreams of becoming a speedy taxi.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In a world wherein cars act like humans, Junior wants to be a taxi, but his mother wants him to grow up to be a nice touring car like his father. Mom doesn't know that Junior sometimes skips school and ventures into the city to ride in traffic, drink hi-test gas, and race trains.

cartoons.title: Sunbonnet Blue
cartoons.desc: After a hat shop closes for the night, mice have a party with the merchandise until a cat crashes it.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A rat comes between two mice in love.

cartoons.title: The Case of the Stuttering Pig
cartoons.desc: Porky and his siblings face a villain trying to steal their inheritance using a Jekyll-and-Hyde potion.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig and his family inherit Uncle Solomon's estate, but if they die everything goes to the lawyer, who turns himself into a Mr. Hyde-style monster in an effort to kill off the pigs.

cartoons.title: The Daffy Doc
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck's wild antics in a hospital operating room get him kicked out by the head surgeon.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After being thrown out of the operating room as Dr. Quack's assistant, Dr. Daffy Duck makes Porky Pig his own - unwilling - patient.

cartoons.title: The Lyin' Mouse
cartoons.desc: A gray mouse tells a cat the Aesop fable of The Lion and the Mouse to save his own skin.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A mouse is trying to free himself from a trap when a cat arrives. The mouse, desperate, asks if the cat has heard the story of the lion and the mouse.

cartoons.title: The Sneezing Weasel
cartoons.desc: A brown chick named Wilbur catches a cold after getting caught in the rain and sneezes uncontrollably.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When Mama hen takes her chicks out to get breakfast, little Wilbur is soaked in a sudden rainstorm and comes down with a head cold. Mama puts him to bed, then goes back out to get the doctor. A conniving weasel, seeing Mama leave, disguises himself as a doctor and comes calling on the unattended chicks.

cartoons.title: The Woods Are Full of Cuckoos
cartoons.desc: Forest animal caricatures of celebrities sing and perform at a woodland talent show.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Forest animals participate in the Woodland Community Swing Variety Show on radio station KUKU.

cartoons.title: A Day at the Beach
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon featuring various comedic vignettes set at a busy seaside beach.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The whole family is at the beach for an outing, and each is having their own little adventure. The Captain fights the sun with his beach umbrella, in an attempt to nap. Grandpa tries to build a sand castle, but the waves keep wiping it out. Mama, after trying to defend her picnic basket, tries dipping a cautious toe into the big bad ocean, eventually needing to be rescued by the Captain.

cartoons.title: A Feud There Was
cartoons.desc: The Weaver and Winfield hillbilly families continue their generations-long feud until a pacifist elf intervenes and convinces both clans to lay down their arms.

cartoons.title: Cinderella Meets Fella
cartoons.desc: An adaptation of Cinderella where Elmer Fudd plays Prince Charming searching for his mystery woman.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Cinderella goes to the ball, where she meets Prince Charming (Egghead).

cartoons.title: Count Me Out
cartoons.desc: Egghead dreams of battling the boxing champion Biff Stew in a wild wrestling match.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Egghead decides his road to riches is through a boxing correspondence course. When he graduates, he takes on champion Biff Stew. Biff pummels him mercilessly (the correspondence course record continues to coach him during the match), but by accident, he knocks Biff out until we see it was all in Egghead's head, after being knocked out by the practice equipment.

cartoons.title: Cracked Ice
cartoons.desc: While ice skating, W.C. Squeals discovers a bird trapped in the ice and comedic mayhem follows.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's ice skating time. After a few generic ice-skating gags, we get to the main story. An animal falls through the ice, and a pig doing W.C. Fields (W.C. Squeals, apparently) calls for help from a Saint Bernard dog. The dog dispenses a drink, and Squeals begins scheming to get some himself. First he tries faking his own fall through the ice, but the dog sees through it and downs the drink himself. Then Squeals tries using a dish of bones and a magnet, but the magnet falls through the ice and gets stuck around a fish. The fish then swims through a liquor spill from the dog's casket; the drunken fish grabs an ax and, swimming in a circle, dunks another skater. He then latches onto Squeals' skates, and hauls him into an ice-skating contest, where the fish-induced antics win him first prize. Squeals fills the loving cup from the dog's cask, and the fish swims off with it.

cartoons.title: Daffy Duck in Hollywood
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck wreaks havoc at a movie studio, hoping to become a film star.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck causes trouble on a Hollywood film set.

cartoons.title: Hamateur Night
cartoons.desc: A bored emcee hosts an amateur talent night at a theater, featuring various oddball performers.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's amateur night at the local theatre, and a procession of bad acts comes and goes: various musicians, a magician, and some actors. But they keep getting interrupted by Egghead singing "She'll Be Comin' 'Round the Mountain".

cartoons.title: Have You Got Any Castles?
cartoons.desc: Characters from well-known literary works come to life inside a library for a midnight party.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A night in a small library. The characters of famous books come to life, referring to movie versions of these books.

cartoons.title: It's an Ill Wind
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig and his friend Dizzy Duck go fishing, but a tornado ruins their trip.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky and his friend Dizzy Duck go fishing, but their trip is cut short by a thunderstorm. They take refuge in an old building that appears haunted, though the biggest hazards are an old bearskin that lands on a swivel chair, a dog that gets tangled up in chains and a diving helmet, and their own clumsiness generally.

cartoons.title: Katnip Kollege
cartoons.desc: At Katnip Kollege, students in the 'Swingology' class must master jazz to pass their exams.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: At the Katnip Kollege, we see a roomful of cats taking a course in Swingology. Everyone swings except Johnny, who can't cut it and has to sit in the dunce chair. Miss Kitty Bright tells him to look her up when he learns how to swing. Finally, listening to the pendulum clock at night, Johnny gets the beat. He rushes out to where everyone is playing and sings "Easy As Rollin' Off a Log" to Kitty Bright. She joins in; he grabs a trumpet for an instrumental break, with the complete band. They both fall off a log; she covers him with kisses.

cartoons.title: Love and Curses
cartoons.desc: An old couple looks through a photo album recalling their melodramatic youth in a silent-film parody.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An aging hero is looking through the photo album and remembering the Gay 90's, and in particular a picnic interrupted by villainous Roger St. Clair, who tries to tempt Emily to the big city and away from Harold; when that fails, he takes her by force. Six months later, Harold is still searching; Emily is forced to sing on the stage of Roger's bar, accompanied by a barbershop quartet on "All Is Not Gold That Glitters." Harold passes by and hears her. Roger beans him with the curtain and ties Emily to the railroad tracks. Harold rescues her; there follows a sawmill scene, a shootout, and ultimately victory for our hero. Back in the present day, they wonder what happened to Roger, which is his cue.

cartoons.title: Mice Will Play
cartoons.desc: A colony of mice play freely outside their mouse holes when the cat is away.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The mice are on the loose after hours in a doctor's office, playing with the various pieces of medical apparatus. Susie Mouse is caged for research until her lover Johnnie frees her. A mouse orchestra plays a swinging wedding song. But throughout, a cat is stalking...

cartoons.title: Now That Summer Is Gone
cartoons.desc: A young squirrel gambles away his winter nut supply instead of saving responsibly.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Summer is gone, and throughout the forest young squirrels are working hard gathering acorns for the long cold winter ahead. But one such squirrel has a better idea: winning acorns by shooting dice.

cartoons.title: Old Smokey
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon with comedic vignettes set around a mountain or wilderness theme.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Engine Co. No. 1 is replacing faithful fire horse Old Smokey with a new engine, which Der Captain is very proud of. He soon gets a chance to test it, when his panicked wife calls; unfortunately, he's still learning how to use it. As he arrives, the ladder extends to its full range, with the Captain on top and most of the rungs missing.

cartoons.title: Porky & Daffy
cartoons.desc: In the home of Porky and Daffy, Porky finds a boxing ad and enters Daffy as a fighting rooster.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig is Daffy Duck's fight manager who gets Daffy a fight with "The Champion", but things get looney.

cartoons.title: Porky In Egypt
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig is a tourist in Egypt who misses his caravan and wanders into the desert encountering a sphinx.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky is a tourist. He's missed the main camel, so he rents one of his own. Both of them are soon overcome by the hot desert sun; the camel starts hallucinating, and marches off, playing the bagpipes. Porky sees the camel swimming in a pool, but it turns out to be a mirage. The camel eventually recovers enough to bring both of them back to town, where Porky goes mad.

cartoons.title: Porky in Wackyland
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig travels to the surreal land of Wackyland to capture the last Do-Do bird.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig travels to a surreal land in order to hunt and catch the elusive Do-Do bird, reportedly the last of its kind.

cartoons.title: Porky the Gob
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig serves on a battleship run by dogs, and the crew receives orders for a dangerous mission.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A battleship, manned mainly by dogs (and Porky), and whose captain sounds rather like Yosemite Sam, sets sail. When the crew threatens to get to the mess hall before the captain, he orders them to halt (and they do, some in mid-air). The ship gets a radio message of a reward for capturing the pirate submarine. The crew sets out in planes to go looking, leaving Porky behind. Naturally, the sub comes after the ship, and defense is up to Porky. He manages to repel the boarding party and winches the sub onboard with a plunger.

cartoons.title: Porky's Naughty Nephew
cartoons.desc: Porky takes his nephew Pinky to the beach, but the mischievous youngster keeps causing trouble.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky and Pinky go to the beach. As Porky tries to nap, Pinky keeps whacking him with his little shovel. Then he fakes drowning in a shallow puddle. Porky enters a swim race, and Pinky sets a fake shark to follow him.

cartoons.title: Porky's Spring Planting
cartoons.desc: Porky and his dog Streamline plant vegetables on the farm, but crows threaten to steal the crops.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky and his dog, Streamline, plant a large garden. The neighbor chickens see the garden as one big buffet/cafeteria.

cartoons.title: Porky's Tire Trouble
cartoons.desc: Porky works at the Snappy Rubber Company where his dog sneaks in and causes factory chaos.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky works at the Snappy Rubber Company. His dog, Flat Foot Flookey, is determined to follow him into the plant, despite the rules. And Flookey's clumsiness means he's not exactly going to be able to sneak in like when he falls into a vat of rubberizing solution, and molds his face into a number of then-popular movie stars, or makes Porky's boss (a walrus) drop a stack of tires.

cartoons.title: Poultry Pirates
cartoons.desc: Porky owns a bakery that a bee and a fly invade, leading to a slapstick pest-control battle.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The ducks and chickens next door eye the Captain's garden covetously through a poorly mended fence. The Captain, armed with a board, is standing guard (but not fixing the fence). He falls asleep, and the poultry attack, stripping the garden methodically. When the Captain comes after them, they lock him into a shed. He gets out, and fetches his shotgun. That stops them, and they drop their booty, until the Captain sets his gun down to collect the veggies; the birds all rush in, snatch them back.

cartoons.title: Prest-O Change-O
cartoons.desc: Two curious puppies hide in a magician's house where a mischievous proto-Bugs rabbit torments them.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Fleeing from a dog catcher, the Two Curious Puppies run to an old remote house where, upon entering, they are continually subject to the antics of a mischievous magician's rabbit and surprise by the house's magical items.

cartoons.title: Robin Hood Makes Good
cartoons.desc: Three squirrels read about Robin Hood and decide to act out the legend in their forest.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A fox captures two young squirrels while they're playing "Robin Hood". Their small younger friend uses his ingenuity to try to rescue them.

cartoons.title: The Captain's Christmas
cartoons.desc: A holiday-themed cartoon featuring a captain and his crew celebrating Christmas aboard their vessel.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Pirate John and his crew threaten Christmas after taking over the Captain's role as Santa.

cartoons.title: The Mice Will Play
cartoons.desc: Mice come out to play while the household cat is away, throwing a wild party.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The mice are on the loose after hours in a doctor's office, playing with the various pieces of medical apparatus. Susie Mouse is caged for research until her lover Johnnie frees her. A mouse orchestra plays a swinging wedding song. But throughout, a cat is stalking...

cartoons.title: The Night Watchman
cartoons.desc: A kitten takes over night watchman duties in a kitchen and must fend off a sneaky cat burglar.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A little cat must take his sick father's place as night watchman, but is bullied by a tough mouse and his gang, leaving the rest of the mice free to eat all the food and stage a musical floor show.

cartoons.title: The Penguin Parade
cartoons.desc: A nightclub for penguins called 'The Club Iceberg' hosts a jazzy evening of entertainment in the Arctic.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An evening at a night club for penguins, (and a walrus or two). The stage show includes an appearance by a penguin incarnation of Bing Crosby, who sings a jazzy version of, "When my dreamboat comes home". The band's three singers do a scat version as well. This is followed by a full swing band instrumental of the song which works the band into a "Hot Jazz" frenzy, literaly melting some of the instruments.

cartoons.title: Wacky Wildlife
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon featuring comedic nature observations about animals living in the wild.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A series of typical Avery spot gags set around wild animals. A dainty deer drinks very loudly and rudely from a lake. A pack rat swaps an egg and an acorn, then back again ("monotonous, isn't it?"). A flock of ducks lands; a hunter fires; all fly away, except one with an American flag on its side. A termite fells a huge tree. A cowboy rides across the plains well, no; his horse is just slapping itself with the front hooves. A coyote calls to its mate: "Hey, Mabel, come on out!" A camel contradicts the narrator, saying he's really thirsty. A wild dog: because of the lumbermen.

cartoons.title: What Price Porky
cartoons.desc: When Porky goes to feed his chickens, a group of ducks led by Daffy steal all the corn.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky tries to feed his chickens, but some ducks steal the corn he puts out, then declare war. The battle rages, with the ducks against the chickens, sometimes in wing-to-wing combat, but also aerial attacks, and Porky finally turning the tide with his machine gun improvised from a wringer washer and a bag of corn. But the ducks still get the last laugh.

cartoons.title: Wholly Smoke
cartoons.desc: On his way to church, Porky encounters a bully and tries smoking, then has a nightmare about tobacco.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A neighborhood bully convinces Porky to take a puff from his cigar, causing Porky to hallucinate a smoke-man named Nick O. Teen, along with a musical number done by cigars, cigarettes and pipes in the likeness of the 3 Stooges, etc.

cartoons.title: A Day at the Zoo
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon set at the 'Kalama Zoo' featuring animal gags narrated by Elmer Fudd.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A tour of the zoo, in typical Tex Avery style: a series of one-liners and sight gags, punctuated by Egghead teasing a lion at intervals, despite the admonishments of the narrator.

cartoons.title: Bars and Stripes Forever
cartoons.desc: Anthropomorphic dog prisoners use every trick possible to attempt a jailbreak.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A collection of gags set inside a prison.

cartoons.title: Believe It Or Else
cartoons.desc: A parody of Ripley's Believe It or Not featuring absurd and impossible facts narrated by Egghead.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In this Ripley's Believe It or Not! parody, some of the supposed curiosities we are shown are a man who daily drinks fifty quarts of milk, the world's loudest hog caller, a human basketball, a new giant telescope showing life on Mars, and a man who saws people in half.

cartoons.title: Chicken Jitters
cartoons.desc: Porky runs a poultry farm where a newly hatched chick causes havoc among the hens.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky runs a poultry farm. All is well, if a bit unconventional, until the wolf attacks.

cartoons.title: Daffy Duck and the Dinosaur
cartoons.desc: A caveman and his pet dinosaur hunt Daffy Duck in prehistoric times.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Casper Caveman is hungry, so he tries to hunt for a duck, Daffy Duck.

cartoons.title: Dangerous Dan McFoo
cartoons.desc: A parody of 'The Shooting of Dan McGrew' starring anthropomorphic dogs in a Yukon saloon.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An arctic saloon. The tiny dog, Dan McFoo, is playing a pinball-like marble game in the back. His girlfriend, Sue, sounding like Katharine Hepburn, stands by. A stranger comes in with eyes for Sue; he begins a boxing match with Dan. After Dan gets knocked down, he accuses the stranger of having something in the glove; the ref finds four horseshoes and a horse. After the fight goes on a while with no conclusion, the narrator tosses a couple of guns, the lights go out, and Dan is shot or is he?

cartoons.title: Dog Gone Modern
cartoons.desc: Two curious puppies explore a futuristic automated house full of malfunctioning modern gadgets.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Two Curious Puppies visit a model home with a panoply of modern inventions, including an annoying robot that sweeps up anything that touches the floor.

cartoons.title: Fresh Fish
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon involving sea animals and underwater life seen from a tour boat.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A tour of the waters near a South Sea island, introducing us to the various kinds of marine life, including the pickled herring, the hermit crab, the starfish, a seahorse race, and many other puns. Among the running gags, a two-headed fish who keeps asking for directions to Mr. Ripley and a professor in a diving sphere looking for a rare wim-wam whistling shark.

cartoons.title: Gold Rush Daze
cartoons.desc: A gas station attendant tells the story of his fruitless chase for gold during the Gold Rush.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A hound dog drives to the hills to dig for gold. However, a gas station hound explains that, in 1849, there was a major gold rush in the hills at the first sight of gold, and he didn't have any success digging, mining, or panning any gold. When a horseman reports a sign of gold, the station hound trades his gas station for the other hound's car and equipment.

cartoons.title: Hare-um Scare-um
cartoons.desc: A hunter goes after a wacky rabbit (a proto-Bugs Bunny) who proves impossible to catch.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Facing high meat prices, a man decides to take his dog and go hunt for his own. Unfortunately, the crazy rabbit they are after is cleverer than they are.

cartoons.title: Little Brother Rat
cartoons.desc: Sniffles steals a cat's whiskers and brings them to a party as a dare, nearly getting caught.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sniffles the mouse has to get an owl's egg for a scavenger hunt, but once he's gotten it, the egg hatches and draws the attention of the mouse-eating father owl.

cartoons.title: Mama's New Hat
cartoons.desc: A mother's fancy new hat causes comedic problems for her family during an outing.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The boys buy mama a new hat for Mother's Day, but on the way home fall in the mud and ruin it. They swap the bad hat with one that a nearby horse is wearing and head home.

cartoons.title: Naughty but Mice
cartoons.desc: Sniffles the mouse catches a cold and ventures to a drugstore late at night to find medicine.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sniffles the mouse, in his first appearance in a Warner Bros. cartoon, goes to a drugstore and gets drunk on a cold remedy, then befriends an electric razor and gets it drunk as well.

cartoons.title: Naughty Neighbors
cartoons.desc: The McCoy clan led by Porky and the Martin clan led by Petunia feud until romance bridges the gap.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Petunia Pig and Porky Pig, each from two families, declare peace between their respective families, the Martins and the McCoys, who have been violently feuding all this time. The happy disposition doesn't last long, and soon the Martins and McCoys are fighting again.

cartoons.title: Old Glory
cartoons.desc: Uncle Sam teaches Porky Pig a patriotic lesson about American history and the Pledge of Allegiance.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig balks at learning the Pledge of Allegiance until Uncle Sam appears to him in a dream and gives him a lesson in American history.

cartoons.title: Pied Piper Porky
cartoons.desc: Porky plays his pipe like the Pied Piper, accidentally attracting a persistent mouse who won't leave.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig stars as the Pied Piper, who thinks he has taken care of all the mice. However, there is one rogue mouse that he is unable to catch. So he tries an old-fashioned mouse trap (a cat) and when the cat tries to fight, the result is the mouse wearing his fur (i.e. he killed him).

cartoons.title: Polar Pals
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig lives in an igloo in the Arctic and befriends the local polar bears and walruses.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig inhabits an igloo in the Arctic, where he beds with a covering a several live, furry polar bears, bathes in a shower whose water instantly freezes into long icicles, and dances in the ice and snow with the native fauna. When a greedy fur trapper named I. Killem arrives to threaten Porky's walrus, bear, and seal friends, Porky acts to repel the trapper by firing a musket which spits out buckshot and explosives. Killem flees in what he thinks is a kayak but is actually a whale.

cartoons.title: Porky and Teabiscuit
cartoons.desc: Porky's father sends him to deliver feed to the horse racetrack, where Porky enters a race instead.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig is sent out by his father with $11.00 spending money for help on the farm, unfortunately, he accidentally spends it on an auction, for a sickly, broken-down race horse known as Tea Biscuit. Porky shapes him up for a race, although Tea Biscuit's attention is diverted to a trombone. However, a balloon pop assures that Porky wins with Tea Biscuit and gets the reward...

cartoons.title: Porky the Giant Killer
cartoons.desc: Porky sneaks inside a giant's castle in a retelling of Jack and the Beanstalk.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig is tapped for a giant-killing expedition. When the expedition reaches the giant's castle (which is normal sized, requiring the giant to stoop), everyone but Porky runs at the first sign of trouble, leaving Porky alone to awaken the giant's sleeping baby. Porky then must soothe the baby back to sleep and attempt his escape.

cartoons.title: Porky's Hotel
cartoons.desc: Porky runs a small-town hotel where a goat guest causes nonstop destruction eating everything in sight.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky runs a small-town hotel. An old goat with gout checks in for a rest, but a talkative goose child will prevent him from getting it.

cartoons.title: Screwball Football
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon set in a football stadium with absurd plays and audience reactions.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A series of gags hung on a football game.

cartoons.title: Seal Skinners
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon set in the Arctic featuring comedic encounters with seals and polar wildlife.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A trained seal has escaped from the Jingling Brothers circus; there's a $100,000 reward. Both the Captain and John Silver hear this news, as does the seal. They show up, offering the seal a ride to wherever she wants to go; home to the arctic, as it happens. The Captain wins round one, grabbing the seal while John is engaged in a fight with yet another reward seeker.

cartoons.title: Sniffles and the Bookworm
cartoons.desc: Sniffles the curious little mouse embarks on a gentle adventure that leads to unexpected complications.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Literary characters come to life late at night in a bookshop, serenading Sniffles the mouse with swing music until the Frankenstein monster intrudes.

cartoons.title: Snowman's Land
cartoons.desc: A small Mountie in northern Canada goes up against a massive outlaw in the frozen wilderness.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In the Canadian north, a little Mountie runs afoul of the dread outlaw, Dirty Pierre.

cartoons.title: The Curious Puppy
cartoons.desc: Two curious puppies explore a closed amusement park at night, getting into trouble on the rides.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While pursuing a little dog who's wandered into an amusement park at night, the park's watchdog accidentally switches on the power to all the rides and attractions, bewildering the pair of canines.

cartoons.title: The Film Fan
cartoons.desc: Porky is sent to the grocery store but gets distracted sneaking into a movie theater instead.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig is on his way to the store to pick up some groceries for his mother when he walks by a sign saying that the local movie theater is having a "kids admitted free" day. The excited Porky rushes in and views a series of spoofs of newsreels, movie trailers, feature films, and even the Lone Ranger!

cartoons.title: The Good Egg
cartoons.desc: A hen with no chicks of her own adopts a turtle egg and raises the hatchling as her own.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A hen adopts an abandoned egg which hatches into a turtle. The baby turtle becomes the butt of all the real chicks' jokes until danger threatens.

cartoons.title: Thugs with Dirty Mugs
cartoons.desc: Gangster Killer Diller and his crew rob banks in numerical order while the police bungle the case.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Killer Diller and his gang are robbing every bank in town in numerical order (except the 13th National Bank, which they skip out of superstition). Despite their predictable actions, the police are unable to catch them...until they get a tip from an unlikely source.

context.title: The 1940s — The Golden Age Begins
context.summary: The 1940s are widely considered the creative peak of American animation. Bugs Bunny made his definitive debut in 1940's A Wild Hare and immediately became a cultural phenomenon — cool, confident, and utterly unflappable. World War II brought a wave of patriotic and satirical cartoons, with Bugs, Daffy, and Porky regularly outsmarting Nazi and Japanese caricatures. Tex Avery left for MGM in 1942 but Chuck Jones, Bob Clampett, and Friz Freleng continued pushing the art form. The studio pioneered expressionistic backgrounds, surreal gag timing, and self-referential humor that broke the fourth wall years before it was fashionable. Tweety Bird debuted in 1942, Yosemite Sam in 1945, and Pepé Le Pew in 1945.

cartoons.title: A Gander at Mother Goose
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon featuring comedic twists on classic nursery rhymes.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A series of gags based on Mother Goose stories.

cartoons.title: A Wild Hare
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd hunts rabbits in the forest but is thoroughly outsmarted by Bugs Bunny in his official debut.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While hunting rabbits, Elmer Fudd comes across Bugs Bunny who tricks and harasses him.

cartoons.title: Bedtime for Sniffles
cartoons.desc: Sniffles the curious little mouse embarks on a gentle adventure that leads to unexpected complications.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Christmas Eve, and Sniffles is determined to stay awake to see Santa. Not an easy task.

cartoons.title: Busy Bakers
cartoons.desc: A local baker facing bankruptcy receives help from a kind deed that saves his shop.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Struggling Swenson the baker is down to just a single donut remaining in his shop. He gives it to a blind beggar who stops in. Later, while Swenson sleeps, the kindness is rewarded.

cartoons.title: Calling Dr. Porky
cartoons.desc: Porky is a medical doctor treating patients at New Rightus Hospital with comedic remedies.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A dog thinks he is being chased by small pink elephants, and goes to the hospital. While Porky is working on medication, the pink elephants find him and cause havoc. Porky finally gives him some medication, that only works temporarily, but then sees them again. He rushes back in and is once again ill.

cartoons.title: Ceiling Hero
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon featuring the latest absurd developments in modern aviation and flying.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A series of blackout gags parodying aviation and aviation films. Gags include a parchutist whose parachute reads "Good to the last drop", jokes about LA's expanding city limits, and a satire of test pilot and their bravery.

cartoons.title: Circus Today
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon set in a circus featuring gags about acrobats, clowns, and performing animals.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A barker guides us through a sideshow, a menagerie, and on to the big top, for a series of typical Avery gags. For example, the trapeze artists, the Flying Cadenzas, literally fly; the lion puts his head in the tamer's mouth; and the human cannonball flies around the world.

cartoons.title: Cross Country Detours
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag travelogue featuring comic observations about the wonders of nature across the United States.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A wacky travelogue takes us to the forests of Yosemite, the rocks of Brice Canyon, the frozen wastes of Alaska, the desert wastes of New Mexico, the Grand Canyon, the Colorado River and the giant redwoods of California.

cartoons.title: Elmer's Candid Camera
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd tries to photograph a rabbit with his camera, encountering an early version of Bugs Bunny.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer takes up wildlife photography but finds his subject, a rabbit, much too rascally.

cartoons.title: Elmer's Pet Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd buys Bugs Bunny as a pet from a store — the first cartoon to use Bugs Bunny's name on screen.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd gets more than he bargained for from his new pet rabbit.

cartoons.title: Gallopin' Gals
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon featuring comedic vignettes about horse racing and equestrian events.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Time for the big horse race. We drop in on the fillies, who gossip in New York accents. That's followed by a pre-race fashion parade. In both of these, Maggie doesn't join in; she runs every year, but suffers from hay fever. The actual race is run, and Maggie is far back in the field. The other horses hear it's going to be a photo finish, though, and they all come to an abrupt stop and pose except Maggie, who keeps running and wins the race.

cartoons.title: Ghost Wanted
cartoons.desc: A little ghost applies for a house-haunting job at a spooky address but can't scare anyone.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An inexperienced little ghost tries out for a house-haunting job, but winds up getting terrorized by the fat ghost interviewing him for the position.

cartoons.title: Good Night Elmer
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd desperately tries to blow out a stubborn bedside candle so he can get to sleep.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd spends an endless night trying to fall asleep amid myriad frustrations, in particular, a candle that won't go out.

cartoons.title: Holiday Highlights
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon featuring comedic gags themed around various holidays throughout the year.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Blackout gags about the holidays. New Year's (the baby speaks to us). Valentine's Day, Washington Day, etc.

cartoons.title: Of Fox and Hounds
cartoons.desc: Dim-witted dog Willoughby keeps asking a sly fox where the fox went, never realizing he's talking to him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Willoughby, a big dumb hound, is repeatedly tricked by George, the fox, into jumping off cliffs, among other things.

cartoons.title: Patient Porky
cartoons.desc: Porky visits a hospital with a stomach ache from overeating at his birthday party and encounters wacky patients.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky checks into a hospital with a tummyache; he has the bad luck to encounter a patient posing a "Dr. Chilled-Air" who is a bit too eager to operate.

cartoons.title: Porky's Baseball Broadcast
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig provides the radio play-by-play commentary for a baseball game full of spot gags.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig provides play-by-play radio-broadcast commentary during a World Series baseball game.

cartoons.title: Porky's Hired Hand
cartoons.desc: Porky hires the slow-witted Gregory Grunt to help prevent a marten from raiding the henhouse.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky hires on Gregory Grunt to watch over his chicken coop to stop the fox from raiding it. He falls asleep, and the fox fills his bag. But Grunt wakes up and confronts the fox who then talks him into becoming his partner. As the fox is leaving, he locks himself into the incubator room by mistake. Porky hears the noise and comes running.

cartoons.title: Porky's Poor Fish
cartoons.desc: While Porky is out to lunch from his fish store, the fish attempt to attack the cat left guarding them.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig owns a fish store and goes out to lunch. After a cat is not having much success with a mouse, he goes into the fish store when Porky is away. When the cat thinks he has the good appetite, the fish go to war against him and drive him out of the store. He is then freaked out by the mouse and shrinks as the mouse grows.

cartoons.title: Porky's Snooze Reel
cartoons.desc: Porky presents a newsreel parody loaded with comedic spot gags — the final black-and-white Looney Tune.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig narrates a very strange newsreel.

cartoons.title: Prehistoric Porky
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig attempts to hunt a bear in prehistoric times, set in 'One Billion, Trillion B.C.'
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Caveman Porky awakens and plays with his pet Rover, a massive dinosaur. After Rover's playfulness causes a disruption to the prehistoric peace and quiet, Porky's copy of "Expire" magazine arrives in the mail, filled with ads for fashionable new bearskins. Porky decides to go out and get himself a new suit, and sets off with his trusty club.

cartoons.title: Shop, Look and Listen
cartoons.desc: Little Blabbermouse takes an after-hours tour of a department store guided by W.C. Field Mouse.

cartoons.title: Slap Happy Pappy
cartoons.desc: Most of farmer Eddie Cackler's chicks hatch normally on Porky's farm, except one troublemaking latecomer.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky runs a farm; we see him plowing the fields. But it's primarily a poultry farm; as the sign says, "For sale: Miracle eggs if it's a good egg, it's a miracle." A rabbit, doing a Jack Benny impression (Jack Bunny), paints and inspects eggs. He starts to smash and reject a black egg, but it hatches into a black baby bird doing a Rochester impression. We next visit the Eddie Cackler family, (Eddie Cantor) who have been trying without success to have a son; the next five eggs hatch, and they are again all girls. A Bing Crosby lookalike comes by with a stroller full of sons, and Eddie asks for his secret; he demonstrates by crooning to a chick, who lays dozens of eggs with boys names on them. Eddie croons to his wife, but in a higher pitch, then dances out singing the theme song to other caricatures. The egg hatches, but in answer to Eddie's question, is it really a boy? "Mmmm... could be."

cartoons.title: Sniffles Bells the Cat
cartoons.desc: Sniffles the curious little mouse embarks on a gentle adventure that leads to unexpected complications.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sniffles the mouse's friends talk him into putting a bell around the neck of the troublesome house cat.

cartoons.title: Sniffles Takes a Trip
cartoons.desc: Sniffles the curious little mouse embarks on a gentle adventure that leads to unexpected complications.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sniffles the mouse is in the country for a restful vacation of fresh air, enjoyment of nature, and peace and quiet.

cartoons.title: Stage Fright
cartoons.desc: The Two Curious Puppies chase each other into a vaudeville theater after hours, disrupting the empty stage.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Two Curious Puppies wander into a theater and run afoul of a trickster magician's rabbit, a playful seal and an intimidating little bird.

cartoons.title: The Bears Tale
cartoons.desc: A comedic retelling of Goldilocks and the Three Bears with Tex Avery's signature gag style.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Three Bears meets Little Red Riding Hood, told in the style of Tex Avery.

cartoons.title: The Chewin Bruin
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig listens to an old dog tell the story of the mounted bear head on the wall.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A bear hunter tells Porky the tale of a hunt 30 years ago: a bear got a taste of his chewing tobacco and chased him down to get it; the hunter took the bear on with his bare hands rather than lose the tobacco. Or at least, that's the way he told the story.

cartoons.title: The Egg Collector
cartoons.desc: Sniffles reads 'Egg Collecting for Amateurs' and takes the Bookworm on a dangerous egg-hunting expedition.
