cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sniffles the mouse and his friend the Bookworm decide to take up egg collecting, setting their eyes upon a big barn owl egg. But the big barn owl isn't so hot on the idea.

cartoons.title: The Sour Puss
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig goes fishing with a grumpy cat companion who spoils the trip with his bad attitude.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky decides to go fishing the next day and tells his cat. The cat sleeps fitfully. The next day, while they are fishing, the cat gets into a battle with a flying fish who behaves rather like Daffy Duck.

cartoons.title: The Timid Toreador
cartoons.desc: A timid would-be bullfighter must face a ferocious bull in the arena despite his terrible stage fright.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The bull makes short work of the matador, and then turns on Porky, a tamale vendor who wanders into the ring accidentally. But then he makes the mistake of actually eating most of Porky's extra hot tamales.

cartoons.title: Tom Thumb in Trouble
cartoons.desc: Tiny Tom Thumb falls into water while his father is away, and a bird rescues him from drowning.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tom's father mistakenly believes that the little bird who's just rescued his tiny son from drowning in the dishwater is attacking the boy and drives it away. Tom sets off to find the bird and gets lost in a fierce snowstorm.

cartoons.title: You Ought to Be in Pictures
cartoons.desc: Daffy tricks Porky into quitting cartoons for live-action films in this groundbreaking live-action/animation hybrid.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck convinces Porky Pig to quit the cartoon biz and try his luck in the features. Porky's adventures begin when he tries to enter the movie studio.

cartoons.title: Bug Parade
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon featuring comedic observations about various insects and their behaviors.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A spot gag cartoon presenting amusing facts about bugs.

cartoons.title: Farm Frolics
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon set on a farm featuring animal gags, including newsreel-style wildlife observations.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A series of wacky vignettes involving farm animals.

cartoons.title: Goofy Groceries
cartoons.desc: Food product mascots come alive in a closed grocery store at night, led by a Jack Bunny character.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Grocery store products come to life, along with caricatures of Jack Benny, Rochester and Ned Sparks, and take-offs on Superman and King Kong.

cartoons.title: Hiawatha's Rabbit Hunt
cartoons.desc: A young Hiawatha tries to hunt Bugs Bunny, who easily outwits him at every turn.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny is hunted by Hiawatha, a stereotyped Native American who fills roughly the same role as Elmer Fudd in other Bugs Bunny cartoons of this era.

cartoons.title: Hollywood Steps Out
cartoons.desc: Celebrity caricatures gather for a star-studded evening at Ciro's nightclub in Hollywood.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A tour of Ciro's Nightclub packed with caricatures of many top stars.

cartoons.title: Joe Glow, the Firefly
cartoons.desc: A glowing firefly named Joe investigates a camper's tent at night, creating havoc with his light.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A firefly with a miner's lighted helmet explores a camper's tent and the various people-sized items that, from his perspective, are gigantic, then utters the cartoon's single line of dialog.

cartoons.title: Little Blabbermouse
cartoons.desc: W.C. Field Mouse leads tours of a drugstore for other mice, including the motor-mouthed Little Blabbermouse.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A mouse, imitating W.C. Fields, leads a nighttime tour of a closed drugstore for other mice, that include a very inquisitive little boy.

cartoons.title: Meet John Doughboy
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig presents a World War II newsreel titled 'Porky Pig Pictorial' full of wartime spot gags.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky introduces a newsreel of wartime spot gags, including a spoof of the RKO Pictures logo, and caricatures of Jack Benny and Rochester.

cartoons.title: Notes to You
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig is ready for bed, but a cat singing opera in the alley keeps him awake all night.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Without success, Porky Pig constantly tries to silence an alley cat who has been disturbing his slumber by constantly singing loudly.

cartoons.title: Porky's Bear Facts
cartoons.desc: In an adaptation of 'The Ant and the Grasshopper,' a lazy bear refuses to work while Porky prepares for winter.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig works hard on his farm all year. On a neighboring farm, a bear lazes around and allows his animals to be idle. The winter comes, and he has nothing to eat.

cartoons.title: Porky's Pooch
cartoons.desc: A dog named Rover tells a stray Scottie his life story about finding a master in Porky Pig.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A dog named Rover explains to a Scottish Terrier named Sandy how he got his master. He went into a hotel room and bothered someone taking a bath--Porky Pig. Porky doesn't want Rover as a pet, no matter how many times Rover tries to make Porky adopt him.

cartoons.title: Porky's Prize Pony
cartoons.desc: Porky prepares his horse for a race at the County Fair but things don't go as planned.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When jockey Porky's thoroughbred gets drunk on linament, a goofy milk-wagon horse takes over for the big race.

cartoons.title: Rhapsody in Rivets
cartoons.desc: Construction workers build a skyscraper synchronized to classical music, like a visual symphony of labor.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A construction foreman conducts his workers like a symphony orchestra as they build a skyscraper to Franz Liszt's "Hungarian Rhapsody No. 2."

cartoons.title: Rookie Revue
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon featuring soldiers training for World War II with comedic military mishaps.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Random gags around military life, set on an army base. A bugler uses a jukebox to play reveille. In formation, one private has a great deal of trouble remembering what comes after "3"; after he gets it, he decides not to go for the $32 question. In the mess hall, the machine gunners machine gun their food while the bombers catch falling biscuits. The infantry marches for miles - past a "next time, take the train" billboard.

cartoons.title: Saddle Silly
cartoons.desc: A bumbling cowboy horse dreams of being a great Western steed but proves hilariously incompetent at every task put before him.

cartoons.title: Sport Chumpions
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon featuring comedic takes on various sports and athletic competitions.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Late at night, the mice come out and sing and play to the title tune, among others. That is, until the cat arrives, but he's quickly sent packing.

cartoons.title: The Brave Little Bat
cartoons.desc: Sniffles' car breaks down at night, so he takes shelter in a windmill where a friendly bat lives.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When his car breaks down out in the country, Sniffles the mouse takes shelter in an old mill, where he meets up with "Batty," a non-stop-talking little bat who later save Sniffles from a hungry cat.

cartoons.title: The Cagey Canary
cartoons.desc: A cat tries to catch a clever canary while an old woman in the house hates all the noise.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A cat (not Sylvester) tries to capture a little canary bird (not Tweety), and not get caught by protective Granny.

cartoons.title: The Cats Tale
cartoons.desc: A frustrated mouse hires a cowardly cat bodyguard to protect him from a bulldog.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The mouse, tired of being chased by the cat, convinces him there's no reason for it, and that the cat should talk to the dog and convince him too. The talks are not successful...

cartoons.title: The Crackpot Quail
cartoons.desc: Dim-witted dog Willoughby goes quail hunting but the crackpot quail outsmarts him at every turn.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A dog chasing a quail keeps getting outsmarted.

cartoons.title: The Fighting 69 1/2th
cartoons.desc: Red ants and black ants wage a miniature war at a picnic site after a couple leaves their food behind.

cartoons.title: The Haunted Mouse
cartoons.desc: A starving cat enters a house haunted by a ghostly mouse who torments him — the first one-shot Looney Tune.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A starving cat wandering in the wilderness sees a sign that says "Ma's Place / Home Cooking / 3 Miles". The cat speeds into town, without noticing the sign that says "Ghost Town / Population 100 Ghosts". One of these ghosts is a mouse eager to get revenge on cats for having tormented him in life.

cartoons.title: The Heckling Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is hunted by Willoughby the dog, but the dim pooch falls for every one of Bugs' traps.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs is being chased by hunting dog Willoughby, and outsmarts him at every turn, until the end, where they outsmart the audience together.

cartoons.title: The Henpecked Duck
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck goes to divorce court after losing his wife's egg, with Porky Pig as the judge.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Mrs. Duck sues Daffy for divorce in Judge Porky Pig's courtroom, charging her husband with losing their egg in an abortive magic trick.

cartoons.title: The Wacky Worm
cartoons.desc: A Jerry Colonna-like singing worm evades a hungry crow by using jokes and musical numbers.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A worm is pursued by a crow.

cartoons.title: Tortoise Beats Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny challenges Cecil Turtle to a race, confident he'll win, but Cecil has a clever scheme.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny challenges slick Cecil Turtle to a race.

cartoons.title: Toy Trouble
cartoons.desc: Sniffles and the Bookworm turn on all the toys in a toy store for a wild after-hours playtime.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sniffles the mouse and his friend the Bookworm try to evade a cat in a the toy department of Lacy's department store.

cartoons.title: Trial of Mr. Wolf
cartoons.desc: Mr. Wolf gives his testimony in court, claiming his version of the Little Red Riding Hood story.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Big Bad Wolf is on trial for crimes committed against Little Red Riding Hood and her grandmother. When given a chance to speak in his defense, Mr. Wolf explains the supposed real story: He is the victim.

cartoons.title: Wabbit Twouble
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd goes hunting for 'wabbits' but is thoroughly outsmarted by the clever Bugs Bunny.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd expects to find "west and wewaxation" during his visit to Jellostone National Park, but he sets up camp in Bugs' backyard, and the rabbit (and a neighboring bear) definitely don't have leisure in mind.

cartoons.title: A Tale of Two Kitties
cartoons.desc: Cat duo Babbit and Catstello try to catch a small yellow bird — Tweety's first appearance.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two alley cats, Babbitt and Catsello, decide to make a meal out of Orson as he sleeps in his nest atop a telephone pole. The gullible (and loud) Catsello is repeatedly gulled into trying to "get the bird," earning a variety of thrashings from the casually murderous little canary. Catsello finally resorts to an air strike (with a pair of wooden boards for wings), but it's wartime, and Orson has the cat blasted out of the sky by anti-aircraft guns.

cartoons.title: Aloha Hooey
cartoons.desc: Cecil Crow from Iowa travels to Hawaii hoping to see a hula dancer but finds only trouble.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sammy Seagull and Cecil Crow have stowed away on the same ship. Cecil, from Iowa, wants to see a hula dancer; fortunately, they've just come within flying distance of a tropical island with a lovely dancer. They take turns trying to impress her, with such stunts as skywriting hearts (Cecil almost drowns) and fancy dives (Cecil almost crashes, then gets into a fight with a shark, a turtle, and a starfish).

cartoons.title: Bugs Bunny Gets the Boid
cartoons.desc: Beaky Buzzard's mother sends him to catch a rabbit, but Bugs Bunny is more than he can handle.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Mama Buzzard wants her children to learn to bring back meat for dinner. One buzzardling is shy and has to be kicked out of the nest. He's told to at least bring back a rabbit.

cartoons.title: Case of the Missing Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny crashes magician Ala Bahma's stage show and turns the tables with his own tricks.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After a traveling magician puts a poster over the entrance to his home, Bugs visits his act to get revenge.

cartoons.title: Confusions of a Nutsy Spy
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig and his bloodhound track down a Nazi spy in a wartime espionage parody.

cartoons.title: Conrad the Sailor
cartoons.desc: Conrad the Cat serves on a battleship but keeps getting pranked by the irrepressible Daffy Duck.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Conrad, a sailor aboard a Navy battleship, is swabbing the deck when he is interrupted and tormented by Daffy Duck.

cartoons.title: Daffy's Southern Exposure
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck refuses to fly south for winter and must survive the cold with two hungry foxes nearby.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's the dead of winter, and Daffy Duck is starving. A fox and a weasel invite him into their cabin and feed him beans. But they have an ulterior motive--namely eating Daffy.

cartoons.title: Ding Dog Daddy
cartoons.desc: A lovestruck dog mistakes an iron dog statue for a real female and courts it relentlessly.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: For the first three minutes or so of this one, Bosko doesn't really have much to do and is only seen briefly playing a musical instrument. Instead, the bulk of the time is spent following a lamb and its interactions with a couple of insects.

cartoons.title: Dog Tired
cartoons.desc: The Two Curious Puppies chase each other into a crowded city kennel while fighting over a bone.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Two Curious Puppies get into mischief at the zoo.

cartoons.title: Double Chaser
cartoons.desc: A mouse being chased by a cat enlists a sleeping bulldog as protection, playing them against each other.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A mouse, being chased by a cat, enlists the help of a sleeping bulldog. When the dog awakes, the mouse hides in a hen's nest, and the cat disguises himself as a hen - and even does a hen imitation when the chicks hatch. The mouse then keeps pointing out the cat's hiding places, but when he points to a dump where the cat isn't hiding, the dog turns on him. The mouse paints an apple black and lights the fuse, but it explodes and sends him to mouse heaven.

cartoons.title: Eatin' on the Cuff
cartoons.desc: A pianist narrates a story about a moth preparing for his wedding while avoiding a flame.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A live action piano player tells the story of a clothes-devouring moth who is on his way to marry a honey bee but gets caught by a black widow spider looking for a man of her own.

cartoons.title: Fox Pop
cartoons.desc: A red fox hears a radio ad for a fox farm's reward and tries to collect on himself.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Hearing that silver foxes are all the rage in high society, a fox paints himself silver and gets himself trapped, finding out too late that it's only his fur anyone is interested in.

cartoons.title: Gopher Goofy
cartoons.desc: Two gophers, including slow-paced Virgil, invade a homeowner's lawn and resist all attempts to remove them.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A homeowner is enjoying his lovely lawn and garden when it's invaded by a couple of gophers with Brooklyn accents. The homeowner attacks, but the gophers outsmart him at every turn: They duck his hoe and shotgun. He gasses them with helium, and they float away -- causing a crow to throw away his bottle. The inflated gophers hit a tree and fall to earth. The gardener fishes for the gophers under his hat; they substitute a tomato, and he cries, thinking he's squished a gopher. Next, he tries the garden hose; the gophers stop the flow until there's a huge blast of water, which they direct back at the homeowner. He hits the ground and starts burrowing himself, surfacing in his fountain.

cartoons.title: Hobby Horse-Laffs
cartoons.desc: A parody of the radio program Hobby Lobby featuring comedic blackout gags about people's hobbies.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A spoof of a popular radio program of the time called "Hobby Lobby", with blackout gags showcasing people's various hobbies and/or inventions.

cartoons.title: Hold the Lion, Please
cartoons.desc: Leo the Lion tries to prove he's still King of the Jungle, but Bugs Bunny humiliates him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A lion wants to prove he's still "King of the Jungle" and, to prove it, he hunts rabbit.

cartoons.title: Hop, Skip and a Chump
cartoons.desc: Two blackbirds compete to catch a clever grasshopper named Hopalong Casserole.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A grasshopper toys with two crows trying to catch him.

cartoons.title: Horton Hatches the Egg
cartoons.desc: Faithful elephant Horton sits on a lazy bird's egg through every hardship, based on the Dr. Seuss story.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Horton the elephant agrees to watch over lazy Maisie bird's egg while she vacations. Much later, after...

cartoons.title: Lights Fantastic
cartoons.desc: New York City's bright neon lights and signs all come to life at night for a musical spectacular.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A tour of the bright lights of New York City, where the various advertising signs come to life.

cartoons.title: My Favorite Duck
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig goes camping but Daffy Duck torments him relentlessly, hiding behind hunting regulations.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky tries to relax on a hunting and fishing trip, but Daffy, smugly pointing out the "No Duck Hunting" signs, subjects him to constant irritation. Then the "Duck Hunting Season Open" signs start going up.

cartoons.title: Nutty News
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag newsreel narrated by Elmer Fudd featuring Willoughby the dog in comic news segments.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd narrates a newsreel (but is never seen on screen). A hunter uses a moose call; the moose answers back using a hunter call. A barber uses an invention to startle a boy. A man uses a rear-view mirror to guard his hat while eating, but that's not all he should have guarded. In a laboratory, we see how rabbits multiply: 2x2=4, etc. Fireflies are having a blackout. An artist uses his thumb to get the proportions correct as a model is posing. A baby chick follows along as ducks take their first swim. In the South, the traffic signs read "No U-All Turns." A baseball pitcher throws a dollar across the Potomac, but it gets only halfway; his Scotty dog explains that a dollar doesn't go as far. A fox hunt: the dogs run in circles, because the lead dog is romancing the fox. A new department store is about to be built, and it's already attracted a protestor. Finally, we see a series of battleships, all in the rain except the U.S.S. California, in bright sunshine.

cartoons.title: Pigs in a Polka
cartoons.desc: A retelling of 'The Three Little Pigs' set to Brahms' Hungarian Dances — an Oscar nominee.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A tuxedo-clad wolf Master of Ceremonies announces the evening's program: the tale of the Big Bad Wolf and the Three Little Pigs, set to the music of Johannes Brahms's Hungarian Dances. Queue the fairy tale.

cartoons.title: Porky's Cafe
cartoons.desc: Porky serves as a waiter in his café, using mechanical gadgets to prepare food with comic results.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky uses his cafe's kitchen's mechanical gadgets to fix a meal for a diner, while cook Conrad Cat deals with an ant in the pancake mix.

cartoons.title: Porky's Pastry Pirates
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig owns a bakery that a bee and a fly invade, leading to a slapstick pest battle.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig owns a bakery. Enter a bee and a fly.

cartoons.title: The Bird Came C.O.D.
cartoons.desc: Conrad the Cat tries to deliver a potted palm tree to a theater but a bird keeps interfering.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Conrad Cat, has trouble getting a prop tree he's delivering to a theater into the door. Once inside, he falls victim to an intimidating little bird who hops out of a magician's hat.

cartoons.title: The Dover Boys at Pimento University
cartoons.desc: Three collegiate brothers rescue their girlfriend Dora from the villainous Dan Backslide in this stylized parody.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Three fun-loving, morally upright brothers from Pimento University save their fiancée from their fiendish archenemy, Dan Backslide, in this spoof of the Rover Boys.

cartoons.title: The Draft Horse
cartoons.desc: A patriotic farm horse desperately wants to enlist in the military to help the war effort.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In a burst of patriotism, a farm horse tries to join the army but finds out he's not really soldier material.

cartoons.title: The Ducktators
cartoons.desc: A wartime propaganda satire set on a poultry farm, lampooning Hitler, Mussolini, and Tojo as ducks and geese.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A wartime cartoon that satirizes the Axis leaders of World War II.

cartoons.title: The Hare-Brained Hypnotist
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd tries to use hypnotism to catch Bugs Bunny, but Bugs turns the hypnosis back on him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd goes after Bugs using hypnotism, only the plan backfires.

cartoons.title: The Hep Cat
cartoons.desc: A cool cat stumbles into Willoughby the dog, who tries to be his friend but can't control his appetite.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A cat-about-town fancies himself such an irresistible "hunk" he momentarily resembles Victor Mature. His wooing of a cute kitten gets derailed by a prankster dog using a cat hand puppet to trap him.

cartoons.title: The Impatient Patient
cartoons.desc: Telegram delivery duck Daffy gets a severe case of hiccups and visits Dr. Jerkyl's creepy office for a cure.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy has the hiccups and goes to Dr. Jerkyl to see if he can help stop them

cartoons.title: The Squawkin Hawk
cartoons.desc: Young Henery Hawk wants chicken for dinner and defies his mother, who insists he eat a worm instead.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Henery Hawk, making his first appearance in a Warner Bros. cartoon, refuses the worm his mother is trying to feed him; after all, he's a chicken hawk. That night, he sneaks out to the hen house, but comes up against a protective rooster.

cartoons.title: The Wabbit Who Came to Supper
cartoons.desc: Elmer can't harm Bugs Bunny because his uncle's will threatens to cut him off if he hurts any animals.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny exploits the situation when an uncle leaves Elmer Fudd three million dollars on the condition that he harm no animals, especially rabbits.

cartoons.title: The Wacky Wabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny torments Elmer Fudd as Elmer prospects for gold in the desert during WWII.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While seeking gold in the desert, prospector Elmer Fudd stumbles across mischievous Bugs Bunny.

cartoons.title: Wacky Blackout
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag cartoon set on a farm during World War II featuring wartime-themed animal gags.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: We tour a farm and see how the various animals are preparing for the war, in a series of blackout skits.

cartoons.title: A Corny Concerto
cartoons.desc: A Fantasia parody with Elmer Fudd conducting two musical segments featuring Bugs Bunny and Daffy Duck.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd introduces two pieces of classical music: "Tales of the Vienna Woods" and "The Blue Danube", and acted out by Bugs Bunny, Porky Pig, Laramore the Hound Dog, a family of swans, and a juvenile Daffy Duck.

cartoons.title: An Itch In Time
cartoons.desc: A flea moves into Elmer Fudd's dog's fur, causing the dog to scratch wildly — he'll be put out if he scratches.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer threatens to give his dog a bath if he doesn't stop scratching, but the poor pooch is the victim of a hungry flea whose tools of the trade include pickaxes and dynamite.

cartoons.title: Coal Black and de Sebben Dwarfs*
cartoons.desc: A jazz-infused, swing-era retelling of Snow White set in wartime, featuring bold caricature characters and an all-Black cast. [Controversial content — rough copy only]

cartoons.title: Daffy - The Commando
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck is a commando behind Nazi lines on a mission to Berlin to confront Hitler himself.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Commando Daffy Duck goes behind enemy lines and causes havoc for a Nazi German officer and his troops.

cartoons.title: Falling Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny battles a persistent gremlin who's sabotaging a military aircraft.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Relaxing with a carrot at a U.S. Army air field, Bugs is reading "Victory Through Hare Power" and scoffs at the notion of mentioned gremlins, little creatures who wreak havoc on planes with their diabolical sabotage.

cartoons.title: Fifth Column Mouse
cartoons.desc: A traitorous gray mouse helps a cat subjugate a mouse community, until the mice organize and rebel.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In an allegory for World War II, one mouse's attempt to appease the cat of the house in exchange for a regular supply of cheese puts all the mice in danger.

cartoons.title: Fin'n Catty
cartoons.desc: Claude Cat, who's terrified of water, tries desperately to catch a goldfish without getting wet.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: "As everyone knows," the narrator begins, "goldfish must have water... and cats hate water." And so it goes.

cartoons.title: Greetings Bait
cartoons.desc: A singing worm used as fishing bait must outwit hungry fish — the Wacky Worm's final appearance.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A worm reminiscent of Jerry Colonna is lowered into the water and uses various guises to lure fish. He also tangles with a crab.

cartoons.title: Hiss and Make Up
cartoons.desc: A cat named Wellington and a dog named Roscoe can't stop fighting, driving their owners crazy.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An old woman has a cat, a dog, and a canary. The cat and dog fight even worse than normally. Fed up, she tells them both off, then threatens to throw them both out if there's any more trouble.

cartoons.title: Hop and Go
cartoons.desc: A kangaroo named Claude Hopper gets help from two Scottish rabbits to improve his jumping technique.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Claude Hopper, a kangaroo, and "best darn hopper in the world," is full of himself (and dumb), so a couple of Scottish rabbits take him on. They set up a boxing ring; Claude gets tangled in the ropes. Next, he tries a distance leap, but the rabbits ride on his tail, then leap over as he lands. He tries again, without all the ballast in his pouch, but they've stuck his tail down with chewing gum. Claude falls into the river; the rabbits wash up in his water-filled pouch.

cartoons.title: Jack-Wabbit and the Beanstalk
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny climbs a beanstalk and outwits a bumbling giant at the top.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny encounters the legendary giant.

cartoons.title: Little Red Riding Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is carried to Grandma's house by a loud, obnoxious Little Red Riding Hood.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs, the Wolf and bobby-soxer Red chase each other around while Grandma is off working at Lockheed aircraft.

cartoons.title: Meatless Flyday
cartoons.desc: A spider tries to catch a fly during wartime 'Meatless Tuesday' rationing in a kitchen battlefield.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A hungry spider is trying to catch a fly for dinner. Both pull pranks on each other until the fly is finally caught.

cartoons.title: Porky Pig's Feat
cartoons.desc: Daffy and Porky can't pay their hotel bill and must outwit the manager to escape.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig and Daffy Duck owe an outrageous sum to the Broken Arms Hotel. The manager thwarts their efforts to escape without paying their bill.

cartoons.title: Puss 'N Booty
cartoons.desc: After a pet owner's canary disappears, she gets a new one — but the household cat has dinner plans.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Woman wonders why her little pet birds keep disappearing. Rudolph the cat knows, but other than burping feathers, he's not saying. But it looks like he's met his match when the woman orders another bird from the pet shop: a little yellow canary named "Petey".

cartoons.title: Scrap Happy Daffy
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck guards a wartime scrap metal pile from a Nazi goat trying to destroy it.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: During World War Two, Daffy Duck owns a junkyard which collects scrap metal to use in building weapons to continue the Allied fight against the Axis powers. Hitler reads about Daffy's scrap pile and about Daffy's stated intent to win the war with junk and, after throwing a fit and chewing a carpet like a mad dog, orders Daffy's scrap pile destroyed.

cartoons.title: Super Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny gains superpowers from special carrot formula in a Superman parody, flying to save the day.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny becomes a superhero who does battle with a rabbit-hating cowboy and his horse.

cartoons.title: The Aristo-Cat
cartoons.desc: A pampered house cat named Claude is terrified when mice Hubie and Bertie convince him he's a killer.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Meadows the butler quits after being tormented by the spoiled family cat, who finds he is unable to survive on his own, especially after meeting the mice Hubie and Bertie.

cartoons.title: The Unbearable Bear
cartoons.desc: Sniffles the mouse's chattiness foils both a burglar and a tipsy police officer trying to catch him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sniffles the mouse's non-stop talking foils both the burglar and a tipsy Officer Bear, who's trying to sneak past his rolling pin-toting, sleepwalking wife.

cartoons.title: The Wise Quacking Duck
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck plays pranks on meek Mr. Meek, who desperately wants to cook the duck for dinner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An exceedingly mild-mannered man is sent out to kill a duck for dinner by his wife. Unfortunately for him, he picks Daffy Duck as his victim. The two face off and do battle for the remainder of the cartoon.

cartoons.title: To Duck...Or Not to Duck
cartoons.desc: After Elmer Fudd tries to hunt Daffy, the duck challenges him to a boxing match refereed by other ducks.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy challenges duckhunter Elmer to a boxing match, rigged in his favor with the collusion of the duck referee. In the stands, Elmer's dog Larrimore suspects that something funny is going on, but he's drowned out by Daffy's all-duck cheering section.

cartoons.title: Tortoise Wins by a Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny disguises himself as a turtle for a rematch with Cecil, but the plan backfires.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs challenges Cecil Turtle to race, only this time he's wearing an aerodynamic suit like Cecil's. Unfortunately, the gambling ring has bet everything on the rabbit, and Bugs now looks like a tortoise.

cartoons.title: Wackiki Wabbit
cartoons.desc: Two starving castaways land on Bugs Bunny's tropical island and try to cook him for dinner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: On a tropical island, a pair of castaways look to Bugs as a source of food.

cartoons.title: What's Cookin' Doc?
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny crashes the Academy Awards ceremony, convinced he deserves the Best Actor Oscar.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: At the Academy Awards ceremony, Bugs Bunny tries to convince the audience that he deserves the Oscar. Opens with live action scenes of Hollywood.

cartoons.title: Yankee Doodle Daffy
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck is a persistent talent agent trying to get Porky Pig to watch his child client perform.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy is an agent representing Sleepy Lagoon, trying to sell him to talent scout Porky. Daffy spends a great deal of time and energy explaining and demonstrating what the kid can do, while the kid sits on a couch licking a giant sucker.

cartoons.title: Birdy and the Beast
cartoons.desc: Tweety outsmarts a hungry cat in an early appearance, using his innocent charm as a deadly weapon.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tweety is set upon by a fat, jowly cat, who winds up with, among other things, a dozen eggs and a gallon of gasoline in his mouth instead of the little bird.

cartoons.title: Booby Hatched
cartoons.desc: A mother duck hatches her eggs in a snowstorm, but one duckling named Robespierre is a wild troublemaker.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A duck struggles mightily and finally hatches her eggs in the bitter cold. All but one, that is: poor little Robespierre. Mama doesn't notice him missing until after he has sprouted legs and run off in search of warmth.

cartoons.title: Brother Brat
cartoons.desc: When a mother goes to work in a wartime factory, Porky Pig babysits her monstrous, uncontrollable toddler.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A "Rosie the Riveter" type is in need of a baby-sitter for her awful child. The only person available is a clueless Porky Pig. His only instructions are to use a book of child psychology. After fruitless attempts to control the brat, his mother returns to show Porky how to use the book - as a paddle on his little behind.

cartoons.title: Buckaroo Bugs
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny plays the Masked Marauder, a notorious carrot thief terrorizing the West — his first Looney Tune.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Red Hot Ryder is sent to catch the Masked Marauder (Bugs Bunny) who is terrorizing a small Western town.

cartoons.title: Bugs Bunny and the Three Bears
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny wanders into the Three Bears' home, and Mama Bear becomes obsessively smitten with him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The bears tempt Goldilocks with carrot soup, the scent of which brings Bugs on the scene. Bugs romances Mama Bear, and she becomes infatuated with him.

cartoons.title: Draftee Daffy
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck desperately tries to dodge the draft board, but the little man from the draft keeps finding him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Despite an initial outburst of patriotism, Daffy is terrified to learn that "the little man from the Draft Board" has a letter for him, and tries his best to hide.

cartoons.title: Duck Soup to Nuts
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck outsmarts hunter Porky Pig after the shooting disturbs Daffy's pond friends.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig is out hunting duck, but Daffy shows him that he is no ordinary duck.

cartoons.title: From Hand to Mouse
cartoons.desc: A fast-talking mouse cons Leo the Lion into a partnership that works entirely in the mouse's favor.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: "Spare me!" pleads the mouse to the dumb lion who just caught him, "And some day I'll save your life." Once out of the lion's clutches, though, the mouse taunts, "Sucker!"

cartoons.title: Hare Force
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny swings into an old lady's house during a snowstorm, and her jealous dog Willoughby fights to kick him out.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Granny lets Bugs Bunny come in from the cold, but her dog Sylvester will have none of it.

cartoons.title: Hare Ribbin
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny outwits a Russian-accented dog who wants to hunt him near a frozen river.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs is chased into a lake by a Russian Poodle who speaks with a thick Russian accent; the rest of the story unfolds under water.

cartoons.title: Herr Meets Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny tunnels into Nazi Germany and faces off against Hermann Göring in a wartime comedy.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs disguises himself as Hitler, Stalin and Brunhilde when he confronts Nazi Hermann Goering in the Black Forest.

cartoons.title: I Got Plenty of Mutton
cartoons.desc: A starving wolf deprived of meat by wartime rationing disguises himself as a female sheep to infiltrate a flock.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A wolf, deprived of meat by war rationing and starving, sees an article in the newspaper about a sheepdog leaving his flock to join the army and thinks it will be easy pickings. However, if he had read the rest of the article, he would know that the flock is now guarded by the ram Killer Diller, a most formidable foe.

cartoons.title: Lost and Foundling
cartoons.desc: Sniffles hatches an egg that turns out to be a baby hawk, and must protect the chick from its angry mother.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A mouse adopts a hawk.

cartoons.title: Mouse Menace
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig tries everything to catch a tiny but indestructible mouse invading his house.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky has a particularly menacing mouse in his house; after his traps, and an increasingly nasty set of cats all fail, Porky builds a robot cat. This cat proves to be a much bigger challenge for the mouse, who ultimately builds a robot mouse packed with explosives.

cartoons.title: Odor-Able Kitty
cartoons.desc: The amorous French skunk Pepé Le Pew relentlessly pursues a reluctant cat who's been accidentally painted with a white stripe.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A cat, tired of being abused by everyone in his neighborhood, disguises himself as a skunk and inadvertently attracts the romantic advances of a real skunk.

cartoons.title: Plane Daffy
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck is a military messenger who must resist a seductive female Nazi spy to deliver his secret message.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck is a message courier bird delivering a military secret that a femme fatale Nazi spy is determined to get.

cartoons.title: Russian Rhapsody
cartoons.desc: Hitler pilots a bomber toward Russia but is thwarted by a squadron of Kremlin gremlins.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: As Adolf Hitler personally flies a bomber on a mission to the Soviet Union, the gremlins from the Kremlin set about to stop him.

cartoons.title: Snow Time for Comedy
cartoons.desc: The Two Curious Puppies chase a bone through a winter landscape, slipping on ice and crashing into snowdrifts.

cartoons.title: Stage Door Cartoon
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd chases Bugs Bunny into a theater, where Bugs tricks him into humiliating stage performances.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: That wascawwy wabbit is chased into a theatre by Elmer Fudd, and ends up having to perform to save himself, as well as convince Elmer to act himself. The vaudeville industry was never this wacky!

cartoons.title: Swooner Crooner
cartoons.desc: Porky's egg farm goes haywire when his hens swoon over a Sinatra-like rooster crooner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig's egg farm faces production problems when a crooning rooster distracts the hens from their jobs.

cartoons.title: The Old Grey Hare
cartoons.desc: An elderly Elmer Fudd chases an equally aged Bugs Bunny in a flash-forward to the year 2000.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Failed hunter Elmer Fudd laments that he's never able to catch the rabbit (Bugs Bunny); just then a bolt of lightning strikes, and the voice of God takes him through a flash-forward to the year 2000. Elmer and Bugs, now both elderly, look back to when they first met as babies.

cartoons.title: The Stupid Cupid
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd plays Cupid, shooting love arrows at animals and causing mismatched romances across the farm.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Cupid (Elmer Fudd) is on the prowl around the farm. With his ever-accurate arrows, he spreads love to sometimes unwilling recipients. But when he sets his sights on Daffy, the duck wants no part of it. When Elm...erm...Cupid fires the largest arrow at his disposal at the hapless duck, Daffy falls for the nearest hen...who happens to be the main squeeze of the cock of the walk...

cartoons.title: The Unruly Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny pranks Elmer Fudd while Elmer tries to survey for a new railroad through Bugs' home.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When Elmer Fudd disturbs Bugs with his railroad surveying, Bugs fights back.

cartoons.title: The Weakly Reporter
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag spoof of homefront sacrifices during World War II, parodying wartime newsreels.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A newsreel spoof with WWII homefront gags, including rationing, air raid drills and women filling in men's jobs.

cartoons.title: Tick Tock Tuckered
cartoons.desc: Daffy and Porky must get to work on time after a night of insomnia — their boss will fire them if they're late again.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky and Daffy are workers at an aircraft company and are chronically late. Why? Because they have a great deal of trouble getting to sleep, between the noisy cats, the full moon shining insistently, and the sudden rain shower (and leak in the roof).

cartoons.title: Tom Turk And Daffy
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck befriends a turkey named Tom who must hide from Porky Pig on Thanksgiving.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's Thanksgiving, and Tom Turk is trying to avoid become the main attraction on Porky Pig's dinner table. Fellow bird Daffy Duck is willing to help him, until he realizes that he'll miss out on a delicious meal. Hilarity ensues as each tries to get the other caught by Porky.

cartoons.title: A Gruesome Twosome
cartoons.desc: Two rival cats compete for a female cat's affection by trying to catch Tweety Bird.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two cats (one a caricature of Jimmy Durante) battle violently for the affections of a pretty girl cat, who'll dispense her favors on the one who brings her a little bird. Unfortunately for the lovestruck felines, the bird in question is a vicious little thing named Tweety.

cartoons.title: A Tale of Two Mice
cartoons.desc: Mouse versions of Babbit and Catstello try to steal cheese from under a cat's watchful eye.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Babitt and Catstello return; their goal: steal the cheese the cat is guarding.

cartoons.title: Baby Bottleneck
cartoons.desc: The stork delivery system is overwhelmed, so Porky and Daffy run a baby factory to handle the overflow.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: As the baby boom commences, and with the delivery service overworked, Porky Pig and Daffy Duck are placed in charge of a baby preparation factory, where they help the stork keep up.

cartoons.title: Baseball Bugs
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny single-handedly takes on an entire baseball team, playing every position to win the game.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny single handedly takes on the “Gas-House Gorillas,” a baseball team of hulking, cigar-chomping bullies.

cartoons.title: Behind the Meat-Ball
cartoons.desc: A dog named Fido hates meatless meals, and when a steak is dropped by a delivery man, chaos ensues.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A dog, starved for meat, goes to different lengths to get a steak back from a little dog that keeps out-smarting him.

cartoons.title: Book Revue
cartoons.desc: Books come alive at midnight, with Daffy Duck bursting out of a comic book for a wild musical number.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A secluded bookstore comes to life in madcap, pop culture reference-heavy fashion.

cartoons.title: Fresh Airedale
cartoons.desc: A conniving dog named Shep takes credit for heroic acts while framing an innocent Scottish terrier.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Shep the dog is seen by his master as loyal and loving, but the cat knows he is really a self-centered, conniving weasel who lets burglars in the house and takes credit for the good deeds of others.

cartoons.title: Hair-Raising Hare
cartoons.desc: A mad scientist lures Bugs Bunny into his castle as dinner for his enormous orange monster, Gossamer.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A sneaker-wearing, hairy monster chases Bugs through a castle belonging to an evil scientist.

cartoons.title: Hare Conditioned
cartoons.desc: A department store manager tries to stuff Bugs Bunny as a display piece, leading to a chase through the store.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny is working in the display window of a department store when the manager tries to move him to the taxidermy department and have him stuffed.

cartoons.title: Hare Remover
cartoons.desc: Mad scientist Elmer Fudd tests his Jekyll-and-Hyde potion, but Bugs Bunny keeps interfering with the experiment.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd is an amateur scientist who wants to turn a normal character into a devilish fiend; he's all out of experimental animals. Enter Bugs Bunny.

cartoons.title: Hare Tonic
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny tricks Elmer Fudd into believing his house is infected with 'rabbititis' disease.

cartoons.title: Hare Trigger
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny's first encounter with Yosemite Sam, who tries to rob a train.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Yosemite Sam is trying to rob the train that Bugs Bunny is riding on, and the two face off in several different ways.

cartoons.title: Holiday for Shoestrings
cartoons.desc: A parody of The Elves and the Shoemaker where tiny elves make shoes overnight for Elmer Fudd's shop.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: To the tune of The Nutcracker, a number of elves do all the work in a shoe shop.

cartoons.title: Hush My Mouse
cartoons.desc: When a tavern customer orders mouse knuckles, the owner sends a mouse to catch Sniffles — who won't stop talking.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Take-off on the "Duffy's Tavern" radio program, with tough-guy Eddie G. Robincat demanding a meal of mouse knuckles, "of which we ain't got none," waiter Filligan informs his absentee boss on the phone. To fill the plate, Filligan then tries to catch the blabbermouth mouse, Sniffles.

cartoons.title: Life With Feathers
cartoons.desc: A lovesick bird thrown out by his mate tries to get Sylvester to eat him — Sylvester's screen debut.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A spurned love bird tries to get Sylvester to put him out of his misery.

cartoons.title: Nasty Quacks
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck is a pet owned by a girl named Agnes, but her father wants to get rid of the annoying duck.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A doting father gives a cute little duckling to his little daughter. That duckling grows up to become Daffy Duck, who soon develops quite a night life, which he loudly explains at breakfast, in the process of eating everything in sight. When the exasperated father's attempts at violently removing Daffy fail, he tries one final measure to drive Daffy away...

cartoons.title: Peck Up Your Troubles
cartoons.desc: Sylvester tries to catch a woodpecker who just moved into a nearby tree, but the bird fights back hard.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester is determined to get a woodpecker that just moved in, high in a tree. He climbs, but the bird greases the tree; he starts to cut it down, but a mean dog stops him (this becomes a running gag). Several other attempts follow; at one point, he puts his paw into the bird's home, and the bird puts a tomato there; Sylvester squishes it, and the bird dresses as an angel to torment him, but Sylvester sees through the disguise. Finally, Sylvester tries to blow up the tree; the dog again intervenes. Sylvester gets the dynamite off the tree and puts out the fuses, but the bird has lit them again, and now Sylvester really becomes an angel.

cartoons.title: Quentin Quail
cartoons.desc: A quail goes through dangerous trials to get a worm for his family's dinner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Take-off on Fanny Brice's "Baby Snooks" radio program. An exasperated Mr. Quail tries to catch a worm for his whining daughter, Baby Toots, and gets the worst from a tough crow who has designs on the worm himself.

cartoons.title: The Bashful Buzzard
cartoons.desc: Beaky Buzzard is kicked out of the nest by his mother and told to catch something to eat.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Beaky Buzzard, the shyest, dopiest young buzzard in his family, is sent out to catch something to eat.

cartoons.title: Trap Happy Porky
cartoons.desc: Mice Hubie and Bertie invade Porky's house, and he calls in a cat exterminator — who's worse than the mice.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky can't sleep because mice demolish his plates. A cat offers help and gets the mice out, but invites some friends so Porky still can't sleep.

cartoons.title: Wagon Heels
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig encounters the wild Injun Joe and dim Sloppy Moe during the 1849 Gold Rush.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky leads a wagon train into "Injun Joe Territory," and finally comes up against the fearsome Superchief. But Sloppy Moe, a survivor of a previous Injun Joe attack, knows something about him he won't tell... until the very end.

cartoons.title: A Hare Grows in Manhattan
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny tells a reporter about growing up on the streets of Manhattan and fighting a gang of dogs.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny relates his early life in the Manhattan tenements and spotlights his encounter with a gang of canine toughs.

cartoons.title: A Pest in the House
cartoons.desc: Bellhop Daffy Duck must keep hotel guests quiet for an exhausted man, but keeps waking him up instead.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A very tired businessman needs some sleep and checks into a hotel run by Elmer Fudd, where Daffy Duck is the bellhop.

cartoons.title: Acrobatty Bunny
cartoons.desc: A circus sets up right over Bugs Bunny's hole, and he must deal with Nero the Lion performing above him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When the circus arrives they put the lion's cage right over Bugs' rabbit hole.

cartoons.title: Along Came Daffy
cartoons.desc: Yosemite Sam and his twin are starving in a snowbound cabin when Daffy Duck arrives as a door-to-door salesman.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Snowbound in a remote cabin, two starving men begin visualizing each other as food. When salesman Daffy Duck calls at their door, it doesn't take long before the men set their minds on having Daffy as their dinner.

cartoons.title: Bacall to Arms
cartoons.desc: A movie theater shows a newsreel and feature film with celebrity caricatures coming to life — a Bacall parody.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Movie patrons watch and interact with a variety of short subjects and a spoof of the film "To Have and Have Not."

cartoons.title: Birth of a Notion
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck moves in with a dog for the winter, but a mad scientist next door has other plans.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck, hoping to avoid flying south by finding a sucker who will let him stay, ends up at the house of a mad scientist and his dog, Leopold.

cartoons.title: Crowing Pains
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn, Barnyard Dawg, and Sylvester all try to manipulate Henery Hawk for their own purposes.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Henery Hawk hides in an egg to catch his first chicken, while Foghorn Leghorn tells him that Sylvester is the real chicken and the farm dog joins in the fun.

cartoons.title: Daffy Doodles
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck goes on a rampage painting mustaches on every face and advertisement in the city, with Officer Porky Pig hot on his trail.

cartoons.title: Easter Yeggs
cartoons.desc: The depressed Easter Bunny tricks Bugs into taking over egg delivery, where Elmer Fudd is waiting.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs gets roped into delivering the Easter Rabbit's eggs for him.

cartoons.title: Fair and Worm-er
cartoons.desc: A worm, a crow, a cat, and a dog form an escalating food chain, each chasing the one below.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: One long chase: worm chases apple; bird chases worm; cat chases bird; dog chases cat; dogcatcher chases dog; dogcatcher's wife chases dogcatcher; mouse chases dogcatcher's wife. With occasional interruptions by a skunk.

cartoons.title: Goofy Gophers
cartoons.desc: Mac and Tosh, the impeccably polite gopher duo, debut as they try to steal vegetables from a garden.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two polite twin gophers raid a vegetable patch guarded by a rather smug dog, whose various unsuccessful schemes to nullify the crafty and modest gophers involve a female gopher disguise, a hand grenade, and a carrot stuffed with TNT.

cartoons.title: Hobo Bobo
cartoons.desc: A baby Indian elephant named Bobo runs away to America to join a circus and play baseball.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Little Bobo the Elephant decides to leave a jungle, where he is assigned to the thankless task of moving logs with his trunk, for a glamorous life in a circus in America. On the advice of a minah bird, Bobo paints himself pink to gain access to a ship bound for the U.S., because nobody on the ship will admit to seeing a pink elephant much less act to remove the presumed hallucination. After Bobo arrives in America, a steet-cleaner washes his pink color away, and people are now willing to acknowledge seeing the little elephant. Bobo is arrested by the police and chained for trial by judge, and the judge sentences him to life - in a circus, where he is bat "boy" for the big top baseball team, and laments that he's carrying logs (i.e. bats) yet again!

cartoons.title: Hollywood Canine Canteen
cartoons.desc: Dog-styled caricatures of Hollywood celebrities perform in a star-studded canteen show.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A group of celebrity dogs, led by an 'Edward G. Robinson' look-alike and including Jimmy Durante, decide that celebrity dogs need a nightclub of their own.

cartoons.title: Hollywood Daffy
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck tries to sneak into the Warner Bros. studio to meet movie stars but keeps getting thrown out.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy sneaks onto the Warmer Brothers lot, eventually posing as a tour guide. Daffy spoofs a number of contemporary stars, and others appear as "themselves". He also has a number of run-ins with a studio cop.

cartoons.title: Kitty Kornered
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig tries to throw his four cats out for the night, but they fight back with alien costumes.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky puts his cats out in the snow, but then they put him out and have a party. Expelling them again, Porky goes to bed, only to be terrorized by the felines' mock Martian invasion.

cartoons.title: Little Orphan Airedale
cartoons.desc: Charlie Dog escapes from the pound and convinces Porky Pig to adopt him through relentless pestering.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Charlie Dog, looking for a good home and some easy living, thinks he's found the perfect sap in Porky Pig. He tries to ingratiate himself with the pig, all the way avoiding Porky's attempts to get rid of the dead-beat dog.

cartoons.title: Of Thee I Sting
cartoons.desc: Mosquitos launch coordinated attacks on a hapless man trying to enjoy his screened porch.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A mosquito army trains for, then goes on, an attack mission.

cartoons.title: One Meat Brawl
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig and the Barnyard Dawg encounter Grover Groundhog, who keeps popping out of his hole.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: On Groundhog Day, Porky Pig goes hunting groundhogs and takes his dopey dog, Mandrake. They soon encounter Grover Groundhog, who is none too thrilled to be the objective of a hunter on his big day.

cartoons.title: Rabbit Transit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny races Cecil Turtle again, but Cecil has a rocket-powered shell this time.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: This time Bugs' race with Cecil Turtle features a rocket-powered tortoise shell.

cartoons.title: Racketeer Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny plays pranks on gangsters Rocky and Hugo who hide out in his rabbit hole.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Hugo and Rocky (caricatures of Edward G. Robinson and Peter Lorre) make it home to their hideout only to find Bugs already settled down there for the night.

cartoons.title: Rhapsody Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny performs Liszt's 'Hungarian Rhapsody No. 2' on piano while battling a pesky mouse.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When Bugs Bunny attempts to perform Liszt's Second Hungarian Rhapsody, he is troubled by a mouse.

cartoons.title: Roughly Squeaking
cartoons.desc: Hubie and Bertie shave Claude Cat and convince him he's actually a lion.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Scheming mice Hubie and Bertie convince a cat that he is, in fact, a lion.

cartoons.title: Scent-imental Over You
cartoons.desc: A hairless chihuahua borrows a skunk skin for warmth, attracting the unwanted affections of Pepé Le Pew.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Striving to be like all the high-class dogs in their fine coats, a little hairless pooch borrows a black and white fur coat of her owner, not realizing it makes her appear to be a skunk. Once she has it on, she finds everyone fleeing from her - everyone, that is, except for the amorous Pepé Le Pew.

cartoons.title: Slick Hare
cartoons.desc: Waiter Elmer Fudd must serve rabbit to Humphrey Bogart at the Mocrumbo nightclub — so he hunts Bugs.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Humphrey Bogart visits the Mocrumbo Restaurant. He orders fried rabbit and Elmer Fudd has twenty minutes to serve it.

cartoons.title: The Big Snooze
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd quits his cartoon and falls asleep — so Bugs enters his dream to cause nightmares.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd walks out of a typical Bugs cartoon, so Bugs gets back at him by disturbing Elmer's sleep using "nightmare paint."

cartoons.title: The Eager Beaver
cartoons.desc: An enthusiastic but inept beaver tries to chop down a massive tree on a mountain.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Anxious to get to work with the big guys damming the river before the flood hits, a little beaver keeps getting in the way of their work. Finally, the foreman sends him off to chop down that big tree "way over there." Meanwhile, the flood rushes closer and closer.

cartoons.title: The Gay Anties
cartoons.desc: A colony of ants invade a couple's 1890s park picnic, stealing their food in an elaborate heist.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The 1890s; a picnic in the park. A man is pitching woo to his girl, while behind them a steady stream of ants is methodically devouring and carting off their food. Some other interludes include four ants eating Russian rye bread, then breaking for a Russian dance; a torch singer that sends everyone scurrying for some kind of ear plugs; a chef ant that coordinates the creation of three sandwiches that the humans eat.

cartoons.title: The Great Piggy Bank Robbery
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck imagines himself as detective Duck Twacy, battling a rogues' gallery of bizarre villains.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While reading his favorite comic book, Daffy accidentally knocks himself unconscious and dreams he's Duck Twacy, famous detective, trying to solve the case of the missing piggy banks. Taking a streetcar (conducted by Porky Pig, in a non-speaking cameo role) to the gangsters' hideout, he meets up with such grotesque criminals as Pickle Puss, Eighty-Eight Teeth and Neon Noodle.

cartoons.title: The Mouse-Merized Cat
cartoons.desc: Mouse duo Babbit and Catstello hypnotize each other to work up the courage to face a household cat.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Babbit hypnotizies Catsello, despite his efforts to resist, into believing he's Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra, and Jimmy Durante, then a chicken, and finally a dog, who he sics on the cat. The cat hypnotizes him back. Finally, Catstello hypnotizes both of them into cowboy and horse, leaving him alone to enjoy the deli they live in.

cartoons.title: Tweety Pie
cartoons.desc: Sylvester the cat devises another elaborate scheme to catch Tweety Bird, but the little canary stays one step ahead.

cartoons.title: Walky Talky Hawky
cartoons.desc: Tiny Henery Hawk is determined to catch a chicken, crossing paths with Foghorn Leghorn for the first time.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Young Henery Hawk's father regretfully admits their family's shame: they hunt and eat chickens. Henery set off to find one, and comes across Foghorn Leghorn, where the loudmouth rooster is engaged in his favorite pastime, playing tricks on grumpy Barnyard Dog.

cartoons.title: You Were Never Ducky
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck disguises himself as a rooster at the National Poultry Show to win the bigger prize money.

cartoons.title: A Hick, a Slick and a Chick
cartoons.desc: Country boy Elmo and city slicker Blackie compete for the affections of beautiful Daisy Lou.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A mouse named Elmo, who's a bit of a yokel, goes to beautiful Daisy Lou to woo her. However, he finds her with the slick Blackie.

cartoons.title: A Horse Fly Fleas
cartoons.desc: A flea and his horse fly companion search for the perfect dog host, settling on a stubborn bulldog.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A flea befriends a horsefly, who has hooves like those of a horse, and rides the horsefly into the hair of a dog. The flea chops down strands of the dog's hair to use as "logs" with which to build a cabin, unaware that the dog's coat is the sacred territory of a tribe of Indian fleas, who declare war on the interlopers. The Indians capture and are about to burn the flea and the horsefly when the dog jolts in pain from the fire. The flea and the horsefly free themselves and flee the Indians through the hairs on the dog's carcass.

cartoons.title: Awful Orphan
cartoons.desc: Charlie Dog shows up in Porky's hotel room via a pet shop truck and refuses to leave.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig's quiet life in his high-rise apartment building is rudely disrupted when an obnoxious mutt sneaks in and refuses to leave.

cartoons.title: Back Alley Oproar
cartoons.desc: Sylvester sings all night in the alley, driving Elmer Fudd crazy with insomnia.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester sings opera and popular tunes while standing on a back alley fence; Elmer, who wants to sleep, tries to thwart him.

cartoons.title: Bear Feat
cartoons.desc: Papa Bear forces his reluctant family into practicing a circus act with painful slapstick results.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The three bears try to train to become vaudeville stars, but things do not go well for Pa Bear.

cartoons.title: Buccaneer Bunny
cartoons.desc: Pirate Yosemite Sam tries to bury treasure in Bugs Bunny's rabbit hole, sparking a swashbuckling battle.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Yosemite Sam as a pirate makes the mistake of trying to bury his treasure chest in Bugs' hole, and pays with the loss of his ship.

cartoons.title: Bugs Bunny Rides Again
cartoons.desc: Bugs and Yosemite Sam face off in a series of Western-style confrontations in a frontier town.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In the Western town of Rising Gorge, Bugs faces off against Yosemite Sam, "the roughest, toughest, he-man stuffest hombre who's ever crossed the Rio Grande."

cartoons.title: Catch as Cats Can
cartoons.desc: A pipe-puffing parrot tells Sylvester he needs vitamins, tricking the cat into eating dangerous things.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An emaciated canary, singing like Frank Sinatra, is getting on the nerves of a pipe-puffing parrot, who speaks like Bing Crosby. The parrot spots Sylvester, foraging through the trash. Telling the cat he needs more vitamins (which the canary has been swallowing in bulk), he lures the cat inside to snare the canary. The straightforward approach fails (the canary bops him in the nose). He carves a female canary from soap, lures Frankie there; the birds slide down a greased counter, into the sink, and down the drain, but only the soap bird goes through the pipe and down Sylvester's throat. A trail of birdseed into the garage seems to work, but Frankie jacks Sylvester's mouth open. Sylvester laces the vitamins with buckshot; like all cartoon magnets, his attracts everything metal in sight except his prey.

cartoons.title: Daffy Duck Hunt
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck evades Porky Pig and the Barnyard Dawg during hunting season with his usual manic energy.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig goes on a hunting expedition, accompanied by his dog, and they bring home a live Daffy Duck, to put into a freezer until cooking time.

cartoons.title: Doggone Cats
cartoons.desc: Sylvester and an orange cat friend torment a bulldog named Wellington, who has been ordered by his mistress to deliver a package and must not let go of it under any circumstances.

cartoons.title: Gorilla My Dreams
cartoons.desc: A mother gorilla adopts Bugs Bunny as her baby, much to her husband's displeasure.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny is sailing the South Seas when a gorilla mother, desperate for a child, hijacks his barrel and presents Bugs to her husband. Bugs decides to play along, but quickly discovers his new "father" plays a bit rough.

cartoons.title: Hare Do
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd chases Bugs Bunny through various movie theaters and stage shows across town.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd chases Bugs Bunny all the way from the woods to a local movie theater, where cinema-related hijinks ensue.

cartoons.title: Hare Splitter
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny and his neighbor compete for the affections of the pretty girl rabbit next door.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When Bugs arrives for his date with Daisy Lou and finds her out shopping, he puts on her clothes to fool his rival Casbah.

cartoons.title: Haredevil Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is launched to the moon where he encounters Marvin the Martian planning to blow up Earth.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs is the test rabbit shot to the moon. There, he meets Commander X-2, who is intent on destroying the Earth with his Aludium Q-36 Explosive Space Modulator.

cartoons.title: Hop, Look and Listen
cartoons.desc: Baby kangaroo Hippety Hopper escapes the zoo and Sylvester mistakes him for a giant mouse.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A baby kangaroo hops out of his zoo cage and roams into the surrounding city. The kangaroo stops at Sylvester Cat's home while Sylvester is hunting for mice with a fishing rod. When Sylvester "reels in" the kangaroo, he thinks he has caught a giant mouse and makes a humiliatingly unsuccessful attempt to catch him.

cartoons.title: Hot Cross Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is 'Experimental Rabbit #46' in a hospital lab where a scientist wants to swap his brain.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: At Eureka Experimental Hospital, a doctor plans to switch the characteristics of a chicken into the brain and a rabbit, into each other. Bugs Bunny was registered as the experimental rabbit, Number 46.

cartoons.title: House Hunting Mice
cartoons.desc: Hubie and Bertie explore the automated 'Super House of Tomorrow' with its push-button gadgets.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Mice Hubie and Bertie wander into an automated house of tomorrow.

cartoons.title: Mississippi Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny accidentally ends up on a Mississippi riverboat and battles riverboat gambler Colonel Shuffle.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After getting mixed in with a bale of cotton, Bugs ends up on a Mississippi riverboat, where he meets up with the notorious gambler Col. Shuffle.

cartoons.title: Mouse Wreckers
cartoons.desc: Hubie and Bertie use psychological warfare to drive the paranoid Claude Cat out of their dream house.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Mice Hubie and Bertie drive Claude the cat insane through an escalating series of head games.

cartoons.title: Odor of the Day
cartoons.desc: The amorous French skunk Pepé Le Pew relentlessly pursues a reluctant cat who's been accidentally painted with a white stripe.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: On a cold winter's day, a stray dog is looking for shelter, then finds and sneaks into a cabin with an open fireplace and a cozy bed. But he has a stinky rival for occupancy of the cabin - a skunk.

cartoons.title: Porky Chops
cartoons.desc: A hipster squirrel living in the North Woods annoys Porky Pig, who's trying to chop down his tree.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Lumber jack Porky Pig intrudes upon the peace of a hipster squirrel vacationing in the Northwoods by trying to chop down the squirrel's tree. The squirrel retaliates by enclosing the base of his tree with steel so that Porky's axes cannot penetrate. The ensuing conflict between Porky and the squirrel awakens an angry bear.

cartoons.title: The Foghorn Leghorn
cartoons.desc: Henery Hawk follows his father to the henhouse but gets caught up between Foghorn and the Barnyard Dawg.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Little Henery the Chicken Hawk wants to prove he's big enough to hunt chickens, but he doesn't know what a chicken is. He labels Foghorn Leghorn a loud-mouthed shnook and dismisses him, prompting Foggy to indignantly try to prove he's a chicken and therefore fit to be Henery's prey.

cartoons.title: The Foxy Duckling
cartoons.desc: An insomniac fox tries to catch a duck, hoping to stuff his pillow with feathers for a good night's sleep.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An insomniac fox residing in a forest needs duck down for his pillow in order to be comfortable enough in his bed to fall asleep, and to this purpose he pursues a wily yellow duck. The fox uses a decoy and duck call, and is blasted by hunters' rifles. He builds a series of wooden extensions from a tree branch in an effort to reach the airborne duck, but the duck drops a feather onto the fox's head, and the extensions beneath the fox collapse, with the fox plunging mortally to ground. His spirit, while ascending to Heaven, encounters and chases the duck.

cartoons.title: The Pest That Came To Dinner
cartoons.desc: Pierre, the world's biggest termite, invades Porky Pig's wooden house and eats it from the inside out.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig soon discovers that a termite is responsible for his belongings crumbling to dust. When he can't exterminate the termite himself, he goes to a shyster who offers him a series of unsuccessful methods to remove the termite.

cartoons.title: The Rattled Rooster
cartoons.desc: A frustrated rooster tries to catch a worm before dawn, but the clever worm fights back.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A rooster is unable to get worms; the other chickens either get there first or trick him out of the worms. But there's one worm nobody else competes for, because it's a trickster.

cartoons.title: The Shell-Shocked Egg
cartoons.desc: A half-hatched baby turtle named Clem searches for a warm, safe place to finish coming out of his shell.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A mother turtle buries her eggs in the sand. While she is away, one of the egg partly hatches and begins roaming the countryside trying to finish hatching.

cartoons.title: The Stupor Salesman
cartoons.desc: Salesman Daffy Duck tries to sell products to criminal Slug McSlug, who just wants to be left alone.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Slug McSlug, a notorious bank robber, is chased by police after his latest heist. He reaches his country hideout, where he is promptly visited by an uninvited Daffy Duck, who is a door-to-door vendor of a variety of items.

cartoons.title: Two Gophers from Texas
cartoons.desc: A dog reads a book on how to catch the Goofy Gophers, but Mac and Tosh outsmart every method.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A theatrical dog decides to answer the call of the wild and hunt for his food. He targets two polite twin gophers as his first conquest and tries to kill them with a falling-rock trap hooked to a radish patch, then plots to attract them into range of his clutches by dressing himself like a baby, then by playing music. The gophers foil all of these schemes and trap the dog in his own piano as they play the keys, which are linked to hammers whacking the dog's rear.

cartoons.title: What Makes Daffy Duck?
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck must outwit both Elmer Fudd and a fox during duck hunting season.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's duck season, so Daffy plays hunter Elmer and a hungry fox off against each other.

cartoons.title: What's Brewin' Bruin
cartoons.desc: The Three Bears prepare to hibernate for winter, but Junyer Bear keeps disrupting Papa's plans.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Pa Bear's attempts to hibernate are constantly frustrated by Junyer's snoring, Ma repeatedly opening the window, a persistent drip from the ceiling and finally, the voices of spring.

cartoons.title: A Ham in a Role
cartoons.desc: A theatrical dog tired of cartoon work wants to perform Shakespeare, but the Goofy Gophers have other plans.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A dog decides to quit the slapstick comedy of cartoons and go to his country home to concentrate on Shakespeare, but two troublesome yet polite gophers foil his grand plans.

cartoons.title: Big House Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny tunnels into a prison and is mistaken for an escaped convict by guard Yosemite Sam.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While attempting to evade a group of hunters, Bugs Bunny jumps into a rabbit hole and inadvertently tunnels into Sing Song prison, where the malevolent prison guard, Sam Schultz, assumes he's an inmate.

cartoons.title: Bone, Sweet Bone
cartoons.desc: A silent dog steals a dinosaur bone from a museum and goes on a wild chase to keep it.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An archaeologist at a museum scolds his small, silent dog, Shep, for supposedly removing a bone belonging to a dinosaur skeleton and orders Shep to bring the bone back, but Shep finds that the place where he buried his most recent bone has been dug up and a bulldog is walking away with the bone in his mouth. Shep chases the bulldog with intent of retrieving the bone, and so begins a battle of wits between Shep and the bulldog.

cartoons.title: Bye, Bye Bluebeard
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig eats enormous amounts of food while listening to an exercise radio show, unaware a killer is lurking.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky has a series of altercations with a mischievous mouse and a vicious serial killer.

cartoons.title: Curtain Razor
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig is a talent agent auditioning increasingly desperate and dangerous performers.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky, a talent scout for "Goode and Korney Talent Agency," auditions various acts. A final gag has a wolf performing this "stupendous act" where he wears a devil hat, cape and the like, drinks nitroglycerin, gasoline and other explosive stuff, then swallows a match. KABOOM! Porky thinks that the act is really good until the wolf's ghost comes in and says that there's a catch... "I can only do it once!"(Source: bcdb.com)

cartoons.title: Daffy Dilly
cartoons.desc: Struggling salesman Daffy Duck tries to make a dying tycoon laugh to claim a huge reward.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tired of selling gag novelties on the street, Daffy tries for the million-dollar reward offered by J.P. Cubish for the first person to make him laugh. But he first has to get past the rich man's haughty butler, and in the process subjects the servant to a Bogart-like grilling.

cartoons.title: Daffy Duck Slept Here
cartoons.desc: Porky must share his hotel room with the obnoxious Daffy Duck during a sold-out convention in town.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig has to share a hotel room with endlessly annoying roommate Daffy Duck.

cartoons.title: Dough for the Do-Do
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig travels to Wackyland to capture the last Dodo bird — a color remake of Porky in Wackyland.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig has an adventure in Wackyland while searching for the last Do-Do bird.

cartoons.title: Dough Ray Me-ow
cartoons.desc: A parrot helps his dim cat companion, but when the parrot reads the owner's will, the cat turns on him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Louie the Parrot finds a written will stating that his master bequeathes the family fortune not to him, but to his fellow household pet, a lunkheaded cat named Heathcliff, with the proviso that Louie is next in line to inherit the wealth if Heathcliff dies. So, Louie plots the untimely demise of Heathcliff.

cartoons.title: Fast and Furry-Ous
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote deploys another series of elaborate Acme contraptions in his endless pursuit of the Road Runner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: This was the debut for Wile E. Coyote and the Road Runner. It was also their only cartoon made in the 1940s. It set the template for the series, in which Wile E. Coyote (here given the ersatz Latin name Carnivorous Vulgaris) tries to catch Roadrunner (Accelleratii Incredibus) through many traps, plans and products, although in this first cartoon not all of the products are yet made by the Acme Corporation.

cartoons.title: For Scent-Imental Reasons
cartoons.desc: The amorous French skunk Pepé Le Pew relentlessly pursues a reluctant cat who's been accidentally painted with a white stripe.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Pepé Le Pew invades a Parisian perfumery, where he sniffs the various scents. The shopkeeper runs in horror and recruits a female cat to run the skunk out of the shop. She tosses the cat inside, and a bottle of dye falls over, accidentally painting a white stripe down the cat's back. Pepé gives chase...

cartoons.title: Grey Hounded Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny winds up at a greyhound racetrack and falls in love with the mechanical rabbit.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs goes to the dog track, falls in love with the mechanical rabbit there, and has to outsmart the dogs to get to her.

cartoons.title: High Diving Hare
cartoons.desc: Yosemite Sam tries to force Bugs Bunny onto a high-diving platform, but keeps ending up in the water himself.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Yosemite Sam tries to force Bugs Bunny to do a high-diving act when the regular act cancels.

cartoons.title: Hippety Hopper
cartoons.desc: Baby kangaroo Hippety Hopper saves a mouse from despair, and Sylvester mistakes him for a giant mouse.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A mouse is saved from committing suicide by a baby kangaroo, Hippety Hopper, who he frees from a crate on the docks. His new friend, who looks like a "king-sized mouse," then helps him get revenge on Sylvester the cat.

cartoons.title: Home, Tweet Home
cartoons.desc: Sylvester the cat devises another elaborate scheme to catch Tweety Bird, but the little canary stays one step ahead.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tweety Bird is washing in a bird bath in a city park when Sylvester Cat interrupts him. Sylvester chases Tweety, and Tweety takes refuge near a feisty nanny and her toddler. Sylvester dresses as the toddler to try to grab Tweety but is stopped and spanked. Tweety flies to a building ledge, and Sylvester unsuccessfully uses chewing gum to try to reach him. Next, Sylvester angers a bulldog, who chases him away.

cartoons.title: Hurdy-Gurdy Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny encounters a hurdy-gurdy man and his monkey in a musical street performance gone wrong.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Organ grinder operator Bugs must get rid of his chimp when the ape steals the take from him. The replacement is a gorilla.

cartoons.title: Kit for Cat
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd adopts Sylvester and another kitten, who frame each other for household destruction.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd takes in Sylvester Cat and an orange kitten during a cold winter night. He'd like to adopt both, but can only keep one. He decides to go to bed and make up his mind in the morning. Sylvester and the kitten both want to be the one who is adopted, so each tries framing the other for noisy misdeeds.

cartoons.title: Knights Must Fall
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny faces a jousting knight in a medieval tournament, outwitting the armored warrior.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs must joust with Sir Pantsalot of Drop Seat Manor when he tosses a partially eaten carrot into a suit of armor.

cartoons.title: Long-Haired Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny takes revenge on opera singer Giovanni Jones by impersonating a famous conductor.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny vs. a famous opera singer at the Hollywood Bowl.

cartoons.title: Mouse Mazurka
cartoons.desc: Sylvester chases a mouse through the Slobovian Mountains in a snowy Eastern European setting.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In the Slobovian mountains, Sylvester Cat and a mouse move to the tune of East European folk music as this chase cartoon is propelled to an explosive climax.

cartoons.title: My Bunny Lies Over the Sea
cartoons.desc: Bugs takes a wrong turn to Scotland and challenges a kilted Scotsman to a round of golf.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In Scotland, Bugs Bunny rescues a woman from a monster. The "woman" is a kilted Scotsman, and the "monster" is his bagpipe. The Scotsman then challenges Bugs to a game of golf.

cartoons.title: Often an Orphan
cartoons.desc: Charlie Dog manipulates Porky Pig into adopting him after his old owner abandons him by the roadside.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Abandoned in the country by his old master, Charlie Dog tries to force himself upon farmer Porky Pig, playing upon his sympathies with a histrionic rendition of the horrors of big-city life.

cartoons.title: Rabbit Punch
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny's loud booing at a boxing match angers the champion Crusher, leading to a ring showdown.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Heckling the Champ gets Bugs into the world championship fight as the challenger.
