cartoons.title: Rebel Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is offended by the tiny bounty on rabbits and goes on a nationwide rampage to prove rabbits are dangerous.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The signs indicate current bounty prices: $50 for a fox, $75 for a bear, only 2 cents for a rabbit. Bugs is insulted.

cartoons.title: Riff Raffy Daffy
cartoons.desc: Vagrant Daffy Duck is arrested by patrol officer Porky Pig for sleeping on park benches.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: No matter where vagabond Daffy Duck goes to sleep, policeman Porky Pig is there to toss him out. Finally, Porky kicks him out the city park entirely, and it starts snowing. Daffy decides to take shelter at the closed Macys department store. When Porky catches him, he's determined to be rid of Daffy once and for all.

cartoons.title: Scaredy Cat
cartoons.desc: Porky and Sylvester move into a creepy old house where murderous mice are systematically eliminating all cats — only Sylvester can see the danger while Porky remains blissfully unaware.

cartoons.title: Strife With Father
cartoons.desc: A baby bird's father tries to teach him to catch a worm, but the youngster keeps botching the lesson.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foundling Beaky Buzzard is adopted by a couple of polite, English sparrows named Monte and Gwendlyn. When Monte tries to teach lame-brained Beaky to catch a chicken, Beaky's ineptitude results in Monte being repeatedly struck with a mallet and caught in a grenade explosion.

cartoons.title: Swallow the Leader:
cartoons.desc: A cat tries to catch swallows but they're too fast and organized for him to nab even one.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Migrating swallows are making their annual spring return to San Juan Capistrano, and a hungry cat awaits them.

cartoons.title: The Bee-Deviled Bruin
cartoons.desc: Papa Bear of the Three Bears attempts to get honey from a beehive, with Junyer Bear making things worse.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's breakfast time, and Pa finds the honeypot empty. Literally risking life and limb, he has Junyer help him raid a nearby beehive. In the end, he finds he should have listened to Ma in the first place, rather than telling her to "Shaddap!"

cartoons.title: The Lion's Busy
cartoons.desc: A lion tries to maintain his dignity as King of the Jungle while smaller animals undermine him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In an African jungle, hungry Beaky Buzzard can't wait until Leo the Lion is decently deceased before trying to devour him. Leo takes a rocket to the Moon to try to escape Beaky, but finds Beaky already there waiting for him.

cartoons.title: The Up-Standing sitter
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck babysits a human toddler and a bulldog, and both make his evening a living nightmare.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck is working as a babysitter for the Acme Baby Sitting Agency. While he's sitting on a chicken egg, it hatches. The chick decides Daffy is a stranger and he should have nothing to do with him, so flees. Daffy has to catch it.

cartoons.title: The Windblown Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is conned into buying the straw and stick houses from the Three Little Pigs, then seeks revenge on the Wolf.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs buys the homes of the three little pigs and the wolf starts blowing them down. Of course you know "this means war."

cartoons.title: 8-Ball Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny helps a little penguin travel from Brooklyn all the way to the South Pole.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs helps a penguin return home.

cartoons.title: A Fractured Leghorn
cartoons.desc: A stray cat tries to catch Foghorn Leghorn's adopted baby chick, but the rooster fights back.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foghorn Leghorn and a cat fight over a worm. The cat wants the worm as bait for a fish, while Foghorn just wants the worm for a quick snack.

cartoons.title: All A-Bir-r-r-d
cartoons.desc: Tweety rides a train while Sylvester chases him from car to car, facing a bulldog conductor.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tweety Bird is on a train with Sylvester.

cartoons.title: Bad Ol' Putty Tat
cartoons.desc: Tweety uses clever tricks and disguises to evade Sylvester's persistent attempts to catch him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat starts to saw down Tweety Bird's house. Tweety flees into a badminton court, where he becomes the birdie in the game. Sylvester disguises himself as a player, and Tweety drops a TNT stick into Sylvester's mouth.

cartoons.title: Bowery Bugs
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny tells an old man the supposedly true story of Steve Brodie's famous Brooklyn Bridge jump.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After a man down on his luck comes looking for a rabbit's foot, Bugs Bunny embarks on a campaign of terror that eventually provokes him to jump off the Brooklyn Bridge.

cartoons.title: Bunker Hill Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny defends a fort against Yosemite Sam during the Revolutionary War.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sam Von Schamm The Hessian and Bugs Bunny fight it out in the little known American Revolutionary War Battle of Bagel Heights.

cartoons.title: Canary Row
cartoons.desc: Sylvester uses increasingly elaborate schemes to get into Granny's apartment building to catch Tweety.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat spots Tweety Bird in a San Francisco apartment and tries to gain access but cannot make it past Granny or the cat-hating desk clerk.

cartoons.title: Canned Feud
cartoons.desc: Sylvester is locked in the house when his owners leave on vacation, and a mouse has the only can opener.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat finds that his people have gone on vacation and left him alone in a locked house with a large stash of canned food in a cupboard. Sylvester needs a can opener, or he'll starve. And a pesky mouse has the only can opener in the house and torments Sylvester into trying more and more desperate measures to obtain it.

cartoons.title: Dog Collared
cartoons.desc: A dog on a leash tries desperately to reach a bone just beyond his chain's length.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: On Be Kind to Animals Week, Porky Pig decides to practice the principle and affectionately pets a large, slobbering dog. The dog takes an instant liking to Porky and follows the pig everywhere.

cartoons.title: Frigid Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs accidentally ends up at the South Pole and helps a playful little penguin escape a hungry Eskimo.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs rescues a penguin from an Inuit hunter at the South Pole and becomes obligated to it beyond his wildest dreams.

cartoons.title: Golden Yeggs
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck is forced to lay golden eggs for gangster Rocky after the real goose retires.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: On Porky Pig's farm, a goose lays a golden egg and says that Daffy Duck laid it. Daffy, now the most sought-after duck in the world, is quite willing to take the credit and resultant fame- until Rocky the gangster kidnaps Daffy and orders him at gunpoint to lay more.

cartoons.title: Hen House Henery
cartoons.desc: Henery Hawk tries to catch a chicken but Foghorn Leghorn keeps steering him toward the Barnyard Dawg instead.

cartoons.title: His Bitter Half
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck marries a bossy hen and immediately regrets it when she and her son take over his life.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck marries for money, but the bossy wife and her raucous, trouble-making little son soon have him wanting out.

cartoons.title: Homeless Hare
cartoons.desc: A construction crew demolishes Bugs Bunny's home to build a skyscraper, so Bugs wages war on the foreman.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A construction worker destroys Bugs' home with a steam shovel and refuses to repair the damage.

cartoons.title: It's Hummer Time
cartoons.desc: A cat tries to catch a hummingbird, but the tiny bird proves far too fast and clever.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A cat chases a hummingbird and repeatedly stumbles onto the property of a sleepy bulldog, who punishes the cat for each interruption of his slumber.

cartoons.title: Mutiny on the Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is shanghaied aboard pirate Yosemite Sam's ship and must escape the high seas.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Shanghai Sam needs a new crew for his ship. Bugs signs on but rebels at the captain's cruelty.

cartoons.title: Paying the Piper
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig is hired as the Pied Piper to rid a town of cats, but the displaced cats retaliate by disguising themselves as a giant mouse to terrorize Porky.

cartoons.title: Pop 'im Pop!
cartoons.desc: Sylvester's son encourages his father to catch a mouse, but the 'mouse' is really a baby kangaroo.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A circus comes to town featuring Gracie the Fighting Kangaroo and her youngster, Hippety Hopper.

cartoons.title: Rabbit Hood
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny raids the royal vegetable garden and tangles with the Sheriff of Nottingham in Sherwood Forest.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While trespassing in the royal gardens in search of carrots, Bugs runs afoul of the Sheriff of Nottingham, who tries to apprehend him for poaching. Of course Bugs sets out to endlessly turn the tables on the hapless sheriff.

cartoons.title: Rabbit of Seville
cartoons.desc: Bugs and Elmer perform an escalating barber routine on the opera stage set to Rossini's overture.

cartoons.title: Scent-imental Romeo
cartoons.desc: The amorous French skunk Pepé Le Pew relentlessly pursues a reluctant cat who's been accidentally painted with a white stripe.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A hungry cat disguises herself as a skunk to get in on feeding time at the zoo, but amorous Pepé thinks she's the real thing and pours on his Maurice Chevalier impression to win her over.

cartoons.title: The Ducksters
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck hosts a sadistic radio quiz show where Porky Pig is the hapless contestant facing violent penalties.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Quiz-show contestant Porky is the one who's supposed to be penalized each time he misses a question, but host Daffy is the one who winds up getting safes and boulders dropped on him and deluged by torrents of water.

cartoons.title: The Hypo-chondri-Cat
cartoons.desc: Mice Hubie and Bertie gaslight Claude Cat into believing he has every illness in the medical book.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Those crazy mice Hubie & Bertie are at it again with Claude. This time the mice see that Claude is seriously ill, so they give him an operation.

cartoons.title: The Leghorn Blows at Midnight
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn must stay awake all night to guard the henhouse, but a weasel keeps trying to sneak in.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foghorn Leghorn tricks a naive young chicken hawk into believing the barnyard dog is a pheasant.

cartoons.title: Two's A Crowd
cartoons.desc: A cat and a dog must share the same house, leading to an escalating war over territory.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Claude Cat is determined to get rid of the mistress's birthday present: a new puppy.

cartoons.title: What's Up Doc?
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny tells a reporter his life story, from his birth through his rise to cartoon stardom.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs' showbiz career is recounted from babyhood to stardom. Bugs and Elmer Fudd perform the title song.

context.title: The 1950s — The Classic Era
context.summary: The 1950s produced many of the cartoons now considered the greatest ever made. Chuck Jones was at the absolute height of his powers, creating masterworks like Duck Amuck (1953), What's Opera, Doc? (1957), and One Froggy Evening (1955). The Rabbit Season / Duck Season trilogy — Rabbit Fire, Rabbit Seasoning, and Duck! Rabbit, Duck! — redefined comedic timing in animation. Road Runner and Wile E. Coyote debuted in 1949 and became one of the most enduring comedic duos in film history, their cartoons built on the same escalating logic as a Buster Keaton film. The decade also saw Speedy Gonzales become a star, winning the Academy Award in 1955. By the late 1950s the theatrical short was dying — television was changing everything — but the quality remained extraordinary right to the end of the theatrical run.

cartoons.title: A Bear for Punishment
cartoons.desc: Papa Bear of the Three Bears suffers through Father's Day as Junyer and Mama shower him with painful gifts.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Junyer Bear has a number of surprises for Good Ol' Pa on Good Ol' Father's Day, whether he wants them or not.

cartoons.title: A Bone for a Bone
cartoons.desc: A dog and a gopher battle over a bone buried in the gopher's territory.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two polite gophers are in their underground home, playing gin, when a dog buries his bone right on top of them. They try to negotiate with the dog so that he will bury the bone elsewhere. But the dog refuses to be cooperative.

cartoons.title: A Fox in a Fix
cartoons.desc: A fox gets into a complicated predicament trying to raid a henhouse.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A bulldog guards a chicken coop that a hungry fox wants to raid. To gain the friendship and trust of the bulldog, the fox shaves his tail and pretends to be a hard-luck terrier looking for a place to live. The bulldog instantly sees through the fox's ploy but acts as though he's fooled. He agrees to share his home with the fox.

cartoons.title: A Hound For Trouble
cartoons.desc: Charlie Dog tries to find a new owner in Italy, pestering everyone he meets.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Kicked off the boat in Italy, Charlie forces himself upon a pizzeria owner. Donning his best Italian accent and garb, Charlie sets to work as a waiter, astonishing and horrifying the customers with his barefoot grape-stomping and musical rendition of "Atsa Matta for You?"

cartoons.title: Ballot Box Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny and Yosemite Sam run against each other in a mayoral election.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When Yosemite Sam campaigns on a platform including rabbit genocide, Bugs Bunny runs against him.

cartoons.title: Big Top Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny joins the circus and competes with a strongman bear who doesn't appreciate the competition.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny is hired to perform in Colonel Korny's Circus alongside Bruno the Magnificent, the Slobokian Acrobatic Bear, but Bruno doesn't want to share the limelight.

cartoons.title: Boobs in the Woods
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig goes on a nature retreat but Daffy Duck's antics in the forest ruin his peaceful vacation.

cartoons.title: Bunny Hugged
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny substitutes for a beaten-up wrestler and must face the massive Crusher in the ring.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs gets involved in a wrestling match to save Ravishing Ronald from the Crusher.

cartoons.title: Bushy Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny encounters an Australian bushman who wants to catch him, with the chase spanning the Outback.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs encounters marsupials and an aborigine in Australia's outback.

cartoons.title: Cheese Chasers
cartoons.desc: Two mice eat so much cheese they never want to see it again, and try to get a cat to eat them instead.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After eating their fill at a cheese factory, Hubie and Bertie decide there is nothing left to live for, and try to get Claude Cat to eat them.

cartoons.title: Chow Hound
cartoons.desc: A bullying dog forces a cat and mouse to steal food for him from various owners.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A muscular dog exploits a cat and a mouse for food, but they keep forgetting to bring him gravy!

cartoons.title: Corn Plastered
cartoons.desc: A crow pilfers corn from a farm while dodging the farmer's scarecrow and dog.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A beany-capped, wise-cracking crow invades a corn field owned by an elderly farmer. The farmer unsuccessfully attempts to kill the crow by using a gun, an axe, and a cannon.

cartoons.title: Dog Gone South
cartoons.desc: Charlie Dog stows away on a ship to the South and tries to convince a plantation owner to adopt him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Charlie Dog attempts to ingratiate himself to a southern plantation owner.

cartoons.title: Drip-Along Daffy
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck plays a hopeless Western hero while sidekick Porky Pig accidentally saves the town.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck plays a western hero, but things don't go as he hoped in a one horse town.

cartoons.title: Early to Bet
cartoons.desc: A cat loses bets to a bulldog and must pay with painful forfeits from a penalty wheel.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Gambling Bug causes gambling fever in anyone he bites.

cartoons.title: Foxy By Proxy:
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny substitutes for a fox in a fox hunt and runs circles around the hunting dogs.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs is provoked by a pack of foxhounds and their hunters stampeding over his hole, so he gets out his Halloween costume from last year (a fox suit) and sets out to lead the dogs on a merry chase. The stupidest of the dogs, whose objective is to cut a fox's tail off, becomes his main victim; Bugs tricks him into chasing a train instead. He eventually tricks the dog pack into running off a cliff, but the stupid dog ends up with Bugs' tail.

cartoons.title: French Rarebit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny visits Paris and outwits two French chefs who want to cook him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While visiting Paris, Bugs Bunny wanders past the restaurants of Louie and Francois, rival chefs who fight to cook him until he promises to teach them the recipe for 'Louisiana Back-bay Bayou Bunny Bordelaise à la Antoine'.

cartoons.title: Hare We Go
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny goes with Columbus on his voyage to America, causing havoc aboard the ship.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In 1492, Bugs Bunny sails the ocean blue, as mascot for Christopher Columbus.

cartoons.title: Hillbilly Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny outwits two hillbilly hunters by calling a square dance that sends them crashing everywhere.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While vacationing in the Ozark Mountains, Bugs Bunny encounters Curt and Pumpkinhead Martin, two dimwitted hillbillies who are duped by Bugs into a violent square dance.

cartoons.title: His Hare Raising Tale
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny tells his nephew exaggerated stories of his past adventures, using clips from earlier cartoons.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs tells stories to his nephew Clyde derived from earlier cartoons ("Baseball Bugs", "Stage Door Cartoon", "Rabbit Punch", "Falling Hare", and "Haredevil Hare").

cartoons.title: Lovelorn Leghorn
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn is pursued by a love-struck hen who won't take no for an answer.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The other hens make fun of Miss Prissy, who still has not found a husband. Prissy sets out, rolling pin in hand, to find one, and she comes upon confirmed bachelor Foghorn Leghorn in the midst of his feud with the barnyard dog. The dog helps Prissy take Foghorn as her mate by knocking him out and stuffing him in a picnic basket!

cartoons.title: Operation Rabbit
cartoons.tags: Road Runner / Wile E. Coyote, Bugs Bunny
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote, self-proclaimed 'super genius,' introduces himself to Bugs Bunny and tries elaborate traps.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote, genius, announces to Bugs Bunny that he is going to catch him and eat him, and then employs a variety of gadgets and plans in an attempt to do so.

cartoons.title: Putty Tat Trouble
cartoons.desc: Two cats compete with each other to catch Tweety, but the canary plays them against one another.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tweety Bird is shoveling out his nest atop a city pole after a snowstorm and is spotted by Sylvester Cat and a one-eyed orange tabby, who fight over Tweety. Tweety runs into a cellar where he befriends a wooden dunking bird. The two cats then chase Tweety into a park and onto a sheet of ice covering a pond. Tweety cuts a circle around the cats so that they fall into the freezing water and become bedridden with cold.

cartoons.title: Rabbit Every Monday
cartoons.desc: Yosemite Sam tries to cook Bugs Bunny in his cabin oven, but Bugs keeps outsmarting him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Yosemite Sam traps Bugs Bunny and tries to cook him -- until he sees that Bugs is having a party and wants to join in the fun.

cartoons.title: Room and Bird
cartoons.desc: Tweety and Sylvester are both pets in the same hotel, leading to a chaotic chase through the building.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tweety and Sylvester are Granny's pets in the Spinsters Arms Hotel, where pets aren't allowed.

cartoons.title: Sleepy Time Possum
cartoons.desc: A young possum tries to stay awake while his father insists he play dead like a proper possum.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Pa Possum dresses up like a dog to try to get Junior Possum to stop sleeping all the time and do his chores.

cartoons.title: Stooge For a Mouse
cartoons.desc: A mouse tricks a cat into thinking a bulldog is his buddy, using the threat to steal food freely.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A crafty mouse decides to remove his one obstacle to obtaining a block of cheese - Sylvester Cat - by stirring up trouble between Sylvester and Mike the Bulldog, two buddies turned to enemies by the mouse's clever set-ups implicating Sylvester in attacks on Mike.

cartoons.title: The Fair Haired Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny and Yosemite Sam both claim the same rabbit hole, waging an underground property war.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Yosemite Sam and Bugs battle it out over property rights above Bugs' rabbit hole.

cartoons.title: The Scarlet Pumpernickel
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck pitches a swashbuckling screenplay to the studio head, escalating the plot to absurd extremes.

cartoons.title: The Wearing of the Grin
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig stays the night in an Irish castle haunted by two leprechauns guarding their pot of gold.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig spends the night at an Irish castle after being caught in a storm, and gets in trouble with the two leprechauns who live there.

cartoons.title: Thumb Fun
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck hitchhikes with Porky Pig and makes the road trip miserable for his reluctant driver.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky Pig regrets picking up a hitchhiking Daffy Duck, whose anarchic driving habits forced on Porky result in the two being apprehended.

cartoons.title: Tweet Tweet Tweety
cartoons.desc: Tweety Bird outwits a hungry cat in a battle of wits between predator and prey.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat leaves a trailer in a National Forest Camping Ground to go bird hunting and discovers an egg in a nest. Sylvester decides to sit on the egg to hatch it, and when it hatches, out crawls Tweety Bird! Sylvester chases Tweety into a geyser and down a river in a boat toward a waterfall.

cartoons.title: Tweety's SOS
cartoons.desc: Sylvester the cat devises another elaborate scheme to catch Tweety Bird, but the little canary stays one step ahead.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat stows away aboard a seagoing passenger liner to try and catch Tweety Bird, who is guarded by his mistress, Granny. Sylvester becomes seasick and runs to the sickbay for a remedy. Tweety mixes nitro into the medicine before Sylvester drinks it. When Granny hits Sylvester with her broom, he is blown sky-high.

cartoons.title: Water, Water Every Hare
cartoons.desc: A flood washes Bugs Bunny into a mad scientist's castle where Gossamer the monster chases him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny's rabbit hole floods, causing him to float to the laboratory of an evil scientist who wants to use his brain for a robot.

cartoons.title: Who's Kitten Who?
cartoons.desc: The amorous French skunk Pepé Le Pew relentlessly pursues a reluctant cat who's been accidentally painted with a white stripe.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A baby kangaroo, Hippety Hopper, breaks free from a crate at the Zoo Office and hops into the house of Sylvester Cat and his son, Junior. They mistake Hippety for a giant mouse, and Sylvester is pummelled again and again by the playful kangaroo, causing Junior to put a paper bag over his head in shame for his father.

cartoons.title: 14 Carrot Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny prospecting for gold in the Klondike battles claim-jumper Nasty Canasta.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Yosemite Sam (as Chilikoot Sam) tries unsuccessfully to steal gold from Bugs Bunny during the Yukon gold rush.

cartoons.title: A Bird in a Guilty Cage
cartoons.desc: Tweety outsmarts Sylvester inside a department store after hours.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat spots Tweety Bird in a display window of an after-hours department store and sneaks inside through a mail server chute. Tweety flees Sylvester by hiding in a hat pile and a doll house, evades the shots from a rifle Sylvester uses, and escapes in a vacuum tube. Tweety sends a dynamite stick through another tube, and Sylvester swallows it, thinking it is Tweety. The dynamite blows up inside Sylvester after the cat leaves the store and walks down the street.

cartoons.title: Ain't She Tweet
cartoons.desc: Tweety Bird outwits a hungry cat in a battle of wits between predator and prey.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat discovers Tweety Bird in a pet store window. Tweety is taken to be delivered by truck to a new owner - Granny. Sylvester chases the delivery truck to Granny's home, where Granny has a huge, fenced-in area for her army of bulldogs. Sylvester makes several unsuccessful attempts to pass the dogs and reach Tweety inside Granny's house.

cartoons.title: Beep Beep
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote deploys another series of elaborate Acme contraptions in his endless pursuit of the Road Runner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Coyote chases the Road Runner through a maze of mine shafts.

cartoons.title: Don't Give Up the Sheep
cartoons.desc: Ralph Wolf and Sam Sheepdog clock in for work and spend the day as adversaries guarding a flock.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A sheepdog thwarts the efforts of a thieving wolf whose tricks include altering the time clock, hiding in a bush, imitating Pan, digging a tunnel, unleashing a wildcat and disguising himself as the dog's coworker.

cartoons.title: Feed the Kitty
cartoons.desc: A tough bulldog secretly adopts a tiny kitten and goes to hilarious lengths to hide it from his owner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A tough bulldog secretly adopts a tiny kitten and goes to hilarious lengths to hide it from his owner.

cartoons.title: Fool Coverage
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck sells Porky Pig an insurance policy, then tries to collect by causing Porky accidents.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck is an insurance peddler, who arrives uninvited at Porky Pig's door to persuade him to purchase an accident policy on the pretext that his home is loaded with hazards. When Porky rejects Daffy's claim that accidents in the home are "waiting" to happen, Daffy rigs some.

cartoons.title: Gift Wrapped
cartoons.desc: Sylvester disguises himself as a Christmas gift to get close to Tweety on Christmas morning.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: It's Christmas Day in the home of Granny, and her pet cat Sylvester delights at chasing her new Tweety Bird and takes fright at the bulldog unwrapped from under the tree.

cartoons.title: Hare Lift
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny boards Yosemite Sam's airplane and a battle for control of the plane ensues at high altitude.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny boards Yosemite Sam's airplane and a battle for control of the plane ensues at high altitude.

cartoons.title: Leghorn Swoggled
cartoons.desc: Henery Hawk actually manages to catch Foghorn Leghorn fair and square, but Foghorn schemes his way out by cutting a deal that leaves Henery with the Barnyard Dawg instead.

cartoons.title: Little Beau Pepe
cartoons.desc: The amorous French skunk Pepé Le Pew relentlessly pursues a reluctant cat who's been accidentally painted with a white stripe.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After driving the Foreign Legionnaires from their fort with his aroma, lovesick skunk Pepe falls for the camp mascot, a cat who's accidentally gotten a white stripe painted down her back.

cartoons.title: Little Red Rodent Hood
cartoons.desc: A mouse retells Little Red Riding Hood with a cat as the Big Bad Wolf.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An elderly mouse tells the bedtime story of Little Red Riding Hood to her grandson, who visualizes the tale in cat-and-mouse terms, with himself as Red and Sylvester as the Big Bad Wolf.

cartoons.title: Mouse Warming
cartoons.desc: A mouse moves into a house already occupied by Claude Cat, setting off a turf war.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A teen-aged boy mouse falls in love with the girl mouse who lives in the hole across the room. But Claude Cat literally comes between them, and also tries to stir up a feud between their two families.

cartoons.title: Oily Hare
cartoons.desc: A Texas oilman tries to drill on Bugs Bunny's property, but Bugs won't give up his home.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A Texas oilman fights Bugs over property rights to his rabbit hole.

cartoons.title: Rabbit Fire
cartoons.tags: Bugs Bunny, Elmer Fudd, Daffy Duck
cartoons.desc: The classic hunting trilogy begins as Bugs and Daffy debate whose season it is in front of hunter Elmer.

cartoons.title: Rabbit Seasoning
cartoons.desc: The second installment of the hunting trilogy: Bugs and Daffy argue over whose season it is while Elmer hunts.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd is hunting both Daffy Duck and Bugs Bunny again.

cartoons.title: Rabbit's Kin
cartoons.desc: A little rabbit asks Bugs Bunny for protection from the terrifying Pete Puma.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs rescues a young rabbit from Pete Puma and gives lessons on how to heckle.

cartoons.title: Snow Business
cartoons.desc: Granny, Tweety, and Sylvester are snowed in, and the starving cat eyes the canary as his only meal.

cartoons.title: The Hasty Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny faces off against Marvin the Martian in an interplanetary confrontation.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A Martian, with his green dog-soldier, K-9, arrive on Earth with instructions to bring back an Earth creature. He chooses Bugs Bunny.

cartoons.title: The Prize Pest
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig wins a chimpanzee in a radio contest and the destructive primate proceeds to tear his house apart while Porky desperately tries to maintain control.

cartoons.title: The Super Snooper
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck plays a hardboiled detective investigating a mystery.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In this parody of trench-coat detective films, Daffy Duck is Duck Drake, a "Private Eye, Ear, Nose, and Throat" who receives a telephone call summoning him to the J. Cleaver Axe-Handle Estate, where a murder has supposedly taken place.

cartoons.title: The Turn-Tale Wolf
cartoons.desc: The Big Bad Wolf tells his nephew his version of Three Little Pigs, casting himself as the hero.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Big Bad Wolf's proper little nephew has learned at school that his uncle was the fiend who blew the Three Little Pigs' houses down and is ashamed that his uncle could have committed such a deed, so his uncle tells him what REALLY happened.

cartoons.title: Tree For Two
cartoons.desc: A bulldog and Sylvester compete in a tough-guy contest, but a baby kangaroo makes Sylvester look like a champ.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A rough and tough bulldog named Spike sets out with his admirer, a small dog named Chester, to rough up a cat. They encounter Sylvester and chase him into a junkyard, where a black panther that escaped from a zoo just happens to be hiding out. Every time Spike goes into the junkyard to thrash Sylvester, he is clawed into pieces by the panther, which he, in a dark maze of crates, thinks is Sylvester. Chester has no problem pummelling Sylvester before Spike's eyes, which convinces Spike that Chester must be tougher than him.

cartoons.title: A Mouse Divided
cartoons.desc: Speedy Gonzales, the fastest mouse in all of Mexico, outruns and outsmarts his feline pursuers.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A drunken stork delivers a baby mouse to the home of Mr. and Mrs. Sylvester Cat. Sylvester is about to eat the little rodent when it calls him Daddy. Touched, Sylvester adopts the mouse as his son - which, distressingly, attracts every hungry cat in the neighborhood to his door!

cartoons.title: A Peck o' Trouble
cartoons.desc: A mama woodpecker must protect her eggs from a hungry cat lurking near the nest.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Lazy Dodsworth the Cat wants to catch a woodpecker for his breakfast. The woodpecker has built its home inside the upper trunk of a very tall tree, and Dodsworth puts on a professor's cap, pretending to be a passive teacher of bird-catching and thereby deceive an eager-to-learn kitten into doing the perilous ascending of the tree to try to catch the woodpecker.

cartoons.title: A Street Cat Named Sylvester
cartoons.desc: Sylvester ends up in a bulldog household and must survive the hostile territory.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The title of this cartoon is a misnomer, because it is in fact Tweety Bird who is the homeless one here, and Sylvester is Granny's pet. Tweety seeks shelter from a blizzard and taps on Granny's house door. Sylvester answers and grabs the canary. He tries to hide Tweety from Granny while evading the attacks of Hector, Granny's bed-ridden bulldog, who wants revenge on Sylvester for his broken leg. Tweety keeps escaping Sylvester's clutches, with Hector's help.

cartoons.title: Ant Pasted
cartoons.desc: An ant colony declares war on a bratty young boy who's been destroying their hill.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd, on a fourth of July picnic, throws some of his firecrackers into an ant colony, and the ants declare all-out war on him.

cartoons.title: Bully for Bugs
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny takes a wrong turn into a bullfighting ring and outsmarts a ferocious bull.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny takes a wrong turn into a bullfighting ring and outsmarts a ferocious bull.

cartoons.title: Cat-Tails for Two
cartoons.desc: Two dim-witted cats try to catch Speedy Gonzales near the Mexican border but are hopelessly outmatched.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two cats try to catch Speedy Gonzales aboard a ship, without much success.

cartoons.title: Catty Cornered
cartoons.desc: Sylvester is tormented by a super-strong tiny mouse who won't stay caught.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Rocky the gangster kidnaps Tweety Bird for a million dollar ransom and holes up in an abandoned city building...

cartoons.title: Cracked Quack
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck sneaks into Porky's home to escape the winter cold and hides as a stuffed duck ornament, but Porky's dog keeps trying to expose him.

cartoons.title: Duck Dodgers in the 24½th Century
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck as Duck Dodgers races Marvin the Martian to claim Planet X for Earth.

cartoons.title: Duck! Rabbit Duck!
cartoons.desc: The third hunting trilogy finale — Daffy keeps tricking Elmer with signs but still ends up getting shot.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The final installment of the "Hunting Trilogy" once again has Elmer out hunting, while Bugs and Daffy try to con him into shooting the other.

cartoons.title: Forward March Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is accidentally drafted into the army when he's mistaken for a human recruit.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny gets a draft notice by mistake and joins the army, with disastrous results, especially for the sergeant of his platoon.

cartoons.title: Fowl Weather
cartoons.desc: Sylvester disguises himself as a scarecrow to get close to the chickens, but a dog guards them.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Granny is Tweety Bird's mistress on a farm. She assigns a bulldog named Hector to take care of Tweety while she's away. Sylvester Cat disguises himself as a scarecrow to sneak up on Tweety. Tweety runs into a chicken coop and is protected by a mother hen and an aggressive rooster. Hector, seeing that Tweety is gone and fearing Granny's wrath, paints Sylvester yellow and puts him in Tweety's cage to fool Granny. Tweety returns and makes like a cat since turnabout is fair play.

cartoons.title: Going Going Gosh
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote unleashes another round of elaborate Acme contraptions — roller skates, grenades, dynamite crossbow, and a jet unicycle — in his perpetually doomed pursuit of the Road Runner.

cartoons.title: Hare Trimmed
cartoons.desc: Yosemite Sam and Bugs Bunny both disguise themselves as suitors to win a rich widow's fortune.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Yosemite Sam hears that Granny has inherited fifty million dollars. Good guy Bugs tries to save Granny from Sam's clutches.

cartoons.title: Hoppy-Go-Lucky
cartoons.desc: Sylvester teams up with a dim-witted bulldog named Beaky to hunt what they believe is a giant mouse — but their quarry is Hippety Hopper the baby kangaroo.

cartoons.title: Kiddin' the Kitten
cartoons.desc: A lazy tomcat tricks a naive young kitten into doing all his mouse-catching work for him, then passes the kitten's catches off to his owner as his own.

cartoons.title: Much Ado About Nutting
cartoons.desc: A determined squirrel tries every possible method to crack open a stubborn coconut.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A squirrel in a downtown park lugs a giant coconut back home, but nothing he tries will crack it open.

cartoons.title: Punch Trunk
cartoons.desc: A tiny elephant appears in the city, causing mass panic despite being only a few inches tall.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A tiny elephant emerges from a banana boat and wanders about town, causing an uproar among the populace. Sightings are attributed variously to mass hysteria, insanity and dipsomania.

cartoons.title: Robot Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd builds a robot to catch Bugs Bunny, but the mechanical hunter has a mind of its own.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny faces off against Farmer Fudd's robot.

cartoons.title: Sock A Doodle Do
cartoons.desc: A small banty rooster stands up to a much larger rooster who's been bullying the barnyard.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A prize-fighting banty rooster, so slap-happy that he goes into a punching spree whenever he hears a bell, falls out of a truck and onto the farm where Foghorn Leghorn is in the midst of his usual sparring match with the barnyard dog. Foghorn and the dog use the fighter-rooster's manic punching against each other by ringing a bell once the rooster is within striking distance of their intended victim.

cartoons.title: Southern Fried Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny tries to cross into the South but encounters Yosemite Sam guarding the Mason-Dixon line.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny attempts to shake off Yosemite Sam (here, cast as a Civil War-era colonel), who is preventing him from crossing the Mason-Dixon Line.

cartoons.title: Terrier-Stricken
cartoons.desc: A dog chases a cat into a department store for a wild pursuit through the aisles.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Frisky Puppy's sudden barking and playful antics send Claude Cat on wild rides through their house, down the chimney, in and out of faucets, out the door, and eventually diving into an empty swimming pool.

cartoons.title: There Auto Be A Law
cartoons.desc: A gopher repeatedly undermines a man's attempts to build a highway through his burrow.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: This documentary-style cartoon tells of the development of the automobile in America and the comical effects of cars, traffic, and road design on various kinds of people.

cartoons.title: Bell Hoppy
cartoons.desc: A frog bellhop at a hotel deals with Sylvester the cat trying to catch him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester has been "blackballed" out of membership to the Loyal Order of Alley Cats Mouse and Chowder Club again. To gain the long-coveted membership, the Grand Master offers to let the lisping puddy tat place a big bell around the neck of the largest mouse he can find, so the cats can pounce on the mouse when they hear the bell. Just as that's going on, Hippety Hopper escapes from a city zoo truck. It's not long before he encounters the hapless Sylvester. Each attempt to place the bell around Hippety's neck ends with Sylvester wearing the bell (and the cats pounding the puddy into submission). In the end, Sylvester finally does get the bell around Hippety's neck, but by the time the cats are ready to pounce on the baby kangaroo-mistaken-for-a-giant-mouse, Hippety has been recaptured. The oblivious cats end up jumping in front of the city zoo truck! Sylvester now gets to serve as Loyal Order's Grand Master.

cartoons.title: Bewitched Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny encounters a witch and uses his wits to escape her clutches.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs must rescue Hansel and Gretel from Witch Hazel's clutches.

cartoons.title: Bugs and Thugs
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is kidnapped by gangsters Rocky and Mugsy but turns the tables on them from inside their hideout.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When Bugs calls a cab, he doesn't know it's the getaway car for a couple of bankrobbers (however, he does know the capital of Nevada).

cartoons.title: Captain Hareblower
cartoons.desc: Pirate Yosemite Sam fires on Bugs Bunny's ship, launching a naval comedy of errors.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs will not bend to the threats of the pirate Yosemite Sam.

cartoons.title: Cats A-weigh!
cartoons.desc: A cat aboard a ship tries to catch a mouse, but a protective bulldog keeps intervening.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat accepts a position as mouse-catcher on a ship, and his son, Junior, accompanies him. They encounter baby kangaroo Hippety Hopper being shipped from Australia and, as usual, mistake Hippety for a giant mouse.

cartoons.title: Claws For Alarm
cartoons.desc: Porky and Sylvester check into a haunted desert hotel where mice plot to murder Porky in his sleep.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Porky and Sylvester stay overnight in what Sylvester realizes is a terrifying hotel filled with endless imminent danger.

cartoons.title: Design For Leaving
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck is a door-to-door salesman who installs push-button automation throughout Porky's house with disastrous results.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck is a salesman for a futuristic appliance company, who, against Elmer Fudd's will, modernizes Fudd's house with many screwball gadgets, none of which work in Fudd's favor.

cartoons.title: Devil May Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny meets the Tasmanian Devil for the first time and must outwit the ravenous whirlwind.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Tasmanian Devil is on the loose. Bugs offers to help him find his dinner.

cartoons.title: Dog Pounded
cartoons.desc: Tweety's cage falls into a dog pound, and Sylvester must brave the dogs to reach him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat tries to catch Tweety Bird, who is up in a tree in the middle of the city dog pound.

cartoons.title: Dr. Jerkyl's Hide
cartoons.desc: Sylvester and a bulldog encounter Spike's owner who drinks a Jekyll-and-Hyde potion.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two cockney canines chase Sylvester Cat into the lab of Dr. Jerkyl, where the cat drinks Hyde formula...

cartoons.title: Duck Amuck
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck battles an unseen animator who keeps changing the cartoon around him — revealed to be Bugs Bunny.

cartoons.title: Easy Peckin's
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn tries to pawn off an annoying little chick on a hungry cat.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A no-nonsense, hulky rooster guards a chicken coop that a sneaky fox repeatedly tries to raid.

cartoons.title: Feline Frame-Up
cartoons.desc: Claude Cat is framed by a mouse for household destruction in front of his owner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After Claude frames Marc Antony, making it look like the bulldog ate the kitty, Marc must try various methods of getting back at Claude from outside the yard.

cartoons.title: From A to Z-Z-Z-Z
cartoons.desc: A schoolboy named Ralph daydreams elaborate fantasies during class, from knights to fighter pilots.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Ralph is a daydreamer... and he is quick to adapt his current surroundings into new, adventurous dreams.

cartoons.title: Gone Batty
cartoons.desc: Two baseball teams face off in a wacky game full of slapstick gags.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bobo the Elephant is baseball team mascot for the lean and meek Sweetwater Shnooks, all of whom are rendered unconscious by their opponents, the husky and brutal Greenville Goons. The Shnooks' manager, rather than forfeit the game, decides to bring in Bobo to play every position - and he does rather well!

cartoons.title: Goo Goo Goliath
cartoons.desc: A giant baby is accidentally delivered to a normal-sized family by a stork.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A drunken stork delivers the baby of a giant to a normal-sized couple instead, and they try to raise him as well as they can.

cartoons.title: I Gopher You
cartoons.desc: The Goofy Gophers raid a vegetable garden, politely taking turns outsmarting the gardener.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two polite twin gophers are indignant at the swiping of all their vegetables by "vandals" in trucks. They follow the trucks to a food processing plant and become caught in the machinery when they try to retrieve their property.

cartoons.title: Kiss Me Cat
cartoons.desc: Bulldog Marc Antony fights to keep his beloved kitten Pussyfoot out of trouble after it causes havoc in the house, risking both of them being thrown out by their owner.

cartoons.title: Little Boy Boo
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn tries to toughen up a nerdy bookworm chick.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foghorn Leghorn, shivering at the thought of another cold winter in his dilapidated roost, decides to court the well-to-do Miss Prissy, but Prissy won't marry him unless he can prove he'll be a good father to her son, a bespectacled egghead genius who, by scientific means, bests Foghorn in every game they play.

cartoons.title: Lumber-Jack Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny meets a giant dog in the forest in this cartoon originally shown in 3-D.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny stumbles on the carrot patch of Paul Bunyan, but doesn't realize that it is guarded by a 124-foot, 4,600-ton dog named Smidgen.

cartoons.title: Muscle Tussle
cartoons.desc: Pepé Le Pew's would-be girlfriend brings a muscular cat to make the skunk jealous.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck takes his girl to a beach, where a muscle-bound duck attracts the attentions of Daffy's fickle chick. She leaves Daffy and walks off with the hunky duck. A salesman sells Daffy a bogus strength-building tonic, and Daffy takes some, thinking it has made him into a virile power-house! He challenges the muscular duck to a series of contests involving bar-bending, chain-chewing, and weight-lifting.

cartoons.title: Muzzle Tough
cartoons.desc: Tweety outsmarts Sylvester and a bulldog in a three-way chase.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tweety Bird moves into a city brownstone with his mistress, Granny. A stray Sylvester Cat watches them move in and delights on seeing Tweety. Another of Granny's pets is a bulldog who complicates Sylvester's plan to sneak up close enough to make a grab for Tweety. Sylvester unsuccessfully tries all sorts of disguises, including a moving man, a lamp, a bearskin, and a female dog. He ends up being captured by the dog catcher and placed in the back of a truck surrounded by snarling canines.

cartoons.title: No Barking
cartoons.desc: A nervous dog is haunted by a tiny, invisible flea who taunts him relentlessly.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A homeless cat (Claude Cat) searching for food is harassed by the playful antics and barking of an energetic pup (Frisky Puppy). Frisky repeatedly sneaks up behind the poor tabby cat (who hates the dog) and scares it into jumping vertically when it barks. After Claude finally silences the pup, he encounters a larger dog, whose bark has a disastrous effect. Tweety Bird has two lines. Can you guess what they are?

cartoons.title: No Parking Hare
cartoons.desc: A highway construction crew threatens Bugs Bunny's home, and he refuses to move for the freeway.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A construction worker wants to blast Bugs out of his rabbit hole so he can build a freeway.

cartoons.title: Of Rice and Hen
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn's rival in love with Miss Prissy hatches a scheme to win her affection.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Miss Prissy, the slow-witted hen, sets out to land a husband - Foghorn Leghorn, and Barnyard Dog is willing to help her by dressing as a rooster to "rival" Foghorn Leghorn's non-existent affections and make him jealous so that he'll marry Prissy without thinking. Foghorn Leghorn falls for the scheme - hook, line, and sinker.

cartoons.title: Pizzicato Pussycat
cartoons.desc: A concert mouse makes music by plucking a sleeping cat's whiskers like a harp.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Mr. and Mrs. Jones hear a piano being played in their living room. They automatically assume it is their cat who is making the music, when in fact, the talented one is a mouse whom the cat has forced into being his stooge to make him famous. The cat is showered with media attention and set to play at Carnegie Hall, where he hopes nobody will notice that he is pantomiming the movements with the keys while the mouse is playing his miniature piano inside the full-scale model.

cartoons.title: Plop Goes the Weasel
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn battles a sneaky weasel trying to raid the henhouse for an easy chicken dinner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A lip-smacking weasel invades the barnyard of Foghorn Leghorn and his usual canine foe, and Foghorn is quite willing to put baby chicks in danger of being taken by the weasel so long as it makes the dog appear to be failing his job of guarding the chicks.

cartoons.title: Satan's Waitin'
cartoons.desc: Sylvester uses up his nine lives trying to catch Tweety while a devilish cat waits below.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester's carnivorous pursuit of Tweety Bird continues, winding up the cat's spirit in Hell, where he meets a satanic bulldog.

cartoons.title: Sheep Ahoy
cartoons.desc: Ralph Wolf and Sam Sheepdog square off in another day of sheep-guarding, clocking in and out like coworkers.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After punching in for work, Sam Sheepdog deals with Ralph Wolf's attempts to steal the flock, which this time make use of a balloon, a fake Acme-brand rock and a bicycle-propelled submarine.

cartoons.title: Stop, Look and Hasten!
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote deploys another series of elaborate Acme contraptions in his endless pursuit of the Road Runner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A Burmese tiger trap, a pop-up steel wall, a motorcycle, and a box of Acme-brand leg-building vitamins can't help the Coyote (Eatibus anythingus) catch the Road Runner (Hot Rodicus supersonicus).

cartoons.title: The Cat's Baw
cartoons.desc: Pepé Le Pew pursues his feline quarry through an apartment building in amorous pursuit.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Pepé Le Pew pursues his feline quarry through an apartment building in amorous pursuit.

cartoons.title: The Oily American
cartoons.desc: An oily American tourist visits a foreign country and clashes with the local culture.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Moe Hican, an Indian, has struck it rich. Oil has been found on his property, and he now owns an estate with oil rigs everywhere. Even the fountain spouts oil! His mansion is as upper crust as any, but he prefers to live in a tipi and hunt moose, within the rooms of his vast home, which have forests of their own. Moe and his butler go on a hunting expedition, with the butler being hit with every instrument Moe uses to try to kill a pint-sized moose.

cartoons.title: Upswept Hare
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd tries to catch Bugs Bunny in a department store after the rabbit sets up home there.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Penthouse dwelling Elmer Fudd brings home a beautiful flowering desert plant and - unknowingly - Bugs Bunny.

cartoons.title: Wild Over You
cartoons.desc: The amorous French skunk Pepé Le Pew relentlessly pursues a reluctant cat who's been accidentally painted with a white stripe.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A wildcat escapes from the zoo, disguises herself as a skunk to fool her pursuers, but that only attracts lovestruck Pepe le Pew.

cartoons.title: Wild Wife
cartoons.desc: A housewife recounts her chaotic day of errands and shopping to her husband when he gets home from work.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A harried housewife is criticized by her male-chauvinist husband, who remarks that she doesn't make effective use of her time during the day and insinuates that she doesn't finish her chores because of laziness.

cartoons.title: Zipping Along
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote sets up elaborate desert traps for the Road Runner, all of which spectacularly backfire.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Hypnosis doesn't help the Coyote catch the Road Runner, nor do a clutch of string-controlled rifles or dozens of mousetraps, but they all manage to backfire on him, naturally.

cartoons.title: All Fowled Up
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn protects a young chick from a hungry cat prowling the barnyard.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Intending to catch a chicken for his dinner, little Henery the Chicken Hawk ventures onto the farm of the eternally feuding Foghorn Leghorn and barnyard dog. Foghorn tries to dump a load of concrete on top of the dog, but the chute for dropping the concrete suddenly extends itself to a position directly above Foghorn, who is covered over by the concrete and frozen in a "Thinker" pose. Little Henery attaches a rope to the cement-laden Foghorn and drags him home for a tough-to-chew chicken dinner.

cartoons.title: Baby Buggy Bunny
cartoons.desc: A tiny gangster disguises himself as a baby to retrieve his stolen loot from Bugs Bunny's rabbit hole.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Baby-Faced Finster robs a bank, but the baby carriage with the money in it goes down Bugs' rabbit hole.

cartoons.title: Beanstalk Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny and Daffy Duck climb a beanstalk and encounter Elmer Fudd as the giant.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny and Daffy Duck (as Jack) find themselves at the top of a beanstalk where they get chased around by a giant Elmer Fudd.

cartoons.title: By Word of Mouse
cartoons.desc: A German mouse lectures American mice about capitalism using a department store as an example.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Hans, a mouse from Germany, comes to America to visit his cousin Willie, and learns about the wonders of the capitalist system.

cartoons.title: Dime to Retire
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig checks into a cheap hotel run by a swindler who charges extra for every nuisance he secretly causes.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Exhausted traveler Porky Pig drives into a town looking for a hotel. He is delighted to find one with a 10 cents per-night fee. Unfortunately, its manager is Daffy Duck.

cartoons.title: Double or Mutton
cartoons.desc: Ralph Wolf and Sam Sheepdog battle again in their daily sheep-guarding routine.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Ralph Wolf wants to steal sheep; Sam Sheepdog wants to stop him. Ralph's tricks include digging a tunnel, walking a tightrope, launching a guided missile, dressing as Little Bo Peep, shooting a cannon and growing Sam's hair.

cartoons.title: Feather Dusted
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn must deal with a prissy young rooster who challenges his barnyard authority.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foghorn Leghorn decides to teach Miss Prissy's genius son Egghead Jr. how to have fun by playing croquet, cowboys and Indians.

cartoons.title: Jumpin' Jupiter
cartoons.desc: Porky Pig and Sylvester are camping when aliens abduct them, mistaking Earth creatures for specimens.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A strange alien captures Porky Pig and Sylvester's entire campsite as a sample to take back to its planet, but only Sylvester figures out what is really going on.

cartoons.title: Lighthouse Mouse
cartoons.desc: A cat is assigned to guard a lighthouse from mice, but the mice prove to be formidable opponents.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat is a lighthouse keeper's mouse-catcher assigned to keep a mouse from unplugging the light. The mouse only wants a good night's sleep and asks Hippety Hopper, the baby kangaroo who has just crashed off of a ship on the nearby rocks, to help him fight Sylvester and keep the lighthouse light turned off.

cartoons.title: Lumber Jerks
cartoons.desc: The Goofy Gophers try to save their tree home from being chopped down and turned into furniture.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two polite gophers find that their home, a tree, has been cut down and taken away. They find it in a log pile about to be taken inside a processing factory. Following it into there, they become caught in the daunting machinery.

cartoons.title: My Little Duckaroo
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck as the Western Quacker plays Robin Hood of the range, battling Nasty Canasta.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck is a Wild West outlaw named "The Masked Avenger", righter of wrongs and doer of heroic deeds. Porky Pig is his sidekick. Together, they seek to arrest Nasty Canasta, a villain whose crimes include gag-stealing and square dancing in a round house.

cartoons.title: Past Perfumance
cartoons.desc: Pepé Le Pew chases a cat through a series of historical periods.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Paris, 1913: Passionate, odiferous Pepe Le Pew pursues the latest love of his life, a cat who's been made up to look like a skunk, through the sets of a silent-movie studio.

cartoons.title: Pests For Guests
cartoons.desc: The Goofy Gophers move into Elmer Fudd's house and politely eat everything in sight.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd buys a wooden chest of drawers not knowing that two polite twin gophers (known as The Goofy Gophers) have claimed the piece of furniture as their new home.

cartoons.title: Quack Shot
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck outwits Elmer Fudd during duck hunting season with his trademark zaniness.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd goes duck hunting on a pond, where Daffy Duck proclaims himself guardian of all his web-footed cousins and retaliates against Elmer.

cartoons.title: Rabbit Rampage
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is tormented by an unseen animator who keeps redrawing the scene — revealed to be Daffy getting revenge.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny is playfully harassed by his animator.

cartoons.title: Ready..Set..Zoom!
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote deploys another series of elaborate Acme contraptions in his endless pursuit of the Road Runner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Among the strategies that fail in Wile E. Coyote's attempts to catch the Roadrunner: glue on the road, a giant rubber band, an outboard motor in a wash tub, and dressing in drag as a female Roadrunner.

cartoons.title: Red Riding Hoodwinked
cartoons.desc: Sylvester plays the Big Bad Wolf in a twisted retelling of Little Red Riding Hood.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Red Riding Hood is on her way from the city to the country, to visit Granny. She's bringing Tweety Bird to Granny as a gift - which attracts Sylvester's attention. Along the way she also meets the Big Bad Wolf. Sylvester wants to eat Tweety. Big Bad wants to eat Red.

cartoons.title: Sahara Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny crosses the Sahara Desert and tangles with a Foreign Legion-commanding Yosemite Sam.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Riff-Raff (Yosemite) Sam, riding a camel that won't whoa, chases Bugs into a French Foreign-Legion post.

cartoons.title: Sandy Claws
cartoons.desc: Tweety and Sylvester head to the beach where the cat's pursuit is complicated by a crab and a lifeguard.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tweety Bird goes to the beach with Granny, and Sylvester tries once again to catch him.

cartoons.title: Stork Naked
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck and his wife await their egg's hatching while a hungry dog plots to steal it.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A drunken stork comes to the home of Mr. and Mrs. Daffy Duck with a bundle of joy, but Daffy wants no part of the delivery and takes extreme measures to keep the stork away.

cartoons.title: The Hole Idea
cartoons.desc: An inventor creates a portable hole that can be placed anywhere, leading to comic complications.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A scientist invents the portable hole, only to have a thief steal his samples to go on a crime spree.

cartoons.title: Tweety's Circus
cartoons.desc: Tweety performs at a circus while Sylvester tries to catch him amid the big-top chaos.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat pays a visit to a closed-to-business circus and finds Tweety Bird in one of the cages. Tweety escapes and a mad chase ensues. Meanwhile, Sylvester must flee from an uncaged lion he angered earlier.

cartoons.title: Yankee Doodle Bugs
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny teaches his nephew Clyde about American history through cartoon reenactments.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs lectures his nephew Clyde about early America, inserting himself into events wherever possible.

cartoons.title: A Kiddies kitty
cartoons.desc: A little girl adopts Sylvester as her pet and loves him so roughly that he'd rather face the dog.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: To escape a bulldog, Sylvester Cat allows himself to be adopted by a little girl. The little girl turns out to be rougher than the bulldog, though in her case it is entirely out of love.

cartoons.title: Broom-Stick Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny encounters a witch and uses his wits to escape her clutches.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: On Halloween night, Bugs Bunny, masquerading as a witch, trick-or-treats at the creepy old mansion of Witch Hazel, who prides herself on being the ugliest witch of all.

cartoons.title: Bugs Bonnets
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny and Elmer Fudd find a box of hats that change their personalities with each new hat they wear.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A passing truck spills a variety of hats, causing Elmer and Bugs to change personalities in rapid succession to fit the headgear they wind up wearing.

cartoons.title: Gee Whiz-z-z-z-z-z-z
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote employs rockets, catapults, and other doomed gadgets in his endless Road Runner pursuit.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote unsuccessfully chases the Road Runner using such contrivances as a rifle, a steel plate, a dynamite stick on an extending metal pulley, a painting of a collapsed bridge (which the Coyote falls into while Road Runner passes right through), and a jet motor.

cartoons.title: Guided Muscle
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote straps himself to a rocket and other Acme devices in another failed Road Runner chase.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: While cooking a tin can, the Coyote spots a better meal rushing by: the Road Runner.

cartoons.title: Hare Brush
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd thinks he's a rabbit while Bugs ends up taking Elmer's place — and his tax obligations.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The corporate board has Elmer committed to an asylum because he thinks he's a rabbit. At the sanitarium, Bugs agrees to trade places with Elmer.

cartoons.title: Heaven Scent
cartoons.desc: Pepé Le Pew pursues a painted cat through the streets and gardens of Paris.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: On the French Riviera, a female cat is frightened by sudden outbursts of barking by every dog around her. So, to scare them away, she paints her back with a white stripe like that of a skunk. But she doesn't receive the peace she'd expected, because Pepé Le Pew, the amorous French skunk, sees her, thinks she's a girl skunk, and pursues her.

cartoons.title: Heir Conditioned
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd inherits a fortune and Sylvester tries to convince him to keep it rather than give it all away.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester is a rich cat, courtesy of his deceased mistress, who has left him $3 million. His alley cat friends, hope to sponge off his good fortune, and Sylvester is eager to share with them. But Elmer Fudd, as Sylvester's new financial advisor, lectures him on investing his wealth in business and industry.

cartoons.title: Hyde and Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is adopted by Dr. Jekyll, who keeps transforming into a monster when nobody's looking.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny manages to get himself adopted by kindly Dr. Jekyll, but is surprised when his benefactor turns into the horrible Mr. Hyde after drinking a potion.

cartoons.title: Knight-Mare Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is transported to medieval times where he faces a jousting knight.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An apple falls on Bugs' head, transporting him back to King Arthur's England.

cartoons.title: Mixed Master
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn convinces the Barnyard Dawg's son to follow his teachings instead of his father's.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Harry, a mild-mannered man, brings home a shaggy dog he has named Robert. Harry's wife, Alice, disapproves because they already have a dog named Chang.

cartoons.title: One Froggy Evening
cartoons.desc: A man discovers a singing frog in a building cornerstone, but the frog only performs when nobody else is watching.

cartoons.title: Pappy's Puppy
cartoons.desc: Sylvester's son brings home a bulldog puppy, causing his father endless grief.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Butch, a mean bulldog, teaches his young son about the facts of life, including how to attack cats. The pup is at first frightened when he encounters Sylvester, but the youngster (remembering his father's lesson) soon becomes a thorn in the pussycat's side.

cartoons.title: Rabbitson Crusoe
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is shipwrecked on a desert island where Yosemite Sam has been stranded for years.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Crusoe, played by Yosemite Sam, has been living off coconuts for 20 years when Bugs washes up on his island.

cartoons.title: Rocket Squad
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck and Porky Pig play space police officers in a Dragnet parody set in the future.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In a futuristic city, Detectives Monday and Tuesday pursue a wanted criminal.

cartoons.title: Roman Legion Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is captured and sentenced to the lions in ancient Rome by Emperor Yosemite Sam.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Coliseum, Rome, 54 A.D. Yosemite Sam, as Captain of the Guard, is ordered by Emperor Nero to find a victim to toss to the lions, or else he'll be the victim. Shortly thereafter Sam encounters Bugs Bunny and decides he will make a good victim.

cartoons.title: Speedy Gonzales
cartoons.desc: The fastest mouse in Mexico rescues his friends from Sylvester the cat guarding the cheese factory — an Oscar winner.

cartoons.title: Strangled Eggs
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn is forced to babysit an egg that hatches into a troublesome baby bird.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foghorn Leghorn courts Miss Prissy when a foundling is left on her doorstep. It is Henery the Chicken Hawk.

cartoons.title: Stupor Duck
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck plays a superhero parody, trying and failing to find crimes to stop.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck does Superman as Stupor Duck (aka mild-mannered reporter Cluck Trent) takes on the villainous yet nonexistent Aardvark Ratnik.

cartoons.title: The Egg-Cited Rooster
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn stumbles upon a stray egg and becomes utterly convinced he laid it himself, defending this absurd claim against the skeptical Barnyard Dawg.

cartoons.title: The High and the Flighty
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn and his rival Daffy Duck compete in an escalating prank war.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Salesman Daffy Duck comes upon a farm, the site of Foghorn Leghorn's ongoing feud with the barnyard dog, and proceeds to sell Foghorn and the dog contraptions to continue their violent, mutual heckling.

cartoons.title: This Is a Life?
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny's career is celebrated on a 'This Is Your Life' TV parody with his nemeses as surprise guests.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Parody of "This is Your Life," with Elmer Fudd as the host and Bugs Bunny as the guest of honor, much to the disgust of Daffy Duck. On several occassions, Granny has to whack Daffy over the head to get him to be quiet. Meanwhile, Bugs reminisces with Elmer and Yosemite Sam about their previous encounters (reviewed via footage from past Bugs Bunny cartoons).

cartoons.title: Too Hop to Handle
cartoons.desc: Sylvester and his son chase Hippety Hopper, still convinced the kangaroo is a giant mouse.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat scoffs at his son's idea that a pipe like that of The Pied Piper of Hamelin could lure mice into their home to catch. But when Junior tries it and Hippety Hopper, the baby kangaroo, comes along, Sylvester believes in the power of the pipe and that Hippety is a giant mouse.

cartoons.title: Tree Cornered Tweety
cartoons.desc: Tweety and Sylvester chase each other around the neighborhood and up a tree.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat chases Tweety Bird while Tweety narrates. The chase takes them out of the city to the country, straight into a mine field, down a ski slope, and to the middle of a wooden bridge, where Sylvster stupidly saws a hole, with himself in its center.

cartoons.title: Two Scent's Worth
cartoons.desc: Pepé Le Pew chases a painted cat through the streets of Paris and into the sewers.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A crook disguises a cat as a skunk to scare people out of a bank. Soon after, great lover Pepé Le Pew sees her and the chase is on through the French Alps.

cartoons.title: A Star Is Bored
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny hires Daffy Duck as his stunt double, and Daffy suffers through every dangerous scene.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck must double for Bugs in any slapstick which Warners considers too dangerous for its star Bug Bunny.

cartoons.title: Ali Baba Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs and Daffy tunnel into Ali Baba's treasure cave, where Daffy's greed causes endless trouble.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs and Daffy tunnel into Ali Baba's treasure cave, where Daffy's greed causes endless trouble.

cartoons.title: Barbary Coast Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny discovers gold and visits Nasty Canasta's casino, outsmarting the cheat at his own games.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: After Bugs' giant gold nugget is stolen by Nasty Canasta, he tries to win it back at Canasta's San Francisco gambling hall.

cartoons.title: Bedevilled Rabbit
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny encounters the Tasmanian Devil on the island of Tasmania and must outwit the ravenous beast.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Hidden in a box of carrots, Bugs lands in Tasmania, where he matches wits with the Tasmanian Devil.

cartoons.title: Deduce, You Say!
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck plays detective Dorlock Holmes with Porky Pig as his sidekick Watkins in a London mystery.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck is a detective who is hunting for the Shropshire Slasher.

cartoons.title: Go Fly a Kit
cartoons.desc: A mother cat teaches her kittens life skills, but one kitten befriends a bird instead of catching it.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The story of a cat, raised by an eagle, who learns to fly and uses his ability to save his future girlfriend from a vicious bulldog.

cartoons.title: Half-Fare Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny boards a train and outwits two con-artist kids trying to collect his fare.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny boards the Chattanooga Choo Choo and finds Ralph Kramden and Ed Norton, from "The Honeymooners" TV show. Ralph and Ed are starving, and when they set eyes on Bugs, they yell, "It's foooooood!"

cartoons.title: Napoleon Bunny-Part
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny tunnels into Napoleon's headquarters and drives the French emperor crazy.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs takes a wrong turn off the Hollywood freeway and tunnels into the headquarters of Napoleon Bonaparte.

cartoons.title: Raw! Raw! Rooster
cartoons.desc: A small fighting rooster challenges Foghorn Leghorn's dominance of the barnyard.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foghorn's annoying college buddy, Rhode Island Red, comes for a visit and then won't leave.

cartoons.title: Rocket bye Baby
cartoons.desc: A Martian baby is accidentally delivered to an Earth family by a confused stork, causing alien chaos.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A cosmic mix-up results in a Martian baby being delivered to Earth, while an Earth baby is sent to Mars. Joseph Wilbur and his wife try to raise the green-skinned, ingenious Martian tyke as if he were an Earthling. But the kid builds his own spaceship and flies away, and Wilbur must find him and bring him back, or he'll never be able to make an exchange with the Martian parents for his own boy.

cartoons.title: Scrambled Aches
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote's Acme contraptions backfire in increasingly spectacular ways during another Road Runner chase.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote uses, among other things, a dehydrated boulder to try to catch the Road Runner.

cartoons.title: The Honey-Mousers
cartoons.desc: A Honeymooners parody featuring Ralph and Alice as mice in their cramped apartment.

cartoons.title: The Slap-Hoppy Mouse
cartoons.desc: A tiny mouse repeatedly outsmarts a much larger cat with slaps and clever tricks.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A tiny mouse repeatedly outsmarts a much larger cat with slaps and clever tricks.

cartoons.title: There They Go-Go-Go!
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote deploys another series of elaborate Acme contraptions in his endless pursuit of the Road Runner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote is hungry and schemes to catch the Road Runner.

cartoons.title: Three Little Bops
cartoons.desc: Three hip little pigs form a jazz combo, and the Big Bad Wolf tries to join in — told in beatnik rhyme.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Three hip, Little Pigs are travelling entertainers, moving from straw to wood, to brick nightclubs, playing swinging tunes for high-class, "with it" crowds, but an uncool Big Bad Wolf keeps intruding on their act with with his "corny horn" and uses it to blow their nightclubs down when they throw him out- until they are playing in their brick club and the Wolf tries a more drastic, explosive method for destroying the "House of Bricks".

cartoons.title: To Hare is Human
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote uses a computer brain to devise Bugs Bunny traps, but even technology can't outsmart the rabbit.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote, genius, tries to catch Bugs Bunny with the help of a Univac Electronic Brain.

cartoons.title: Tugboat Granny
cartoons.desc: Granny and Tweety take a tugboat ride while Sylvester swims after them through the harbor.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Tweety Bird and Granny are at the controls of a tugboat that Sylvester tries unsuccessfully to board.

cartoons.title: Tweet and Sour
cartoons.desc: A menacing bulldog warns Sylvester that if anything happens to Tweety, he will answer for it personally — turning Sylvester's usual pursuit of the canary into a no-win predicament.

cartoons.title: Tweet Zoo
cartoons.desc: Sylvester chases Tweety through a zoo, encountering dangerous animals at every exhibit.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat joins a tour group through the City Zoo and finds Tweety Bird among the exhibits. Sylvester chases Tweety and ends up in a bear's den.

cartoons.title: Tweety and the Beanstalk
cartoons.desc: Tweety grows a beanstalk and Sylvester climbs it, finding a giant version of Tweety's world at the top.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Jack's mother throws Jack's magic beans outside under Sylvester Cat's sleeping box, and the cat is whisked to the world above, where he finds a huge Tweety Bird in the castle of the legendary Giant.

cartoons.title: Two Crows From Tacos
cartoons.desc: Two lazy Mexican crows try every shortcut to find food without doing any actual work.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two none-too-bright Mexicali crows chase a grasshopper who outwits them at every turn.

cartoons.title: Video Wabbit
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd uses television to try to trap Bugs Bunny.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny is chased by Elmer Fudd throughout a TV studio and its various productions.

cartoons.title: Weasel Stop
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn protects a baby chick from a sneaky weasel.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A shaggy dog is the guard at a farm's chicken coop when a lip-smacking weasel comes along, intending to gain access to the chickens.

cartoons.title: Yankee Dood It
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd as a king of the elves helps a lazy shoemaker by having elves make his shoes at night.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd is the progressive King of industrial Elves. He visits an outmoded shoemaker's shop to extol the virtues of mass production capitalism to the shoemaker, whose pet cat, Sylvester, uses the magic word, "Jehosophat" to turn Fudd's elf helper into a mouse and chases him around the shoemaker's shop.

cartoons.title: A Waggily Tale
cartoons.desc: A father tells his son a bedtime story about a brave dog to convince him they should keep their new puppy.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A boy named Junior, who treats his dog, Elvis, cruelly, is scolded by his mother and sent to his room to have a nap.

cartoons.title: Banty Raids
cartoons.desc: A beatnik rooster crashes Foghorn Leghorn's barnyard and shakes up the status quo.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A horny hipster rooster, attracted to the hens in Foghorn Leghorn's barnyard, disguises himself as a baby foundling on Foghorn's doorstep. Foghorn adopts the girl-crazy rooster as his son, giving him access to all the chickens on the farm!

cartoons.title: Birds Anonymous
cartoons.desc: Sylvester joins a support group for cats trying to quit eating birds, but temptation keeps calling — an Oscar winner.

cartoons.title: Boston Quackie
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck plays a bumbling secret agent in a spy parody.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Boston Quackie (Daffy Duck) is an American agent in Paris assigned to guard the valuable secret contents of a briefcase. A man in a green hat steals the briefcase and leads Quackie on a chase aboard the Cloak and Dagger Express.

cartoons.title: Boyhood Daze
cartoons.desc: A young boy named Ralph daydreams wild adventures while stuck in his room.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Ralph gets sent to his room for breaking a window. There, he passes the time in Walter Mitty-type fashion, daydreaming that he's a parent-saving jungle explorer, an alien-fighting jet ace and a convict.

cartoons.title: Bugsy and Mugsy
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny hides in a gangsters' hideout and turns Rocky and Mugsy against each other.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny finds that gangsters Rocky and Mugsy have chosen his new abode, a condemned building, as their hideout. Bugs manipulates them into attacking each other to prove that crime doesn't pay.

cartoons.title: Cheese It, the Cat!
cartoons.desc: Two mice sneak into a house for cheese but must outsmart the resident cat.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A working-class mouse, who wants to steal a birthday cupcake for his wife, enlists his friend in an effort to get rid of the cat who has suddenly appeared in the kitchen.

cartoons.title: Drafty, Isn't It?
cartoons.desc: A wartime-themed cartoon short featuring patriotic humor and topical gags.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Ralph Phillips dreams about his future, only to have his dreams interrupted by Willie N. List, using an ACME Anti-Nightmare Machine, to compare military and civilian life.

cartoons.title: Ducking the Devil
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck must guard the escaped Tasmanian Devil and collect the reward money.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy tries to snare the escaped Tasmanian Devil for the $5000 reward offered by the city zoo.

cartoons.title: Fox Terror
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn deals with a fox raiding the henhouse.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foghorn's going fishing, but a fox has other plans for him. Posing as a racetrack tout, he suggests Foggy get a hunting dog and go hunting. Once the dog is gone, the fox comes after the chickens. One of them pulls the fox alarm, and the dog comes running back (too late). The fox next poses as a quiz show host, tricking Foghorn and the dog into blowing each other up. They go through another cycle or two of abuse before identifying their common enemy. They team up and go after him.

cartoons.title: Greedy for Tweety
cartoons.desc: Sylvester, a bulldog, and Granny all end up in the hospital, where Sylvester still tries to catch Tweety.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat chases Tweety Bird into busy city streets as he himself is being chased by a bulldog. All three are in an accident and taken to an animal hospital, each with a broken leg.

cartoons.title: Hardevil Hare
