cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is launched to the moon and encounters Marvin the Martian planning to blow up Earth.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny is launched to the moon and encounters Marvin the Martian planning to blow up Earth.

cartoons.title: Hare-Less Wolf
cartoons.desc: A big bad wolf tries to catch Bugs Bunny but the rabbit turns every trap back on him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: An absent-minded wolf sets out to catch Bugs for dinner but keeps forgetting what he was heading out to shoot in the first place.

cartoons.title: Hare-Way to the Stars
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny accidentally rockets to outer space and encounters Marvin the Martian on his space platform.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny groggily climbs out of bed and his hole and, unknowingly, into a rocket ship that's parked directly above. It transports him into outer space, where he is chased by martians.

cartoons.title: Knighty Knight Bugs
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is sent to retrieve the Singing Sword from Black Knight Yosemite Sam — winner of the Academy Award.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny is sent to retrieve the Singing Sword from Black Knight Yosemite Sam — winner of the Academy Award.

cartoons.title: Mouse-Taken Identity
cartoons.desc: Sylvester Jr. mistakes a baby kangaroo for a giant mouse, embarrassing his father.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat checks in to work at a museum with his son, Junior. He is bragging about his mouse-catching prowess when the baby kangaroo, Hippety Hopper, having escaped from the zoo, turns up in the museum. Sylvester and Junior, as usual, mistake Hippety for a giant mouse and chase him around the exhibits.

cartoons.title: Now, Hare This
cartoons.desc: Grandpa Bugs Bunny reads his nephew a bedtime story — his own twisted version of Little Red Riding Hood.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Big Bad Wolf and his little nephew try to trap Bugs Bunny by making like fairy tale characters.

cartoons.title: Piker's Peak
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny and Yosemite Sam race to climb a towering mountain peak.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In the Alps Bugs and Yosemite Sam vie for 50,000 Cronkites, the prize for the who "climbs the Schmatterhorn."

cartoons.title: Rabbit Romeo
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is gifted to a lovelorn female Slobovian beast named Millicent as a companion.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Elmer Fudd's Uncle Judd sends him an ugly, temperamental Slobovian rabbit named Millicent to babysit until he arrives. Elmer happens upon Bugs Bunny and thinks he'll be the perfect match for Millicent.  But as soon as Bugs gets a look at her, he tries to get away!

cartoons.title: Robin Hood Daffy
cartoons.desc: Daffy insists he's Robin Hood but can't seem to prove it to skeptical Friar Tuck (Porky Pig).
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy attempts to convince Porky, as Friar Tuck, that he really is Robin Hood.

cartoons.title: show Biz Bugs
cartoons.desc: Daffy grows jealous of Bugs Bunny's popularity and tries to upstage him on stage, with explosive consequences.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs and Daffy are vaudevillians competing for praise from the audience. They love Bugs no matter what; just the opposite for Daffy.

cartoons.title: Steal Wool
cartoons.desc: Ralph Wolf and Sam Sheepdog square off in another day of sheep-guarding duty.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Ralph Wolf tries to forcibly remove Sam Sheepdog in order to gain access to a flock of sheep. Without success, he uses a lasso, cannon, a string of firecrackers, and a giant rubber band.

cartoons.title: Tabasco Road
cartoons.desc: Speedy Gonzales and his friend race through town while two hungry cats try to catch them.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Speedy Gonzales, the fastest mouse in all Mexico, runs to the rescue of his two drunken rodent friends, Pablo and Fernando, who keep wandering into the hungry clutches of an alley cat.

cartoons.title: To Itch His Own
cartoons.desc: A flea searches for a new dog home after being evicted, but every option has drawbacks.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Mighty Angelo the Flea takes a vacation on a dog in the country. The muscular insect becomes the little mutt's protector when he is bullied by Butcher the bulldog.

cartoons.title: Touche and Go
cartoons.desc: Pepé Le Pew chases a reluctant cat through the streets of Paris in another amorous pursuit.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The amorous skunk, Pepé le Pew, chases a female cat by the seaside, under the sea and finally on a desert island.

cartoons.title: What's Opera, Doc?
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny and Elmer Fudd act out a Wagnerian opera — widely considered the greatest cartoon ever made.

cartoons.title: Zoom and Bored
cartoons.desc: Another round of Wile E. Coyote's elaborate desert traps backfiring in his pursuit of the Road Runner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote uses a bottle full of bees, a brick wall, a boulder in a catapult, and a harpoon gun in his attempts to catch the Road Runner.

cartoons.title: Baton Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny conducts an orchestra but is driven mad by a persistent bee buzzing around his podium.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs conducts the Warner Brothers Symphony in Franz von Suppé's "Morning, Noon, and Night in Vienna" while reacting to a bothersome fly.

cartoons.title: Feather Bluster
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn faces a challenge from a young rooster trying to take over the barnyard.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Geriatrics Foghorn Leghorn and the barnyard dog recount their years of violent, mutual heckling, unaware that outside the window of their house their grandsons are behaving the same. The short is essentially a clip show, in that the majority of the footage is reused from earlier cartoons.

cartoons.title: Gopher Broke
cartoons.desc: The Goofy Gophers face off against a determined farmer trying to rid his garden of them.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Goofy Gophers are about to harvest the vegetables on the farm when the farmhands beat them to the punch. Worried that their food source is being "vandalized," they follow the truck to the barn so they can recover what they consider to be their food.

cartoons.title: Hook, Line and Stinker
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote tries to catch the Road Runner near a bridge with fishing gear and Acme products.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote hopes to catch the Road Runner using a mallet, a cooking pan, a TNT stick, a balloon, and a piano dropped from a precipice. The last of these results in Wile E. falling to the road below along with the piano and ending up with 88 teeth.

cartoons.title: Mouse-Placed Kitten
cartoons.desc: A mother cat adopts a mouse along with her kittens, confusing the household.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A kitten is dropped in a sack out of a car and rolls down a hill, to arrive at the door of Clyde and Matilda Mouse...

cartoons.title: Pre-Hysterical Hare
cartoons.desc: Elmer Fudd discovers cave paintings showing his ancestor trying to hunt Bugs Bunny's ancestor.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs discovers a Micronesian Film Documentary in "Cromagnonscope" showing Elmer Fuddstone and a sabertooth bunny in 10,000 BC.

cartoons.title: Weasel While You Work
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn protects the henhouse from a crafty weasel.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Amid a snowy barnyard, Foghorn Leghorn deflects the carniverous attentions of a lip-smacking weasel.

cartoons.title: A Broken Leghorn
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn feigns a broken leg to avoid work, but a little chick sees through his scheme.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: On Old MacDonald's farm, an egg hatches in slow-witted hen Miss Prissy's nest, and out of the shell comes a baby rooster. Fearing he will be replaced by the new arrival and sent to be slaughtered, Foghorn Leghorn plots to do away with the tyke.

cartoons.title: A Witch's Tangled Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny encounters a witch and uses his wits to escape her clutches.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Rabbit - in this case Bugs - is an important needed ingredient in Witch Hazel's brew.

cartoons.title: Backwoods Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny outwits two hillbilly buzzards in the backwoods who want to eat him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When Bugs vacations in the Ozarks he is pursued by hungry buzzards.

cartoons.title: Bonanza Bunny
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny strikes it rich during the Klondike Gold Rush and must protect his claim from Blacque Jacque Shellacque.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs fights with Blacque Jacque Shellacque over Klondike gold.

cartoons.title: Crockett-Doodle-Do
cartoons.desc: Foghorn Leghorn tells a young chick the exaggerated tale of his ancestor, a Davy Crockett-like hero.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Foghorn Leghorn decides to take an egghead genius chick out to the woods to distract him from his long-haired atomic science books and teach him about such practical things as scouting and woodcraft, but finds that the kid is more knowledgeable than he in these matters.

cartoons.title: High Note
cartoons.desc: Musical notes come alive on sheet music and have adventures on the page.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The sheet music for Johann Strauss' The Blue Danube is constructed by moving musical symbols. A baton-toting conductor note tries to direct his fellow notes in performing this musical piece, but finds that one of the notes has become drunk.

cartoons.title: Mexicali Shmoes
cartoons.desc: Two Mexican cats, Jose and Manuel, try to catch Speedy Gonzales with increasingly futile schemes.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A pair of not-too-bright Mexican cats, one shorter-tempered than the other, decide to chase Speedy Gonzales, the fastest mouse in all Mexico.

cartoons.title: Mice Follies
cartoons.desc: Two mice flood a kitchen and freeze it into an ice skating rink to escape Sylvester.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In yet another cartoon spoof of TV's "The Honeymooners", rodents Ralph Crumden and Ned Morton have stayed out too late and return home fearing their wives' wrath...

cartoons.title: Mouse and Garden
cartoons.desc: Two cats agree to share a mouse, but both secretly try to catch it for themselves.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat and his orange feline friend, Sam, are rummaging through trash cans for food in the evening on a waterfront when they spot a mouse. They agree to share the little rodent for breakfast the next morning, while during the night each tries to snag the mouse for himself.

cartoons.title: Mutt in a Rut
cartoons.desc: A dog with multiple personalities switches between timid and aggressive, confusing everyone around him.

cartoons.title: People Are Bunny
cartoons.desc: Daffy tricks Bugs into appearing on a TV game show to win the prize money for himself.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy Duck needs to get Bugs Bunny into QTTV's studio ASAP in order to win the thousand dollar prize.

cartoons.title: The Mouse That Jack Built
cartoons.desc: Jack Benny and his radio cast appear as mice in this parody of the famous comedian's show.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In this spoof of "The Jack Benny Program", a mouse with Jack Benny's personality and poor violin playing ability lives, along with a mouse version of Benny's valet, Eddie 'Rochester' Anderson, in a hole in a wall of Jack Benny's own home. Jack the rodent takes a mouse version of 'Mary Livingstone (I)' out to dinner, and the two unwittingly walk right into the disguised mouth of an orange cat!

cartoons.title: The Pied Piper of Guadalupe
cartoons.desc: Sylvester poses as the Pied Piper to lure Mexican mice, but Speedy Gonzales foils his plan.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester the cat imitates the Pied Piper of Hamelin to lure a group of mice into a jug that he seals with a cork. But Speedy Gonzales won't be hypnotized by Sylvester's flute and gradually rescues his friends from Sylvester's clutches.

cartoons.title: Wild and Woolly Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny faces off against Yosemite Sam in a Wild West showdown.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny and Yosemite Sam duel with trains in an Old West shootout.

cartoons.title: Wild, Wild World
cartoons.desc: A spot-gag travelogue featuring comic observations about exotic locations around the world.

context.title: The 1960s — The Final Chapter
context.summary: The theatrical short was on its last legs by the 1960s. Television had changed audience expectations and studio economics. Warner Bros. briefly shut down the animation department in 1963, laying off nearly everyone including Chuck Jones, who moved on to MGM and independent work. The studio briefly outsourced production before re-opening with a skeleton crew. Despite the upheaval, some genuine classics emerged — including Jones's final Warner shorts before his departure. The last original theatrical Looney Tune, Injun Trouble, was released in 1969, ending a 39-year run that produced over 1,000 cartoons and defined American animation. The characters themselves never went away — they moved to television and have never really left the cultural conversation since.

cartoons.title: Dog Gone People
cartoons.desc: A sophisticated dog tries to maintain his dignity while his owners engage in embarrassing behavior.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Hoping for a promotion, Elmer Fudd agrees to take care of his boss' dog and finds he must treat the pooch as a human.

cartoons.title: From Hare to Heir
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny faces off against Yosemite Sam in another battle of wits and bluster.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs Bunny faces off against Yosemite Sam in another battle of wits and bluster.

cartoons.title: Goldimouse and the Three Cats
cartoons.desc: Sylvester Jr. retells the story of Goldilocks and the Three Bears with himself cast as the little girl who wanders into a house occupied by three dangerous cats.

cartoons.title: Person to Bunny
cartoons.desc: A TV reporter interviews Bugs Bunny at home while Daffy Duck tries to steal the spotlight.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In his Hollywood home Bugs is being interviewed by the Edward R. Murrow TV show "People to People" when Daffy and Elmer show up.

cartoons.title: Rabbit's Feat
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote uses his self-proclaimed genius to try to catch Bugs Bunny instead of the Road Runner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote decides to cook and eat Bugs, but Bugs is on to his plan and tries to escape by acting looney.

cartoons.title: Beep Prepared
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote sets up more elaborate Road Runner traps across the desert — all of which fail spectacularly.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote tries yet again to catch the Road Runner.

cartoons.title: Compressed Hare
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote orders elaborate Acme gadgets to catch Bugs Bunny, with predictably disastrous results.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs battles Wile E. Coyote. A ten trillion volt electric magnet draws everything imaginable.

cartoons.title: Crows Feat
cartoons.desc: Two clever crows outsmart a farmer's scarecrow and dog to steal corn from his field.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Two Mexican crows, flying to Guadalajara on the wings of an airplane, spot a corn field on the ground below and dive into it...

cartoons.title: D' Fightin' Ones
cartoons.desc: A junkyard dog and a scrappy cat are accidentally chained together and must reluctantly cooperate just to survive while each desperately tries to escape the other.

cartoons.title: Lickety-Splat
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote's latest batch of Road Runner traps go wrong in increasingly spectacular ways.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Wile E. Coyote chases the Road Runner with roller skis, a bow, a rifle, a boomerang, an anvil, and several exploding darts let loose from a balloon.

cartoons.title: Nelly's Folly
cartoons.desc: A singing giraffe named Nelly becomes a celebrity but loses everything when scandal strikes.

cartoons.title: Prince Violent
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny faces off against Yosemite Sam as a Viking invader.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Viking Yosemite Sam arrives to storm the castle. But Bugs takes charge of the defenses, and between Bugs' cleverness and Sam's stupidity, the castle is never seriously threatened, even when Sam enlists the help of an elephant.

cartoons.title: Rebel Without Claws
cartoons.desc: Tweety and Sylvester are on opposite sides of the Civil War.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Confederate Army wants to get an important message through to General Lee, but all the carrier pigeons have been shot down. Tweety steps in.

cartoons.title: The Last Hungry Cat
cartoons.desc: Sylvester worries he may have eaten Tweety after a blackout, tormented by a Hitchcock-style narrator.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Sylvester Cat tumbles and falls dazed to the floor when making a grab for Tweety Bird. He comes to and thinks he has killed and swallowed the little canary and that he's wanted for murder.

cartoons.title: Bill of Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny outsmarts the Tasmanian Devil who's looking for a rabbit dinner.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The Tasmanian Devil finds Bugs cooking dinner underneath a beach boardwalk.

cartoons.title: Good Noose
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck is marooned on a deserted island and devises an increasingly elaborate scheme involving a noose to signal a passing ship — a cartoon rarely shown on TV due to its gallows humor.

cartoons.title: Honey's Money
cartoons.desc: Yosemite Sam marries a rich widow for her money but must deal with her enormous, dimwitted adult son.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Yosemite Sam marries a wealthy widow for her money.

cartoons.title: Martian Through Georgia
cartoons.desc: A lonely Martian visits Earth hoping to make friends but is feared and chased by everyone.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Way out in space, on another world whose population is contented, one of its people decides that travel broadens the mind and relieves boredom. So, he flies to Earth in hope of helping the alien Earthlings improve their lot, only to cause panic and be declared a monster just because he looks different. So, he decides to return home, where, at least, he can find love.

cartoons.title: Now Hear This
cartoons.desc: A man finds a devil's horn that distorts all sound around him in this experimental, avant-garde cartoon.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In this surreal cartoon that plays with the idea of sound effects, a near-deaf old man finds one of the devil's lost horns and tries to use it as an ear trumpet.

cartoons.title: Shishkabugs
cartoons.desc: Yosemite Sam serves as the king's cook and must prepare a rabbit — leading him to Bugs Bunny.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: A spoiled-rotten monarch orders royal chef Yosemite Sam to make "Hasenpfeffer", the basic ingredient of which is rabbit. When Bugs comes to the door asking to borrow some carrots, Sam decides to cook him!

cartoons.title: The Million Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs and Daffy race to a TV studio to claim a million-dollar prize, sabotaging each other along the way.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs races Daffy to get to the TV station first and win the prize on the "Beat Your Buddy" show.

cartoons.title: Bartholomew versus the Wheel
cartoons.desc: A caveman inventor struggles with his new creation — the wheel — in this comparison of ancient and modern frustrations.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: The story of Bartholomew, a dog who hates wheels, as he grows from a puppy to a very large dog.

cartoons.title: Dr. Devil and Mr. Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny disguises himself as a doctor to escape the Tasmanian Devil.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs and the Tasmanian Devil battle it out in a jungle hospital, with Bugs convincing Taz that he's sicker than he thinks.

cartoons.title: Hare-Breadth Hurry
cartoons.desc: Wile E. Coyote goes after Bugs Bunny when the Road Runner calls in sick.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When Bugs takes Wile E. Coyote's place in a cartoon, the Bugs/Coyote roles and rules become confused.

cartoons.title: Mad as a Mars Hare
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny is sent to Mars where Marvin the Martian uses a time machine on him.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Marvin the Martian is monitoring through his telescope a rocket launch on Earth. The rocket heads straight for him and lands on Mars. The only occupant is Bugs Bunny, lured into Cape Canaveral by a carrot and sent to Mars as an expendable "astro-rabbit". Bugs is to claim Mars in the name of the Earth, but Marvin won't allow an Earth creature to contaminate his atmosphere. He trains a time-projector gun on Bugs and reverts the bunny to a Neanderthal Rabbit, who crushes Marvin with one hand.

cartoons.title: The Unmentionables
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny as an Untouchables-style agent takes on gangsters Rocky and Mugsy in a Prohibition-era parody.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In a spoof of TV's "Untouchables" Rocky and Mugsy chase "Elegant Ness" (Bugs) through the ACME cereal company.

cartoons.title: Transylvania 6-5000
cartoons.desc: Bugs Bunny visits a spooky Transylvanian castle and encounters a vampire.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bugs is given a room for the night at the castle of Count Bloodcount in Transylvania.

cartoons.title: False Hare
cartoons.desc: Big Bad Wolf and his nephew try to lure Bugs Bunny out of his hole — the last original theatrical Bugs Bunny short.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Big Bad Wolf and his nephew create a club for rabbits, Club del Conejo, to try to catch Bugs Bunny.

cartoons.title: Freudy Cat
cartoons.desc: A traumatized Sylvester is taken to psychiatrist Dr. Freud E. Katt by Sylvester Jr. to address his terror of what he believes is a giant mouse — in reality, Hippety Hopper the kangaroo.

cartoons.title: It's Nice to Have a Mouse Around the House
cartoons.desc: A cat is hired to catch a mouse in a household but finds the job harder than expected.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: When Speedy Gonzales invades the home of Granny and rapidly drives her cat, Sylvester, to a nervous breakdown, Granny calls on Daffy Duck of the Jet Age Pest Control company to do the job of removing Gonzales from her home.

cartoons.title: Señorella and the Glass Huarache
cartoons.desc: A Mexican Cinderella story with Speedy Gonzales-style characters.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: In a Mexican restaurant, a man named Jose tells to his friend, Manuel, the story of Senorella, a Mexican version of Cinderella. Senorella's dream of liberation from her slavish existence under the yoke of her wicked "Strap-mother" and "Strap-seesters", comes true after her fairy godmother grants her a night as a ravishing beauty at the fiesta at a bullfighter's father's estate.

cartoons.title: Corn on the Cop
cartoons.desc: A pair of crows steal corn while outwitting a scarecrow and a dog.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Corn on the Cop is a 1965 Merrie Melodies short directed by Irv Spector and starring Daffy Duck and Porky Pig. On Halloween night, a robber disguises himself as a little old lady and goes on a crime spree. The police, including Daffy Duck and Porky Pig, search for the robber but end up tangling with the real Granny instead.

cartoons.title: A-Haunting We Will Go
cartoons.desc: Daffy Duck and Speedy Gonzales encounter a spooky witch named Witch Hazel on Halloween.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Daffy convinces his son that old Witch Hazel isn't what he thinks she is.

cartoons.title: Merlin the Magic Mouse
cartoons.desc: Merlin the Magic Mouse debuts, using his magic act to outsmart a cat.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Merlin, a W.C. Fields-like mouse, puts on a magic show in front of an audience of one cat. Merlin does some bad magic tricks for the feline before he realizes he's a mouse.

cartoons.title: Norman Normal
cartoons.desc: An average office worker navigates the absurdities of modern life in this unusual cartoon.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Norman Normal is pressured to act in ways he finds uncomfortable by his boss, his father, and the people around him.

cartoons.title: Bunny and Claude
cartoons.desc: Bonnie and Clyde-style rabbit outlaws rob carrot stands and evade the bumbling sheriff.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Outlaws Bunny and Claude are chased by the Sheriff. The Sheriff even attempts to disquise himself as a giant carrot to catch the duo.

cartoons.title: The Great Carrot Train Robbery
cartoons.desc: Bunny and Claude return to rob a train full of carrots in the Old West.
cartoons.tmdb_overview: Bunny and Claude are still at their carrot caper. This time, they rob a train as the Sheriff is once again called out to stop them..
